The earthly text is a manifestation of a heavenly original — the ẓāhir/bāṭin of scripture itself. Verse 32 ("We raise some of them above others in d...

357 Verse 32 ("We raise some of them above others in degrees, that some of them may take others in service") describes social hierarchy as a functional design, not a moral ranking — connecting to 6:165's degrees of testing. Verse 63 (Jesus: "I have come unto you with the Wisdom") uses the root -- (wi...

358 Verse 35: "and ornaments (). And all that is but the enjoyment of the life of this world." means to adorn, to embellish, to gild. The surah strips ornament of ultimate value — silver roofs, golden doors, and luxury couches are offered as what God could give to those who cover over, precisely beca...

359 Verse 61: "verily, he is a knowledge () of the Hour." "he" (referring to Jesus) is called a knowledge of the Hour — not merely a sign but an embodied knowing. connects Jesus to eschatology: his appearance signals the approaching end. This verse frames Jesus as a living proof of resurrection.

360 Verse 63 (Jesus: "I have come unto you with the Wisdom") uses the root -- (wisdom/judgement), framing Jesus's mission as clarification of disputes — the same root as the Qur'an's self-description as "judge" () in 13:37.

Surah 44: Ad-Dukhan — The Smoke

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

حم1Ha, Mim.
وَالكِتَٰبِ المُبِينِ2By the clear Book!
إِنَّا أَنزَلنَٰهُ فِي لَيلَة مُّبَٰرَكَةٍ إِنَّا كُنَّا مُنذِرِينَ3Verily, We sent it down on a blessed night; verily, We have ever been warning.361
فِيهَا يُفرَقُ كُلُّ أَمرٍ حَكِيمٍ4Therein every wise command is made distinct,
أَمرا مِّن عِندِنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا مُرسِلِينَ5a command from Us; verily, We have ever been sending,
رَحمَة مِّن رَّبِّكَ إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ العَلِيمُ6a mercy from thy Lord; verily, He is the Hearing, the Knowing,
رَبِّ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَمَا بَينَهُمَا إِن كُنتُم مُّوقِنِينَ7Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, if ye would be certain.
لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحيِ وَيُمِيتُ رَبُّكُم وَرَبُّ ءَابَائِكُمُ الأَوَّلِينَ8There is no god but He; He giveth life and causeth death — your Lord and the Lord of your fathers of old.
بَل هُم فِي شَكّ يَلعَبُونَ9Nay, but they are in doubt, playing.
فَارتَقِب يَومَ تَأتِي السَّمَاءُ بِدُخَان مُّبِين10So watch thou for the Day when the heaven shall bring a manifest smoke,362
يَغشَى النَّاسَ هَٰذَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيم11covering the people: "This is a painful chastisement.
رَّبَّنَا اكشِف عَنَّا العَذَابَ إِنَّا مُؤمِنُونَ12Our Lord! Remove from us the chastisement; verily, we are believers."
أَنَّىٰ لَهُمُ الذِّكرَىٰ وَقَد جَاءَهُم رَسُول مُّبِين13How shall the Reminder avail them, when a clear Messenger had already come unto them,
ثُمَّ تَوَلَّواْ عَنهُ وَقَالُواْ مُعَلَّم مَّجنُونٌ14then they turned away from him and said: "One taught, one possessed!"
إِنَّا كَاشِفُواْ العَذَابِ قَلِيلًا إِنَّكُم عَائِدُونَ15Verily, We shall remove the chastisement a little; verily, ye shall return.
يَومَ نَبطِشُ البَطشَةَ الكُبرَىٰ إِنَّا مُنتَقِمُونَ16On the Day when We shall seize with the mightiest seizure, verily, We shall exact retribution.
وَلَقَد فَتَنَّا قَبلَهُم قَومَ فِرعَونَ وَجَاءَهُم رَسُول كَرِيمٌ17And verily, We tried before them the people of Pharaoh, and there came unto them a noble messenger:
أَن أَدُّواْ إِلَيَّ عِبَادَ اللَّهِ إِنِّي لَكُم رَسُولٌ أَمِين18"Deliver unto me the servants of God; verily, I am unto you a faithful messenger.
وَأَن لَّا تَعلُواْ عَلَى اللَّهِ إِنِّي ءَاتِيكُم بِسُلطَٰن مُّبِين19And exalt not yourselves against God; verily, I come unto you with a clear authority.
وَإِنِّي عُذتُ بِرَبِّي وَرَبِّكُم أَن تَرجُمُونِ20And verily, I seek refuge with my Lord and your Lord, lest ye stone me.
وَإِن لَّم تُؤمِنُواْ لِي فَاعتَزِلُونِ21And if ye believe me not, then withdraw from me."
فَدَعَا رَبَّهُ أَنَّ هَـٰؤُلَاءِ قَوم مُّجرِمُونَ22Then he called upon his Lord: "These are a guilty people."
فَأَسرِ بِعِبَادِي لَيلًا إِنَّكُم مُّتَّبَعُونَ23"So set forth with My servants by night; verily, ye will be pursued.
وَاترُكِ البَحرَ رَهوًا إِنَّهُم جُند مُّغرَقُونَ24And leave the sea parted; verily, they are a host to be drowned."
كَم تَرَكُواْ مِن جَنَّـٰت وَعُيُون25How many gardens and springs they left behind,
وَزُرُوع وَمَقَام كَرِيم26and sown fields and noble stations,
وَنَعمَة كَانُواْ فِيهَا فَٰكِهِينَ27and comfort wherein they rejoiced!
كَذَٰلِكَ وَأَورَثنَٰهَا قَومًا ءَاخَرِينَ28Thus it was; and We bequeathed them unto another people.
فَمَا بَكَت عَلَيهِمُ السَّمَاءُ وَالأَرضُ وَمَا كَانُواْ مُنظَرِينَ29And the heaven and the earth wept not for them, nor were they reprieved.
وَلَقَد نَجَّينَا بَنِي إِسرَـٰءِيلَ مِنَ العَذَابِ المُهِينِ30And verily, We delivered the Children of Israel from the humiliating chastisement,
مِن فِرعَونَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ عَالِيا مِّنَ المُسرِفِينَ31from Pharaoh; verily, he was haughty, of the prodigal.
وَلَقَدِ اختَرنَٰهُم عَلَىٰ عِلمٍ عَلَى العَٰلَمِينَ32And verily, We chose them knowingly above the worlds,
وَءَاتَينَٰهُم مِّنَ الأيَٰتِ مَا فِيهِ بَلَـٰؤاْ مُّبِينٌ33and We gave them of the signs that which was a clear trial.
إِنَّ هَـٰؤُلَاءِ لَيَقُولُونَ34Verily, these say:
إِن هِيَ إِلَّا مَوتَتُنَا الأُولَىٰ وَمَا نَحنُ بِمُنشَرِينَ35"There is naught but our first death, and we shall not be raised.
فَأتُواْ بِـَابَائِنَا إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ36Bring our fathers, if ye be truthful."
أَهُم خَيرٌ أَم قَومُ تُبَّع وَالَّذِينَ مِن قَبلِهِم أَهلَكنَٰهُم إِنَّهُم كَانُواْ مُجرِمِينَ37Are they better, or the people of Tubba and those before them? We destroyed them; verily, they were guilty.
وَمَا خَلَقنَا السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ وَمَا بَينَهُمَا لَٰعِبِينَ38And We created not the heavens and the earth and what is between them in play.363
مَا خَلَقنَٰهُمَا إِلَّا بِالحَقِّ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكثَرَهُم لَا يَعلَمُونَ39We created them not save in truth, but most of them know not.
إِنَّ يَومَ الفَصلِ مِيقَٰتُهُم أَجمَعِينَ40Verily, the Day of Decision is the appointed time of them all,364
يَومَ لَا يُغنِي مَولًى عَن مَّولى شَيـا وَلَا هُم يُنصَرُونَ41the Day when no friend shall avail a friend at all, nor shall they be succoured,
إِلَّا مَن رَّحِمَ اللَّهُ إِنَّهُ هُوَ العَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ42save him upon whom God hath mercy. Verily, He is the Mighty, the Merciful.
إِنَّ شَجَرَتَ الزَّقُّومِ43Verily, the tree of Zaqqum
طَعَامُ الأَثِيمِ44is the food of the sinner;
كَالمُهلِ يَغلِي فِي البُطُونِ45like molten brass, it boileth in the bellies,
كَغَليِ الحَمِيمِ46as the boiling of scalding water.
خُذُوهُ فَاعتِلُوهُ إِلَىٰ سَوَاءِ الجَحِيمِ47"Seize him and drag him into the midst of the Blaze!
ثُمَّ صُبُّواْ فَوقَ رَأسِهِ مِن عَذَابِ الحَمِيمِ48Then pour upon his head the chastisement of scalding water.
ذُق إِنَّكَ أَنتَ العَزِيزُ الكَرِيمُ49Taste! Verily, thou art the mighty, the noble!
إِنَّ هَٰذَا مَا كُنتُم بِهِ تَمتَرُونَ50Verily, this is that which ye used to doubt."
إِنَّ المُتَّقِينَ فِي مَقَامٍ أَمِين51Verily, the pious are in a secure station,
فِي جَنَّـٰت وَعُيُون52amid gardens and springs,
يَلبَسُونَ مِن سُندُس وَإِستَبرَق مُّتَقَٰبِلِينَ53wearing fine silk and brocade, facing one another.
كَذَٰلِكَ وَزَوَّجنَٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِين54Thus it is; and We have paired them with fair ones, wide of eye.365
يَدعُونَ فِيهَا بِكُلِّ فَٰكِهَةٍ ءَامِنِينَ55They call therein for every fruit, secure.
لَا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا المَوتَ إِلَّا المَوتَةَ الأُولَىٰ وَوَقَىٰهُم عَذَابَ الجَحِيمِ56They taste not therein death, save the first death; and He hath shielded them from the chastisement of the Blaze,
فَضلا مِّن رَّبِّكَ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الفَوزُ العَظِيمُ57a bounty from thy Lord. That is the supreme triumph.
فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرنَٰهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لَعَلَّهُم يَتَذَكَّرُونَ58And verily, We have made it easy in thy tongue, that haply they may remember.
فَارتَقِب إِنَّهُم مُّرتَقِبُونَ59So watch; verily, they are watching.

361 Verse 3 ("We sent it down on a blessed night") connects to 97:1 (Laylat al-Qadr) — the night of revelation as the moment when the bāṭin enters the ẓāhir. The Pharaoh narrative (vv.17–31) parallels 73:15–16 where Pharaoh also disobeys a messenger and is seized. The pattern of rejection-seizure is ...

362 Verse 10: "the Day when the heaven shall bring a manifest smoke ()." -- appears only here in the Qur'an. Smoke is what remains when fire has consumed its fuel — neither flame nor ash but the visible trace of destruction. The smoke "covers the people" ( ), using the same verb-pattern as covering o...

363 Verse 38 ("We created not the heavens and the earth and what is between them in play") echoes 21:16 and connects to 51:56 — creation has purpose, not entertainment.

364 Verse 40: "the Day of Decision () is the appointed time." -- means to separate, to decide, to distinguish. The Day of Decision is when all things are separated — truth from falsehood, the concealed from the manifest. This is the ultimate ẓāhir moment: nothing remains bāṭin.

365 Verse 54: "We have paired them with fair ones, wide of eye ( )." -- appears here in its conventional "houris" form. However, read through the root-recovery method of this project, these are the Devoted (ḥūr, from --, to return) — the returners paired with the righteous. The "pairing" () suggests ...

Surah 45: Al-Jathiyah — The Kneeling

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

حم1Ha, Mim.
تَنزِيلُ الكِتَٰبِ مِنَ اللَّهِ العَزِيزِ الحَكِيمِ2The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.
إِنَّ فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ لَأيَٰت لِّلمُؤمِنِينَ3Verily, in the heavens and the earth are signs for the believers.
وَفِي خَلقِكُم وَمَا يَبُثُّ مِن دَابَّةٍ ءَايَٰت لِّقَوم يُوقِنُونَ4And in your creation, and the creatures that He scattereth, are signs for a people who are certain.
وَاختِلَٰفِ الَّيلِ وَالنَّهَارِ وَمَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مِن رِّزق فَأَحيَا بِهِ الأَرضَ بَعدَ مَوتِهَا وَتَصرِيفِ الرِّيَٰحِ ءَايَٰت لِّقَوم يَعقِلُونَ5And the alternation of the night and the day, and the provision that God sendeth down from the heaven whereby He quickeneth the earth after its death, and the turning about of the winds — signs for a people who understand.
تِلكَ ءَايَٰتُ اللَّهِ نَتلُوهَا عَلَيكَ بِالحَقِّ فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثِ بَعدَ اللَّهِ وَءَايَٰتِهِ يُؤمِنُونَ6Those are the signs of God; We recite them unto thee in truth. Then in what discourse, after God and His signs, will they believe?
وَيل لِّكُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيم7Woe unto every sinful liar,
يَسمَعُ ءَايَٰتِ اللَّهِ تُتلَىٰ عَلَيهِ ثُمَّ يُصِرُّ مُستَكبِرا كَأَن لَّم يَسمَعهَا فَبَشِّرهُ بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيم8who heareth the signs of God recited unto him, then persisteth in pride as though he heard them not. So give him tidings of a painful chastisement.
وَإِذَا عَلِمَ مِن ءَايَٰتِنَا شَيـًا اتَّخَذَهَا هُزُوًا أُوْلَـٰئِكَ لَهُم عَذَاب مُّهِين9And when he knoweth aught of Our signs, he taketh them in mockery. Those, for them is a humiliating chastisement.
مِّن وَرَائِهِم جَهَنَّمُ وَلَا يُغنِي عَنهُم مَّا كَسَبُواْ شَيـا وَلَا مَا اتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَولِيَاءَ وَلَهُم عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ10Beyond them is Hell; and what they have earned shall avail them naught, nor those whom they have taken as protectors besides God; and for them is a tremendous chastisement.
هَٰذَا هُدى وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ بِـَايَٰتِ رَبِّهِم لَهُم عَذَاب مِّن رِّجزٍ أَلِيمٌ11This is guidance; and those who conceal the signs of their Lord, for them is a chastisement of painful wrath.
اللَّهُ الَّذِي سَخَّرَ لَكُمُ البَحرَ لِتَجرِيَ الفُلكُ فِيهِ بِأَمرِهِ وَلِتَبتَغُواْ مِن فَضلِهِ وَلَعَلَّكُم تَشكُرُونَ12God it is Who hath subjected unto you the sea, that the ships may run therein by His command, and that ye may seek of His bounty, and that haply ye may give thanks.
وَسَخَّرَ لَكُم مَّا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرضِ جَمِيعا مِّنهُ إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَأيَٰت لِّقَوم يَتَفَكَّرُونَ13And He hath subjected unto you whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, all from Him. Verily, in that are signs for a people who reflect.366
قُل لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ يَغفِرُواْ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يَرجُونَ أَيَّامَ اللَّهِ لِيَجزِيَ قَومَا بِمَا كَانُواْ يَكسِبُونَ14Say unto those who believe to forgive those who hope not for the Days of God, that He may recompense a people for what they used to earn.
مَن عَمِلَ صَٰلِحا فَلِنَفسِهِ وَمَن أَسَاءَ فَعَلَيهَا ثُمَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُم تُرجَعُونَ15Whoso worketh righteousness, it is for his own soul, and whoso doeth evil, it is against it; then unto your Lord ye shall be returned.
وَلَقَد ءَاتَينَا بَنِي إِسرَـٰءِيلَ الكِتَٰبَ وَالحُكمَ وَالنُّبُوَّةَ وَرَزَقنَٰهُم مِّنَ الطَّيِّبَٰتِ وَفَضَّلنَٰهُم عَلَى العَٰلَمِينَ16And verily, We gave the Children of Israel the Book and the judgement and the prophethood, and We provided them with good things and preferred them over the worlds,
وَءَاتَينَٰهُم بَيِّنَٰت مِّنَ الأَمرِ فَمَا اختَلَفُواْ إِلَّا مِن بَعدِ مَا جَاءَهُمُ العِلمُ بَغيَا بَينَهُم إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقضِي بَينَهُم يَومَ القِيَٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُواْ فِيهِ يَختَلِفُونَ17and We gave them clear proofs of the command. And they differed not save after the knowledge had come unto them, out of envy among themselves. Verily, thy Lord shall judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that wherein they used to differ.
ثُمَّ جَعَلنَٰكَ عَلَىٰ شَرِيعَة مِّنَ الأَمرِ فَاتَّبِعهَا وَلَا تَتَّبِع أَهوَاءَ الَّذِينَ لَا يَعلَمُونَ18Then We set thee upon a way of the command, so follow it, and follow not the desires of those who know not.
إِنَّهُم لَن يُغنُواْ عَنكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيـا وَإِنَّ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ بَعضُهُم أَولِيَاءُ بَعض وَاللَّهُ وَلِيُّ المُتَّقِينَ19Verily, they shall avail thee naught against God. And verily, the wrongdoers are friends of one another; and God is the Friend of the pious.
هَٰذَا بَصَـٰئِرُ لِلنَّاسِ وَهُدى وَرَحمَة لِّقَوم يُوقِنُونَ20This is clear insight for humankind, and a guidance and a mercy for a people who are certain.
أَم حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ اجتَرَحُواْ السَّيِّـَاتِ أَن نَّجعَلَهُم كَالَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ سَوَاء مَّحيَاهُم وَمَمَاتُهُم سَاءَ مَا يَحكُمُونَ21Or do those who have wrought evil deeds suppose that We shall make them as those who believe and work righteous deeds, equal in their life and their death? Evil is what they judge!
وَخَلَقَ اللَّهُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ بِالحَقِّ وَلِتُجزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفسِ بِمَا كَسَبَت وَهُم لَا يُظلَمُونَ22And God created the heavens and the earth in truth, and that every soul may be recompensed for what it hath earned, and they shall not be wronged.
أَفَرَءَيتَ مَنِ اتَّخَذَ إِلَٰهَهُ هَوَىٰهُ وَأَضَلَّهُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ عِلم وَخَتَمَ عَلَىٰ سَمعِهِ وَقَلبِهِ وَجَعَلَ عَلَىٰ بَصَرِهِ غِشَٰوَة فَمَن يَهدِيهِ مِن بَعدِ اللَّهِ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ23Hast thou then seen him who taketh as his god his own desire, and God sendeth him astray knowingly, and sealeth up his hearing and his heart, and setteth on his sight a covering? Then who shall guide him after God? Will ye not then remember?367
وَقَالُواْ مَا هِيَ إِلَّا حَيَاتُنَا الدُّنيَا نَمُوتُ وَنَحيَا وَمَا يُهلِكُنَا إِلَّا الدَّهرُ وَمَا لَهُم بِذَٰلِكَ مِن عِلمٍ إِن هُم إِلَّا يَظُنُّونَ24And they say: "There is naught but our life of this world; we die and we live, and naught destroyeth us save Time." And they have no knowledge thereof; they do but conjecture.368
وَإِذَا تُتلَىٰ عَلَيهِم ءَايَٰتُنَا بَيِّنَٰت مَّا كَانَ حُجَّتَهُم إِلَّا أَن قَالُواْ ائتُواْ بِـَابَائِنَا إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ25And when Our signs are recited unto them as clear proofs, their argument is naught but that they say: "Bring our fathers, if ye be truthful."
قُلِ اللَّهُ يُحيِيكُم ثُمَّ يُمِيتُكُم ثُمَّ يَجمَعُكُم إِلَىٰ يَومِ القِيَٰمَةِ لَا رَيبَ فِيهِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعلَمُونَ26Say: "God giveth you life, then causeth you to die, then shall He gather you unto the Day of Resurrection, wherein is no doubt; but most of people know not."
وَلِلَّهِ مُلكُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَيَومَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ يَومَئِذ يَخسَرُ المُبطِلُونَ27And unto God belongeth the dominion of the heavens and the earth; and on the Day when the Hour cometh, on that Day the followers of falsehood shall lose.
وَتَرَىٰ كُلَّ أُمَّة جَاثِيَة كُلُّ أُمَّة تُدعَىٰ إِلَىٰ كِتَٰبِهَا اليَومَ تُجزَونَ مَا كُنتُم تَعمَلُونَ28And thou shalt see every nation kneeling. Every nation shall be called unto its book: "This Day ye are recompensed for what ye used to do.369
هَٰذَا كِتَٰبُنَا يَنطِقُ عَلَيكُم بِالحَقِّ إِنَّا كُنَّا نَستَنسِخُ مَا كُنتُم تَعمَلُونَ29This is Our Book that speaketh against you with truth; verily, We used to transcribe what ye used to do."370
فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ فَيُدخِلُهُم رَبُّهُم فِي رَحمَتِهِ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ الفَوزُ المُبِينُ30Then as for those who believed and worked righteous deeds, their Lord shall admit them into His mercy. That is the manifest triumph.
وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ أَفَلَم تَكُن ءَايَٰتِي تُتلَىٰ عَلَيكُم فَاستَكبَرتُم وَكُنتُم قَوما مُّجرِمِينَ31But as for those who concealed: "Were not My signs recited unto you? But ye were proud and ye were a guilty people."
وَإِذَا قِيلَ إِنَّ وَعدَ اللَّهِ حَقّ وَالسَّاعَةُ لَا رَيبَ فِيهَا قُلتُم مَّا نَدرِي مَا السَّاعَةُ إِن نَّظُنُّ إِلَّا ظَنّا وَمَا نَحنُ بِمُستَيقِنِينَ32And when it was said: "Verily, the promise of God is true, and the Hour, there is no doubt thereof," ye said: "We know not what the Hour is; we suppose it naught but conjecture, and we are not certain."
وَبَدَا لَهُم سَيِّـَاتُ مَا عَمِلُواْ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُواْ بِهِ يَستَهزِءُونَ33And the evil of what they did shall appear unto them, and there shall encompass them that which they used to mock.
وَقِيلَ اليَومَ نَنسَىٰكُم كَمَا نَسِيتُم لِقَاءَ يَومِكُم هَٰذَا وَمَأوَىٰكُمُ النَّارُ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ34And it shall be said: "This Day We forget you even as ye forgot the meeting of this Day of yours; and your abode is the Fire, and for you there are no helpers.
ذَٰلِكُم بِأَنَّكُمُ اتَّخَذتُم ءَايَٰتِ اللَّهِ هُزُوا وَغَرَّتكُمُ الحَيَوٰةُ الدُّنيَا فَاليَومَ لَا يُخرَجُونَ مِنهَا وَلَا هُم يُستَعتَبُونَ35That is because ye took the signs of God in mockery, and the life of this world deceived you." So this Day they shall not be brought forth from it, nor shall they be allowed to make amends.
فَلِلَّهِ الحَمدُ رَبِّ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَرَبِّ الأَرضِ رَبِّ العَٰلَمِينَ36So unto God be praise, Lord of the heavens and Lord of the earth, Lord of the worlds.
وَلَهُ الكِبرِيَاءُ فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَهُوَ العَزِيزُ الحَكِيمُ37And unto Him belongeth the grandeur in the heavens and the earth; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.

366 Verse 13 ("He hath subjected unto you whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, all from Him") echoes 31:20 and connects to the cosmic subjection theme across Qur'anic cosmology. The "kneeling" () of nations before their books parallels 69:19–29 where individuals receive their ...

367 Verse 23: "him who taketh as his god his own desire ()." -- means to desire, to love, but also to fall, to plunge. Desire-as-god is desire-as-gravity: it pulls downward. The verse continues: "God sendeth him astray knowingly, and sealeth up his hearing and his heart, and setteth on his sight a co...

368 Verse 24 ("naught destroyeth us save Time/") presents the materialist position. The denial of resurrection by appeal to Time connects to 76:1 where "a span of time" () precedes human existence — time encompasses humanity, not the reverse. Verse 13 ("He hath subjected unto you whatsoever is in the...

369 Verse 28: "thou shalt see every nation kneeling ()." -- means to kneel, to crouch, to sink to one's knees. This is not the prostration () of worship but the involuntary kneeling of those overwhelmed — every nation brought low before its own record. The kneeling is the ẓāhir of judgement: the body...

370 Verse 29: "We used to transcribe () what ye used to do." -- means to copy, to transcribe, to abrogate. God transcribes human deeds — a divine scribal act. The Book (v.29) "speaketh against you with truth." The record is not passive but active: it speaks, it testifies. Every deed is written in a b...

Surah 46: Al-Ahqaf — The Sand-Dunes

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

حم1Ha, Mim.
تَنزِيلُ الكِتَٰبِ مِنَ اللَّهِ العَزِيزِ الحَكِيمِ2The sending down of the Book is from God, the Mighty, the Wise.
مَا خَلَقنَا السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ وَمَا بَينَهُمَا إِلَّا بِالحَقِّ وَأَجَل مُّسَمّى وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ عَمَّا أُنذِرُواْ مُعرِضُونَ3We created not the heavens and the earth and what is between them save in truth and an appointed term; and those who conceal turn away from that of which they are warned.
قُل أَرَءَيتُم مَّا تَدعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَرُونِي مَاذَا خَلَقُواْ مِنَ الأَرضِ أَم لَهُم شِرك فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ ائتُونِي بِكِتَٰب مِّن قَبلِ هَٰذَا أَو أَثَٰرَة مِّن عِلمٍ إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ4Say: "Have ye considered what ye call upon besides God? Show me what they have created of the earth, or have they a share in the heavens? Bring me a book before this, or some remnant of knowledge, if ye be truthful."
وَمَن أَضَلُّ مِمَّن يَدعُواْ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ مَن لَّا يَستَجِيبُ لَهُ إِلَىٰ يَومِ القِيَٰمَةِ وَهُم عَن دُعَائِهِم غَٰفِلُونَ5And who is more astray than he who calleth upon, besides God, those who will not answer him unto the Day of Resurrection, and who are heedless of their calling?
وَإِذَا حُشِرَ النَّاسُ كَانُواْ لَهُم أَعدَاء وَكَانُواْ بِعِبَادَتِهِم كَٰفِرِينَ6And when people are gathered, they shall be enemies unto them and shall deny their worship.
وَإِذَا تُتلَىٰ عَلَيهِم ءَايَٰتُنَا بَيِّنَٰت قَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِلحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُم هَٰذَا سِحر مُّبِينٌ7And when Our signs are recited unto them as clear proofs, those who conceal say of the Truth when it cometh unto them: "This is manifest sorcery."
أَم يَقُولُونَ افتَرَىٰهُ قُل إِنِ افتَرَيتُهُ فَلَا تَملِكُونَ لِي مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيـًا هُوَ أَعلَمُ بِمَا تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ كَفَىٰ بِهِ شَهِيدَا بَينِي وَبَينَكُم وَهُوَ الغَفُورُ الرَّحِيمُ8Or do they say: "He hath fabricated it"? Say: "If I have fabricated it, then ye have no power to do aught for me against God. He is most knowing of what ye utter thereof. He sufficeth as a Witness between me and you; and He is the Forgiving, the Merciful."
قُل مَا كُنتُ بِدعا مِّنَ الرُّسُلِ وَمَا أَدرِي مَا يُفعَلُ بِي وَلَا بِكُم إِن أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ وَمَا أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِير مُّبِين9Say: "I am not a novelty among the messengers, nor do I know what shall be done with me or with you. I follow naught but what is revealed unto me, and I am but a clear warner."371
قُل أَرَءَيتُم إِن كَانَ مِن عِندِ اللَّهِ وَكَفَرتُم بِهِ وَشَهِدَ شَاهِد مِّن بَنِي إِسرَـٰءِيلَ عَلَىٰ مِثلِهِ فَـَامَنَ وَاستَكبَرتُم إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهدِي القَومَ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ10Say: "Have ye considered? If it be from God, and ye conceal it, and a witness from the Children of Israel hath borne witness unto the like thereof and hath believed, while ye are proud? Verily, God guideth not the people of wrongdoing."
وَقَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَو كَانَ خَيرا مَّا سَبَقُونَا إِلَيهِ وَإِذ لَم يَهتَدُواْ بِهِ فَسَيَقُولُونَ هَٰذَا إِفك قَدِيم11And those who conceal say of those who believe: "Had it been good, they would not have preceded us thereto." And since they are not guided thereby, they will say: "This is an ancient lie."
وَمِن قَبلِهِ كِتَٰبُ مُوسَىٰ إِمَاما وَرَحمَة وَهَٰذَا كِتَٰب مُّصَدِّق لِّسَانًا عَرَبِيّا لِّيُنذِرَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ وَبُشرَىٰ لِلمُحسِنِينَ12And before it was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy; and this is a Book confirming, in the Arabic tongue, to warn those who do wrong, and glad tidings for the doers of good.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ قَالُواْ رَبُّنَا اللَّهُ ثُمَّ استَقَٰمُواْ فَلَا خَوفٌ عَلَيهِم وَلَا هُم يَحزَنُونَ13Verily, those who say: "Our Lord is God," then go straight, no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve.
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ أَصحَٰبُ الجَنَّةِ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا جَزَاءَ بِمَا كَانُواْ يَعمَلُونَ14Those are the companions of the Garden, to abide therein, a recompense for what they used to do.
وَوَصَّينَا الإِنسَٰنَ بِوَٰلِدَيهِ إِحسَٰنًا حَمَلَتهُ أُمُّهُ كُرها وَوَضَعَتهُ كُرها وَحَملُهُ وَفِصَٰلُهُ ثَلَٰثُونَ شَهرًا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغَ أَشُدَّهُ وَبَلَغَ أَربَعِينَ سَنَة قَالَ رَبِّ أَوزِعنِي أَن أَشكُرَ نِعمَتَكَ الَّتِي أَنعَمتَ عَلَيَّ وَعَلَىٰ وَٰلِدَيَّ وَأَن أَعمَلَ صَٰلِحا تَرضَىٰهُ وَأَصلِح لِي فِي ذُرِّيَّتِي إِنِّي تُبتُ إِلَيكَ وَإِنِّي مِنَ المُسلِمِينَ15And We have enjoined upon man kindness unto his parents. His mother bore him in hardship and brought him forth in hardship, and his bearing and his weaning are thirty months. Until when he reacheth his full strength and reacheth forty years, he saith: "My Lord! Inspire me to give thanks for Thy favour wherewith Thou hast favoured me and my parents, and that I may work righteousness that pleaseth Thee; and make righteous for me my offspring. Verily, I have turned unto Thee in repentance, and verily, I am of those who have Devoted."372
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ نَتَقَبَّلُ عَنهُم أَحسَنَ مَا عَمِلُواْ وَنَتَجَاوَزُ عَن سَيِّـَاتِهِم فِي أَصحَٰبِ الجَنَّةِ وَعدَ الصِّدقِ الَّذِي كَانُواْ يُوعَدُونَ16Those are they from whom We accept the best of what they have done and overlook their evil deeds, among the companions of the Garden — the promise of truth which they were promised.
وَالَّذِي قَالَ لِوَٰلِدَيهِ أُفّ لَّكُمَا أَتَعِدَانِنِي أَن أُخرَجَ وَقَد خَلَتِ القُرُونُ مِن قَبلِي وَهُمَا يَستَغِيثَانِ اللَّهَ وَيلَكَ ءَامِن إِنَّ وَعدَ اللَّهِ حَقّ فَيَقُولُ مَا هَٰذَا إِلَّا أَسَٰطِيرُ الأَوَّلِينَ17But he who saith unto his parents: "Fie upon you both! Do ye promise me that I shall be brought forth, when generations before me have passed away?" And they both cry unto God for help: "Woe unto thee! Believe! Verily, the promise of God is true." But he saith: "This is naught but tales of the ancients."
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ حَقَّ عَلَيهِمُ القَولُ فِي أُمَم قَد خَلَت مِن قَبلِهِم مِّنَ الجِنِّ وَالإِنسِ إِنَّهُم كَانُواْ خَٰسِرِينَ18Those are they upon whom the word hath been fulfilled, among nations of the spectres and humankind that have passed before them. Verily, they are the losers.
وَلِكُلّ دَرَجَٰت مِّمَّا عَمِلُواْ وَلِيُوَفِّيَهُم أَعمَٰلَهُم وَهُم لَا يُظلَمُونَ19And for each are degrees according to what they have done, and that He may pay them in full for their deeds, and they shall not be wronged.
وَيَومَ يُعرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ عَلَى النَّارِ أَذهَبتُم طَيِّبَٰتِكُم فِي حَيَاتِكُمُ الدُّنيَا وَاستَمتَعتُم بِهَا فَاليَومَ تُجزَونَ عَذَابَ الهُونِ بِمَا كُنتُم تَستَكبِرُونَ فِي الأَرضِ بِغَيرِ الحَقِّ وَبِمَا كُنتُم تَفسُقُونَ20And on the Day when those who conceal are exposed unto the Fire: "Ye squandered your good things in the life of this world and took your enjoyment therein. So this Day ye are recompensed with the chastisement of humiliation for that ye used to be proud upon the earth without right, and for that ye used to act wickedly."
وَاذكُر أَخَا عَادٍ إِذ أَنذَرَ قَومَهُ بِالأَحقَافِ وَقَد خَلَتِ النُّذُرُ مِن بَينِ يَدَيهِ وَمِن خَلفِهِ أَلَّا تَعبُدُواْ إِلَّا اللَّهَ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيكُم عَذَابَ يَومٍ عَظِيم21And remember the brother of Ad, when he warned his people amid the sand-dunes — and warners have passed away before him and after him — "Worship none but God; verily, I fear for you the chastisement of a mighty Day."373
قَالُواْ أَجِئتَنَا لِتَأفِكَنَا عَن ءَالِهَتِنَا فَأتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَا إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الصَّـٰدِقِينَ22They said: "Hast thou come to turn us away from our gods? Then bring us that which thou threatenest us, if thou art of the truthful."
قَالَ إِنَّمَا العِلمُ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَأُبَلِّغُكُم مَّا أُرسِلتُ بِهِ وَلَٰكِنِّي أَرَىٰكُم قَوما تَجهَلُونَ23He said: "The knowledge is only with God, and I convey unto you that wherewith I have been sent, but I see you are a people of ignorance."
فَلَمَّا رَأَوهُ عَارِضا مُّستَقبِلَ أَودِيَتِهِم قَالُواْ هَٰذَا عَارِض مُّمطِرُنَا بَل هُوَ مَا استَعجَلتُم بِهِ رِيح فِيهَا عَذَابٌ أَلِيم24Then when they saw it as a cloud advancing toward their valleys, they said: "This is a cloud that shall give us rain!" Nay, it is that which ye sought to hasten: a wind wherein is a painful chastisement,
تُدَمِّرُ كُلَّ شَيءِ بِأَمرِ رَبِّهَا فَأَصبَحُواْ لَا يُرَىٰ إِلَّا مَسَٰكِنُهُم كَذَٰلِكَ نَجزِي القَومَ المُجرِمِينَ25destroying all things by the command of its Lord. And in the morning naught was to be seen save their dwellings. Thus do We recompense the guilty people.
وَلَقَد مَكَّنَّـٰهُم فِيمَا إِن مَّكَّنَّـٰكُم فِيهِ وَجَعَلنَا لَهُم سَمعا وَأَبصَٰرا وَأَفـِدَة فَمَا أَغنَىٰ عَنهُم سَمعُهُم وَلَا أَبصَٰرُهُم وَلَا أَفـِدَتُهُم مِّن شَيءٍ إِذ كَانُواْ يَجحَدُونَ بِـَايَٰتِ اللَّهِ وَحَاقَ بِهِم مَّا كَانُواْ بِهِ يَستَهزِءُونَ26And verily, We had established them in that wherein We have not established you, and We had appointed for them hearing and sight and hearts; but their hearing and their sight and their hearts availed them naught, since they used to deny the signs of God, and there encompassed them that which they used to mock.
وَلَقَد أَهلَكنَا مَا حَولَكُم مِّنَ القُرَىٰ وَصَرَّفنَا الأيَٰتِ لَعَلَّهُم يَرجِعُونَ27And verily, We have destroyed the townships round about you, and We have set forth the signs in diverse ways, that haply they might return.
فَلَولَا نَصَرَهُمُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُواْ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ قُربَانًا ءَالِهَةَ بَل ضَلُّواْ عَنهُم وَذَٰلِكَ إِفكُهُم وَمَا كَانُواْ يَفتَرُونَ28Then why did those whom they had taken as gods besides God, as a means of approach, not help them? Nay, they strayed away from them. And that was their lie, and what they used to fabricate.
وَإِذ صَرَفنَا إِلَيكَ نَفَرا مِّنَ الجِنِّ يَستَمِعُونَ القُرءَانَ فَلَمَّا حَضَرُوهُ قَالُواْ أَنصِتُواْ فَلَمَّا قُضِيَ وَلَّواْ إِلَىٰ قَومِهِم مُّنذِرِينَ29And when We turned toward thee a company of the spectres, hearkening unto the Recitation. And when they were in its presence, they said: "Give ear!" Then when it was ended, they turned back unto their people, warning.
قَالُواْ يَٰقَومَنَا إِنَّا سَمِعنَا كِتَٰبًا أُنزِلَ مِن بَعدِ مُوسَىٰ مُصَدِّقا لِّمَا بَينَ يَدَيهِ يَهدِي إِلَى الحَقِّ وَإِلَىٰ طَرِيق مُّستَقِيم30They said: "O our people! Verily, we have heard a Book sent down after Moses, confirming that which was before it, guiding unto the truth and unto a straight way.
يَٰقَومَنَا أَجِيبُواْ دَاعِيَ اللَّهِ وَءَامِنُواْ بِهِ يَغفِر لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُم وَيُجِركُم مِّن عَذَابٍ أَلِيم31O our people! Respond to the Caller unto God and believe in him, that He may forgive you of your sins and shelter you from a painful chastisement.
وَمَن لَّا يُجِب دَاعِيَ اللَّهِ فَلَيسَ بِمُعجِز فِي الأَرضِ وَلَيسَ لَهُ مِن دُونِهِ أَولِيَاءُ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ فِي ضَلَٰل مُّبِينٍ32And whosoever respondeth not unto the Caller unto God, he cannot frustrate God in the earth, and he hath, apart from Him, no protectors. Those are in manifest error."
أَوَلَم يَرَواْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ وَلَم يَعيَ بِخَلقِهِنَّ بِقَٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰ أَن يُحـِيَ المَوتَىٰ بَلَىٰ إِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِير33Have they not seen that God, Who created the heavens and the earth and was not wearied by their creation, is able to give life unto the dead? Yea, verily He is over all things Powerful.
وَيَومَ يُعرَضُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ عَلَى النَّارِ أَلَيسَ هَٰذَا بِالحَقِّ قَالُواْ بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّنَا قَالَ فَذُوقُواْ العَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُم تَكفُرُونَ34And on the Day when those who conceal are exposed unto the Fire: "Is not this the Truth?" They say: "Yea, by our Lord!" He saith: "Then taste the chastisement for that ye used to conceal."
فَاصبِر كَمَا صَبَرَ أُوْلُواْ العَزمِ مِنَ الرُّسُلِ وَلَا تَستَعجِل لَّهُم كَأَنَّهُم يَومَ يَرَونَ مَا يُوعَدُونَ لَم يَلبَثُواْ إِلَّا سَاعَة مِّن نَّهَارِ بَلَٰغ فَهَل يُهلَكُ إِلَّا القَومُ الفَٰسِقُونَ35So be patient, as the Messengers of firm resolve were patient, and hasten not for them. On the Day when they see that which they are promised, it will be as though they had not tarried save an hour of a day. A conveyance! And shall any be destroyed save the wicked people?

371 Verse 9 ("I am not a novelty among the messengers") connects to 57:26–27 and the theme of progressive revelation: each messenger follows the footsteps of those before. The wind destroying Ad (vv.24–25) parallels 51:41–42 (the barren wind upon Ad) and 54:19–20 (the raging wind). The same event nar...

372 Verse 15: the mature human at forty years prays for gratitude toward parents and righteous offspring. connections span the human lifecycle: bearing (), weaning (), and the prayer at full strength. This is the Qur'an's most complete portrait of the human arc from dependence through maturity to dev...

373 Verse 21: the brother of Ad warns "amid the sand-dunes ()." -- refers to curved sand formations — shifting, impermanent ground. The theological setting is precise: the warner stands on unstable terrain, warning people who think they are secure. The sand-dune as metaphor for false security — impre...

Surah 47: Muhammad

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أَضَلَّ أَعمَٰلَهُم1Those who conceal and turn away from the way of God, He shall render their deeds vain.
وَالَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَءَامَنُواْ بِمَا نُزِّلَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّد وَهُوَ الحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِم كَفَّرَ عَنهُم سَيِّـَاتِهِم وَأَصلَحَ بَالَهُم2And those who believe and work righteous deeds and believe in what hath been sent down unto Muhammad — and it is the truth from their Lord — He shall remit from them their evil deeds and set aright their state.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ اتَّبَعُواْ البَٰطِلَ وَأَنَّ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ اتَّبَعُواْ الحَقَّ مِن رَّبِّهِم كَذَٰلِكَ يَضرِبُ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ أَمثَٰلَهُم3That is because those who conceal follow falsehood, and because those who believe follow the truth from their Lord. Thus God setteth forth for people their likenesses.
فَإِذَا لَقِيتُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فَضَربَ الرِّقَابِ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَثخَنتُمُوهُم فَشُدُّواْ الوَثَاقَ فَإِمَّا مَنَّا بَعدُ وَإِمَّا فِدَاءً حَتَّىٰ تَضَعَ الحَربُ أَوزَارَهَا ذَٰلِكَ وَلَو يَشَاءُ اللَّهُ لَانتَصَرَ مِنهُم وَلَٰكِن لِّيَبلُوَاْ بَعضَكُم بِبَعض وَالَّذِينَ قُتِلُواْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَلَن يُضِلَّ أَعمَٰلَهُم4So when ye meet those who conceal, then strike the necks until, when ye have routed them, then bind them fast. Then either grace or ransom, until the war layeth down its burdens. Thus it is; and had God willed, He could have taken vengeance upon them, but that He may try some of you by means of others. And those who are slain in the way of God, He shall never render their deeds vain.
سَيَهدِيهِم وَيُصلِحُ بَالَهُم5He shall guide them and set aright their state,
وَيُدخِلُهُمُ الجَنَّةَ عَرَّفَهَا لَهُم6and shall admit them into the Garden which He hath made known unto them.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِن تَنصُرُواْ اللَّهَ يَنصُركُم وَيُثَبِّت أَقدَامَكُم7O ye who believe! If ye succour God, He will succour you and make firm your feet.374
وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فَتَعسا لَّهُم وَأَضَلَّ أَعمَٰلَهُم8And those who conceal, perdition for them, and He shall render their deeds vain.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُم كَرِهُواْ مَا أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ فَأَحبَطَ أَعمَٰلَهُم9That is because they are averse to what God hath sent down, so He hath rendered their deeds worthless.
أَفَلَم يَسِيرُواْ فِي الأَرضِ فَيَنظُرُواْ كَيفَ كَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبلِهِم دَمَّرَ اللَّهُ عَلَيهِم وَلِلكَٰفِرِينَ أَمثَٰلُهَا10Have they not journeyed in the earth and seen how was the end of those before them? God destroyed them, and for those who conceal is the like thereof.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ مَولَى الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَأَنَّ الكَٰفِرِينَ لَا مَولَىٰ لَهُم11That is because God is the Protector of those who believe, and because those who conceal have no protector.
إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُدخِلُ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ يَتَمَتَّعُونَ وَيَأكُلُونَ كَمَا تَأكُلُ الأَنعَٰمُ وَالنَّارُ مَثوى لَّهُم12Verily, God shall admit those who believe and work righteous deeds into Gardens beneath which rivers flow; and those who conceal take their enjoyment and eat as cattle eat, and the Fire shall be an abode for them.
وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَريَةٍ هِيَ أَشَدُّ قُوَّة مِّن قَريَتِكَ الَّتِي أَخرَجَتكَ أَهلَكنَٰهُم فَلَا نَاصِرَ لَهُم13And how many a township, mightier in strength than thy township which hath driven thee out, have We destroyed, and there was no helper for them.
أَفَمَن كَانَ عَلَىٰ بَيِّنَة مِّن رَّبِّهِ كَمَن زُيِّنَ لَهُ سُوءُ عَمَلِهِ وَاتَّبَعُواْ أَهوَاءَهُم14Is then he who is upon a clear proof from his Lord like him unto whom the evil of his deed is made fair-seeming, and they follow their desires?
مَّثَلُ الجَنَّةِ الَّتِي وُعِدَ المُتَّقُونَ فِيهَا أَنهَٰر مِّن مَّاءٍ غَيرِ ءَاسِن وَأَنهَٰر مِّن لَّبَن لَّم يَتَغَيَّر طَعمُهُ وَأَنهَٰر مِّن خَمر لَّذَّة لِّلشَّـٰرِبِينَ وَأَنهَٰر مِّن عَسَل مُّصَفّى وَلَهُم فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ الثَّمَرَٰتِ وَمَغفِرَة مِّن رَّبِّهِم كَمَن هُوَ خَٰلِد فِي النَّارِ وَسُقُواْ مَاءً حَمِيما فَقَطَّعَ أَمعَاءَهُم15A likeness of the Garden which is promised unto the pious: therein are rivers of water unpolluted, and rivers of milk whose flavour changeth not, and rivers of wine delicious unto the drinkers, and rivers of honey purified; and therein for them is of every fruit, and forgiveness from their Lord. Are they as those who abide in the Fire and are given to drink boiling water that teareth their bowels?
وَمِنهُم مَّن يَستَمِعُ إِلَيكَ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا خَرَجُواْ مِن عِندِكَ قَالُواْ لِلَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ العِلمَ مَاذَا قَالَ ءَانِفًا أُوْلَـٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ طَبَعَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم وَاتَّبَعُواْ أَهوَاءَهُم16And among them are those who hearken unto thee, until when they go forth from thy presence, they say unto those who have been given knowledge: "What said he just now?" Those are they whose hearts God hath sealed, and they follow their desires.
وَالَّذِينَ اهتَدَواْ زَادَهُم هُدى وَءَاتَىٰهُم تَقوَىٰهُم17And those who are guided, He increaseth them in guidance and giveth them their piety.
فَهَل يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا السَّاعَةَ أَن تَأتِيَهُم بَغتَة فَقَد جَاءَ أَشرَاطُهَا فَأَنَّىٰ لَهُم إِذَا جَاءَتهُم ذِكرَىٰهُم18Do they then await aught save the Hour, that it should come upon them suddenly? And verily, its portents have already come. Then how, when it hath come upon them, shall their reminder avail them?
فَاعلَم أَنَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَاستَغفِر لِذَنبِكَ وَلِلمُؤمِنِينَ وَالمُؤمِنَٰتِ وَاللَّهُ يَعلَمُ مُتَقَلَّبَكُم وَمَثوَىٰكُم19So know that there is no god but God, and ask forgiveness for thy sin and for the believing men and the believing women; and God knoweth your going about and your resting place.375
وَيَقُولُ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَولَا نُزِّلَت سُورَة فَإِذَا أُنزِلَت سُورَة مُّحكَمَة وَذُكِرَ فِيهَا القِتَالُ رَأَيتَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَض يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيكَ نَظَرَ المَغشِيِّ عَلَيهِ مِنَ المَوتِ فَأَولَىٰ لَهُم20And those who believe say: "Why hath not a surah been sent down?" Then when a decisive surah is sent down and fighting is mentioned therein, thou seest those in whose hearts is a sickness looking at thee with the look of one fainting at death. So woe unto them!
طَاعَة وَقَول مَّعرُوف فَإِذَا عَزَمَ الأَمرُ فَلَو صَدَقُواْ اللَّهَ لَكَانَ خَيرا لَّهُم21Obedience and a fair word! Then when the matter is resolved, if they were true unto God, it would be better for them.
فَهَل عَسَيتُم إِن تَوَلَّيتُم أَن تُفسِدُواْ فِي الأَرضِ وَتُقَطِّعُواْ أَرحَامَكُم22Would ye then, if ye turned away, work corruption in the earth and sever your ties of kinship?
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَعَنَهُمُ اللَّهُ فَأَصَمَّهُم وَأَعمَىٰ أَبصَٰرَهُم23Those are they whom God hath cursed, so He hath made them deaf and blinded their sight.
أَفَلَا يَتَدَبَّرُونَ القُرءَانَ أَم عَلَىٰ قُلُوبٍ أَقفَالُهَا24Will they not then ponder the Recitation, or are there locks upon their hearts?376
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ارتَدُّواْ عَلَىٰ أَدبَٰرِهِم مِّن بَعدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الهُدَى الشَّيطَٰنُ سَوَّلَ لَهُم وَأَملَىٰ لَهُم25Verily, those who turn back after the guidance hath been made clear unto them, the devil hath seduced them and given them false hopes.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُم قَالُواْ لِلَّذِينَ كَرِهُواْ مَا نَزَّلَ اللَّهُ سَنُطِيعُكُم فِي بَعضِ الأَمرِ وَاللَّهُ يَعلَمُ إِسرَارَهُم26That is because they said unto those who are averse to what God hath sent down: "We will obey you in some of the affair." And God knoweth their secrets.
فَكَيفَ إِذَا تَوَفَّتهُمُ المَلَـٰئِكَةُ يَضرِبُونَ وُجُوهَهُم وَأَدبَٰرَهُم27Then how shall it be when the angels take them, smiting their faces and their backs?
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمُ اتَّبَعُواْ مَا أَسخَطَ اللَّهَ وَكَرِهُواْ رِضوَٰنَهُ فَأَحبَطَ أَعمَٰلَهُم28That is because they followed what angereth God and were averse to His good pleasure, so He rendered their deeds worthless.
أَم حَسِبَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَضٌ أَن لَّن يُخرِجَ اللَّهُ أَضغَٰنَهُم29Or do those in whose hearts is a sickness suppose that God will not bring forth their rancour?
وَلَو نَشَاءُ لَأَرَينَٰكَهُم فَلَعَرَفتَهُم بِسِيمَٰهُم وَلَتَعرِفَنَّهُم فِي لَحنِ القَولِ وَاللَّهُ يَعلَمُ أَعمَٰلَكُم30And had We willed, We would have shown them to thee, so thou wouldst know them by their mark. And thou shalt surely know them by the tone of their speech; and God knoweth your deeds.
وَلَنَبلُوَنَّكُم حَتَّىٰ نَعلَمَ المُجَٰهِدِينَ مِنكُم وَالصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَنَبلُوَاْ أَخبَارَكُم31And We shall surely try you until We know those among you who strive and those who are patient, and We shall test your tidings.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَشَاقُّواْ الرَّسُولَ مِن بَعدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ الهُدَىٰ لَن يَضُرُّواْ اللَّهَ شَيـا وَسَيُحبِطُ أَعمَٰلَهُم32Verily, those who conceal and turn away from the way of God and oppose the Messenger after the guidance hath been made clear unto them, they shall harm God not at all, and He shall render their deeds worthless.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ أَطِيعُواْ اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُواْ الرَّسُولَ وَلَا تُبطِلُواْ أَعمَٰلَكُم33O ye who believe! Obey God and obey the Messenger, and render not your deeds vain.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ ثُمَّ مَاتُواْ وَهُم كُفَّار فَلَن يَغفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُم34Verily, those who conceal and turn away from the way of God, then die while they are concealing, God shall never forgive them.
فَلَا تَهِنُواْ وَتَدعُواْ إِلَى السَّلمِ وَأَنتُمُ الأَعلَونَ وَاللَّهُ مَعَكُم وَلَن يَتِرَكُم أَعمَٰلَكُم35So faint not and call for peace when ye have the upper hand, and God is with you and will not deprive you of your deeds.
إِنَّمَا الحَيَوٰةُ الدُّنيَا لَعِب وَلَهو وَإِن تُؤمِنُواْ وَتَتَّقُواْ يُؤتِكُم أُجُورَكُم وَلَا يَسـَلكُم أَموَٰلَكُم36The life of this world is but play and diversion; and if ye believe and are pious, He will give you your rewards and will not ask of you your possessions.
إِن يَسـَلكُمُوهَا فَيُحفِكُم تَبخَلُواْ وَيُخرِج أَضغَٰنَكُم37Were He to ask them of you and press you, ye would be niggardly, and He would bring forth your rancour.
هَـٰأَنتُم هَـٰؤُلَاءِ تُدعَونَ لِتُنفِقُواْ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَمِنكُم مَّن يَبخَلُ وَمَن يَبخَل فَإِنَّمَا يَبخَلُ عَن نَّفسِهِ وَاللَّهُ الغَنِيُّ وَأَنتُمُ الفُقَرَاءُ وَإِن تَتَوَلَّواْ يَستَبدِل قَومًا غَيرَكُم ثُمَّ لَا يَكُونُواْ أَمثَٰلَكُم38Lo! Ye are those who are called to spend in the way of God, yet among you are those who are niggardly; and whosoever is niggardly is niggardly only against his own soul. And God is the Self-Sufficient, and ye are the needy; and if ye turn away, He will exchange you for another people, and they will not be the likes of you.377

374 Verse 7: "If ye succour () God, He will succour you." means to help, to give victory. The paradox is deliberate — God, Who needs nothing, asks to be succoured. Human effort on God's behalf activates divine aid. The reciprocity is structural: the believer's action unlocks God's response.

375 Verse 19 ("know that there is no god but God") is the surah's theological axis, the shahāda embedded in its middle. -- (to know) here demands not recitation but realization.

376 Verse 24: "Will they not then ponder () the Recitation, or are there locks upon their hearts?" means to consider the end of a matter, to look at what follows. The verse diagnoses the failure to understand as a locked heart, not a weak mind. The Qur'an's demand is not intellectual agreement but co...

377 Verse 38 ("if ye turn away, He will exchange you for another people") echoes 5:54, establishing that no community has permanent tenure — the root -- (exchange) applies to peoples as well as deeds. Verse 19 ("know that there is no god but God") is the surah's theological axis, the shahāda embedded...

Surah 48: Al-Fath — The Victory

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِنَّا فَتَحنَا لَكَ فَتحا مُّبِينا1Verily, We have opened unto thee a manifest victory,378
لِّيَغفِرَ لَكَ اللَّهُ مَا تَقَدَّمَ مِن ذَنبِكَ وَمَا تَأَخَّرَ وَيُتِمَّ نِعمَتَهُ عَلَيكَ وَيَهدِيَكَ صِرَٰطا مُّستَقِيما2that God may forgive thee what hath preceded of thy sin and what shall follow, and may perfect His favour upon thee, and guide thee upon a straight path,
وَيَنصُرَكَ اللَّهُ نَصرًا عَزِيزًا3and that God may succour thee with a mighty succour.
هُوَ الَّذِي أَنزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ فِي قُلُوبِ المُؤمِنِينَ لِيَزدَادُواْ إِيمَٰنا مَّعَ إِيمَٰنِهِم وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلِيمًا حَكِيما4He it is Who sent down the tranquillity into the hearts of the believers, that they might increase in faith along with their faith; and unto God belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth; and God is Knowing, Wise,379
لِّيُدخِلَ المُؤمِنِينَ وَالمُؤمِنَٰتِ جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَيُكَفِّرَ عَنهُم سَيِّـَاتِهِم وَكَانَ ذَٰلِكَ عِندَ اللَّهِ فَوزًا عَظِيما5that He may admit the believing men and the believing women into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein, and may remit from them their evil deeds — and that is in the sight of God a supreme triumph —
وَيُعَذِّبَ المُنَٰفِقِينَ وَالمُنَٰفِقَٰتِ وَالمُشرِكِينَ وَالمُشرِكَٰتِ الظَّانِّينَ بِاللَّهِ ظَنَّ السَّوءِ عَلَيهِم دَائِرَةُ السَّوءِ وَغَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيهِم وَلَعَنَهُم وَأَعَدَّ لَهُم جَهَنَّمَ وَسَاءَت مَصِيرا6and that He may chastise the hypocrite men and the hypocrite women and those men and women who associate, who harbour evil thoughts about God. Upon them is the evil turn of fortune, and God is wroth with them and hath cursed them and hath prepared for them Hell; and wretched is the journeying.
وَلِلَّهِ جُنُودُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيمًا7And unto God belong the hosts of the heavens and the earth; and God is Mighty, Wise.
إِنَّا أَرسَلنَٰكَ شَٰهِدا وَمُبَشِّرا وَنَذِيرا8Verily, We have sent thee as a witness and a bearer of glad tidings and a warner,
لِّتُؤمِنُواْ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُعَزِّرُوهُ وَتُوَقِّرُوهُ وَتُسَبِّحُوهُ بُكرَة وَأَصِيلًا9that ye may believe in God and His Messenger, and may honour him and revere him, and may glorify Him morning and evening.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُبَايِعُونَكَ إِنَّمَا يُبَايِعُونَ اللَّهَ يَدُ اللَّهِ فَوقَ أَيدِيهِم فَمَن نَّكَثَ فَإِنَّمَا يَنكُثُ عَلَىٰ نَفسِهِ وَمَن أَوفَىٰ بِمَا عَٰهَدَ عَلَيهُ اللَّهَ فَسَيُؤتِيهِ أَجرًا عَظِيما10Verily, those who pledge allegiance unto thee do but pledge allegiance unto God. The hand of God is over their hands. So whosoever breaketh his oath, he breaketh it only against his own soul; and whosoever fulfilleth that which he hath covenanted with God, He shall give him a great reward.380
سَيَقُولُ لَكَ المُخَلَّفُونَ مِنَ الأَعرَابِ شَغَلَتنَا أَموَٰلُنَا وَأَهلُونَا فَاستَغفِر لَنَا يَقُولُونَ بِأَلسِنَتِهِم مَّا لَيسَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم قُل فَمَن يَملِكُ لَكُم مِّنَ اللَّهِ شَيـًا إِن أَرَادَ بِكُم ضَرًّا أَو أَرَادَ بِكُم نَفعَا بَل كَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ خَبِيرَا11The desert Arabs who were left behind will say unto thee: "Our possessions and our families kept us busy, so ask forgiveness for us." They say with their tongues what is not in their hearts. Say: "Who hath any power for you against God, if He desireth for you harm or desireth for you benefit? Nay, God is ever Aware of what ye do."
بَل ظَنَنتُم أَن لَّن يَنقَلِبَ الرَّسُولُ وَالمُؤمِنُونَ إِلَىٰ أَهلِيهِم أَبَدا وَزُيِّنَ ذَٰلِكَ فِي قُلُوبِكُم وَظَنَنتُم ظَنَّ السَّوءِ وَكُنتُم قَومَا بُورا12Nay, ye supposed that the Messenger and the believers would never return unto their families, and that was made fair-seeming in your hearts, and ye harboured an evil thought, and ye were a people doomed.
وَمَن لَّم يُؤمِن بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ فَإِنَّا أَعتَدنَا لِلكَٰفِرِينَ سَعِيرا13And whosoever believeth not in God and His Messenger — then verily, We have prepared for those who conceal a Blaze.
وَلِلَّهِ مُلكُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ يَغفِرُ لِمَن يَشَاءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَاءُ وَكَانَ اللَّهُ غَفُورا رَّحِيما14And unto God belongeth the dominion of the heavens and the earth; He forgiveth whom He willeth and chastiseth whom He willeth; and God is Forgiving, Merciful.
سَيَقُولُ المُخَلَّفُونَ إِذَا انطَلَقتُم إِلَىٰ مَغَانِمَ لِتَأخُذُوهَا ذَرُونَا نَتَّبِعكُم يُرِيدُونَ أَن يُبَدِّلُواْ كَلَٰمَ اللَّهِ قُل لَّن تَتَّبِعُونَا كَذَٰلِكُم قَالَ اللَّهُ مِن قَبلُ فَسَيَقُولُونَ بَل تَحسُدُونَنَا بَل كَانُواْ لَا يَفقَهُونَ إِلَّا قَلِيلا15Those who were left behind will say, when ye set forth to take spoils: "Let us follow you." They wish to change the word of God. Say: "Ye shall not follow us; thus God hath said beforehand." Then they will say: "Nay, ye are envious of us." Nay, but they understand not save a little.
قُل لِّلمُخَلَّفِينَ مِنَ الأَعرَابِ سَتُدعَونَ إِلَىٰ قَومٍ أُوْلِي بَأس شَدِيد تُقَٰتِلُونَهُم أَو يُسلِمُونَ فَإِن تُطِيعُواْ يُؤتِكُمُ اللَّهُ أَجرًا حَسَنا وَإِن تَتَوَلَّواْ كَمَا تَوَلَّيتُم مِّن قَبلُ يُعَذِّبكُم عَذَابًا أَلِيما16Say unto those of the desert Arabs who were left behind: "Ye will be called against a people of great might; ye shall fight them, or they shall Surrender. Then if ye obey, God will give you a fair reward; but if ye turn away as ye turned away before, He will chastise you with a painful chastisement."
لَّيسَ عَلَى الأَعمَىٰ حَرَج وَلَا عَلَى الأَعرَجِ حَرَج وَلَا عَلَى المَرِيضِ حَرَج وَمَن يُطِعِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ يُدخِلهُ جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ يُعَذِّبهُ عَذَابًا أَلِيما17There is no blame upon the blind, nor is there blame upon the lame, nor is there blame upon the sick. And whosoever obeyeth God and His Messenger, He shall admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow; and whosoever turneth away, He shall chastise him with a painful chastisement.
لَّقَد رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنِ المُؤمِنِينَ إِذ يُبَايِعُونَكَ تَحتَ الشَّجَرَةِ فَعَلِمَ مَا فِي قُلُوبِهِم فَأَنزَلَ السَّكِينَةَ عَلَيهِم وَأَثَٰبَهُم فَتحا قَرِيبا18God was indeed well pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance unto thee beneath the tree; He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down the tranquillity upon them, and rewarded them with a near victory,
وَمَغَانِمَ كَثِيرَة يَأخُذُونَهَا وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَزِيزًا حَكِيما19and much spoils that they will take; and God is Mighty, Wise.
وَعَدَكُمُ اللَّهُ مَغَانِمَ كَثِيرَة تَأخُذُونَهَا فَعَجَّلَ لَكُم هَٰذِهِ وَكَفَّ أَيدِيَ النَّاسِ عَنكُم وَلِتَكُونَ ءَايَة لِّلمُؤمِنِينَ وَيَهدِيَكُم صِرَٰطا مُّستَقِيما20God hath promised you much spoils that ye will take, and He hath hastened this for you, and hath withheld the hands of people from you, that it may be a sign for the believers, and that He may guide you upon a straight path.
وَأُخرَىٰ لَم تَقدِرُواْ عَلَيهَا قَد أَحَاطَ اللَّهُ بِهَا وَكَانَ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِيرا21And other spoils which ye have not been able to take, God hath already encompassed them; and God is over all things Powerful.
وَلَو قَٰتَلَكُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَوَلَّوُاْ الأَدبَٰرَ ثُمَّ لَا يَجِدُونَ وَلِيّا وَلَا نَصِيرا22And if those who conceal had fought you, they would have turned their backs, then they would have found no protector and no helper.
سُنَّةَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي قَد خَلَت مِن قَبلُ وَلَن تَجِدَ لِسُنَّةِ اللَّهِ تَبدِيلا23It is the way of God that hath passed before; and thou shalt find no change in the way of God.381
وَهُوَ الَّذِي كَفَّ أَيدِيَهُم عَنكُم وَأَيدِيَكُم عَنهُم بِبَطنِ مَكَّةَ مِن بَعدِ أَن أَظفَرَكُم عَلَيهِم وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ بَصِيرًا24And He it is Who withheld their hands from you and your hands from them in the valley of Mecca, after He had given you victory over them; and God is Seeing of what ye do.
هُمُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَصَدُّوكُم عَنِ المَسجِدِ الحَرَامِ وَالهَديَ مَعكُوفًا أَن يَبلُغَ مَحِلَّهُ وَلَولَا رِجَال مُّؤمِنُونَ وَنِسَاء مُّؤمِنَٰت لَّم تَعلَمُوهُم أَن تَطَـُوهُم فَتُصِيبَكُم مِّنهُم مَّعَرَّةُ بِغَيرِ عِلم لِّيُدخِلَ اللَّهُ فِي رَحمَتِهِ مَن يَشَاءُ لَو تَزَيَّلُواْ لَعَذَّبنَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِنهُم عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا25They are those who concealed and turned you away from the Sacred Mosque, and the offering detained from reaching its place. And were it not for believing men and believing women whom ye knew not — lest ye should trample them and there befall you guilt on their account unwittingly — that God might admit into His mercy whom He willeth. Had they been apart, We would have chastised those among them who concealed with a painful chastisement.
إِذ جَعَلَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فِي قُلُوبِهِمُ الحَمِيَّةَ حَمِيَّةَ الجَٰهِلِيَّةِ فَأَنزَلَ اللَّهُ سَكِينَتَهُ عَلَىٰ رَسُولِهِ وَعَلَى المُؤمِنِينَ وَأَلزَمَهُم كَلِمَةَ التَّقوَىٰ وَكَانُواْ أَحَقَّ بِهَا وَأَهلَهَا وَكَانَ اللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلِيما26When those who concealed set in their hearts the zealotry, the zealotry of the Age of Ignorance, then God sent down His tranquillity upon His Messenger and upon the believers, and imposed upon them the word of piety, and they were most worthy thereof and meet for it; and God is of all things Knowing.
لَّقَد صَدَقَ اللَّهُ رَسُولَهُ الرُّءيَا بِالحَقِّ لَتَدخُلُنَّ المَسجِدَ الحَرَامَ إِن شَاءَ اللَّهُ ءَامِنِينَ مُحَلِّقِينَ رُءُوسَكُم وَمُقَصِّرِينَ لَا تَخَافُونَ فَعَلِمَ مَا لَم تَعلَمُواْ فَجَعَلَ مِن دُونِ ذَٰلِكَ فَتحا قَرِيبًا27Verily, God hath fulfilled for His Messenger the vision in truth: "Ye shall surely enter the Sacred Mosque, if God willeth, in security, your heads shaved and your hair cut short, fearing not." He knew what ye knew not, and He appointed beside that a near victory.
هُوَ الَّذِي أَرسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ الحَقِّ لِيُظهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَكَفَىٰ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيدا28He it is Who hath sent His Messenger with the guidance and the religion of truth, that He may cause it to prevail over all religion; and God sufficeth as a Witness.382
مُّحَمَّد رَّسُولُ اللَّهِ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ أَشِدَّاءُ عَلَى الكُفَّارِ رُحَمَاءُ بَينَهُم تَرَىٰهُم رُكَّعا سُجَّدا يَبتَغُونَ فَضلا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرِضوَٰنا سِيمَاهُم فِي وُجُوهِهِم مِّن أَثَرِ السُّجُودِ ذَٰلِكَ مَثَلُهُم فِي التَّورَىٰةِ وَمَثَلُهُم فِي الإِنجِيلِ كَزَرعٍ أَخرَجَ شَطـَهُ فَـَازَرَهُ فَاستَغلَظَ فَاستَوَىٰ عَلَىٰ سُوقِهِ يُعجِبُ الزُّرَّاعَ لِيَغِيظَ بِهِمُ الكُفَّارَ وَعَدَ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ مِنهُم مَّغفِرَة وَأَجرًا عَظِيمَا29Muhammad is the Messenger of God; and those who are with him are stern against those who conceal, merciful among themselves. Thou seest them bowing, prostrating, seeking bounty from God and good pleasure. Their mark is on their faces from the trace of prostration. That is their likeness in the Torah; and their likeness in the Gospel is as a seed that putteth forth its shoot and strengtheneth it, and it groweth stout and riseth upon its stalk, delighting the sowers, that He may enrage thereby those who conceal. God hath promised those among them who believe and work righteous deeds forgiveness and a great reward.

378 Verse 1: "We have opened () unto thee a manifest victory." means to open, to inaugurate, to unlock. The "victory" is fundamentally an "opening" — not merely military conquest but the unlocking of a new spiritual condition. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah, which this surah references, appeared as a setb...

379 Verses 4, 18, 26: God sends down "the tranquillity ()" three times in this surah. means to be still, to dwell, to be at rest. The tranquillity is a descending force — it enters hearts and produces faith. The repetition is structural: tranquillity at the beginning (v.4), at the pledge (v.18), and ...

380 Verse 10: "those who pledge allegiance () unto thee do but pledge allegiance unto God." means to sell, to make a transaction, to covenant. The bayʿa is a transaction — the believer sells his will to God through the Messenger. The hand of God is "over their hands," making the pledge a three-party ...

381 Verse 23 ("thou shalt find no change in the way of God") repeats 35:43 verbatim, establishing the immutability of divine methods — the same cause produces the same effect across dispensations. Verse 28 ("that He may cause it to prevail over all religion") uses the root --r (to manifest/prevail), ...

382 Verse 28 ("that He may cause it to prevail over all religion") uses the root --r (to manifest/prevail), connecting to the ẓāhir/bāṭin framework — the religion of truth will become manifest over all others. Verse 29's parable of the seed ("putteth forth its shoot and strengtheneth it") uses organi...

Surah 49: Al-Hujurat — The Chambers

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تُقَدِّمُواْ بَينَ يَدَيِ اللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيم1O ye who believe! Put not yourselves forward before God and His Messenger, and fear God; verily, God is Hearing, Knowing.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَرفَعُواْ أَصوَٰتَكُم فَوقَ صَوتِ النَّبِيِّ وَلَا تَجهَرُواْ لَهُ بِالقَولِ كَجَهرِ بَعضِكُم لِبَعضٍ أَن تَحبَطَ أَعمَٰلُكُم وَأَنتُم لَا تَشعُرُونَ2O ye who believe! Raise not your voices above the voice of the Prophet, nor speak loudly unto him as ye speak loudly unto one another, lest your works be rendered vain while ye perceive not.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَغُضُّونَ أَصوَٰتَهُم عِندَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ امتَحَنَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُم لِلتَّقوَىٰ لَهُم مَّغفِرَة وَأَجرٌ عَظِيمٌ3Verily, those who lower their voices in the presence of the Messenger of God, those are they whose hearts God hath tested for piety; for them is forgiveness and a great reward.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يُنَادُونَكَ مِن وَرَاءِ الحُجُرَٰتِ أَكثَرُهُم لَا يَعقِلُونَ4Verily, those who call out unto thee from behind the chambers, most of them have no understanding.383
وَلَو أَنَّهُم صَبَرُواْ حَتَّىٰ تَخرُجَ إِلَيهِم لَكَانَ خَيرا لَّهُم وَاللَّهُ غَفُور رَّحِيم5And had they been patient until thou camest forth unto them, it would have been better for them; and God is Forgiving, Merciful.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِن جَاءَكُم فَاسِقُ بِنَبَإ فَتَبَيَّنُواْ أَن تُصِيبُواْ قَومَا بِجَهَٰلَة فَتُصبِحُواْ عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلتُم نَٰدِمِينَ6O ye who believe! If a wicked one cometh unto you with tidings, verify it, lest ye smite a people in ignorance and then repent of what ye have done.
وَاعلَمُواْ أَنَّ فِيكُم رَسُولَ اللَّهِ لَو يُطِيعُكُم فِي كَثِير مِّنَ الأَمرِ لَعَنِتُّم وَلَٰكِنَّ اللَّهَ حَبَّبَ إِلَيكُمُ الإِيمَٰنَ وَزَيَّنَهُ فِي قُلُوبِكُم وَكَرَّهَ إِلَيكُمُ الكُفرَ وَالفُسُوقَ وَالعِصيَانَ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الرَّـٰشِدُونَ7And know that the Messenger of God is among you. Were he to obey you in much of the affair, ye would surely suffer; but God hath endeared the faith unto you and hath adorned it in your hearts, and hath made hateful unto you concealing and wickedness and disobedience. Those are the rightly guided,
فَضلا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَنِعمَة وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيم8a bounty from God and a grace; and God is Knowing, Wise.
وَإِن طَائِفَتَانِ مِنَ المُؤمِنِينَ اقتَتَلُواْ فَأَصلِحُواْ بَينَهُمَا فَإِن بَغَت إِحدَىٰهُمَا عَلَى الأُخرَىٰ فَقَٰتِلُواْ الَّتِي تَبغِي حَتَّىٰ تَفِيءَ إِلَىٰ أَمرِ اللَّهِ فَإِن فَاءَت فَأَصلِحُواْ بَينَهُمَا بِالعَدلِ وَأَقسِطُواْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ المُقسِطِينَ9And if two parties of the believers fight, then make peace between them. And if one of them transgresseth against the other, then fight the one that transgresseth until it returneth unto the command of God. And if it return, then make peace between them with justice, and act equitably; verily, God loveth the equitable.
إِنَّمَا المُؤمِنُونَ إِخوَة فَأَصلِحُواْ بَينَ أَخَوَيكُم وَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُم تُرحَمُونَ10The believers are but brothers, so make peace between your two brothers; and fear God, that haply ye may receive mercy.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا يَسخَر قَوم مِّن قَومٍ عَسَىٰ أَن يَكُونُواْ خَيرا مِّنهُم وَلَا نِسَاء مِّن نِّسَاءٍ عَسَىٰ أَن يَكُنَّ خَيرا مِّنهُنَّ وَلَا تَلمِزُواْ أَنفُسَكُم وَلَا تَنَابَزُواْ بِالأَلقَٰبِ بِئسَ الِاسمُ الفُسُوقُ بَعدَ الإِيمَٰنِ وَمَن لَّم يَتُب فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّـٰلِمُونَ11O ye who believe! Let not a people scoff at another people; it may be that they are better than them. Nor let women scoff at other women; it may be that they are better than them. And defame not one another, nor call each other by nicknames. Wretched is the name of wickedness after faith; and whosoever repenteth not, those are the wrongdoers.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ اجتَنِبُواْ كَثِيرا مِّنَ الظَّنِّ إِنَّ بَعضَ الظَّنِّ إِثم وَلَا تَجَسَّسُواْ وَلَا يَغتَب بَّعضُكُم بَعضًا أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُم أَن يَأكُلَ لَحمَ أَخِيهِ مَيتا فَكَرِهتُمُوهُ وَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّاب رَّحِيم12O ye who believe! Shun much suspicion; verily, some suspicion is a sin. And spy not, nor backbite one another. Would one of you love to eat the flesh of his dead brother? Ye would abhor it. And fear God; verily, God is Relenting, Merciful.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنَّا خَلَقنَٰكُم مِّن ذَكَر وَأُنثَىٰ وَجَعَلنَٰكُم شُعُوبا وَقَبَائِلَ لِتَعَارَفُواْ إِنَّ أَكرَمَكُم عِندَ اللَّهِ أَتقَىٰكُم إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَلِيمٌ خَبِير13O people! Verily, We have created you from a male and a female and made you into peoples and tribes, that ye may know one another. Verily, the noblest of you in the sight of God is the most pious of you; verily, God is Knowing, Aware.384
قَالَتِ الأَعرَابُ ءَامَنَّا قُل لَّم تُؤمِنُواْ وَلَٰكِن قُولُواْ أَسلَمنَا وَلَمَّا يَدخُلِ الإِيمَٰنُ فِي قُلُوبِكُم وَإِن تُطِيعُواْ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ لَا يَلِتكُم مِّن أَعمَٰلِكُم شَيـًا إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُور رَّحِيمٌ14The desert Arabs say: "We believe." Say: "Ye have not believed, but say rather: 'We have Devoted,' for the faith hath not yet entered your hearts. And if ye obey God and His Messenger, He will not diminish aught of your works; verily, God is Forgiving, Merciful."385
إِنَّمَا المُؤمِنُونَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ ثُمَّ لَم يَرتَابُواْ وَجَٰهَدُواْ بِأَموَٰلِهِم وَأَنفُسِهِم فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الصَّـٰدِقُونَ15The believers are only those who believe in God and His Messenger, then doubt not, and strive with their wealth and their souls in the way of God. Those are the truthful.
قُل أَتُعَلِّمُونَ اللَّهَ بِدِينِكُم وَاللَّهُ يَعلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرضِ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلِيم16Say: "Would ye teach God your religion, when God knoweth what is in the heavens and what is in the earth? And God is of all things Knowing."
يَمُنُّونَ عَلَيكَ أَن أَسلَمُواْ قُل لَّا تَمُنُّواْ عَلَيَّ إِسلَٰمَكُم بَلِ اللَّهُ يَمُنُّ عَلَيكُم أَن هَدَىٰكُم لِلإِيمَٰنِ إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ17They count it as a favour unto thee that they have Devoted. Say: "Count not your Devotion as a favour unto me; nay, God conferred a favour upon you in that He guided you unto the faith, if ye be truthful."
إِنَّ اللَّهَ يَعلَمُ غَيبَ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ18Verily, God knoweth the unseen of the heavens and the earth; and God is Seeing of what ye do.

383 The surah's name comes from (the chambers, v.4) — private rooms behind which people call out to the Prophet. -- means to enclose, to protect, to seclude. Those who shout from behind enclosures lack understanding; those who lower their voices in the Prophet's presence have hearts tested for piety....

384 Verse 13: "We have made you into peoples and tribes, that ye may know one another ()." -- means to know, to recognize, to be acquainted. The Form VI () is reciprocal: mutual knowing. Diversity of peoples is not an accident but a divine instrument for mutual recognition. The verse continues: "the ...

385 Verse 14: "The desert Arabs say: 'We believe ().' Say: 'Ye have not believed, but say rather: We have devoted (), for the faith hath not yet entered your hearts.'" This verse draws a sharp distinction between outward devotion (islām) and inward faith (īmān). '-m-n (security, trust, faith) require...

Surah 50: Qaf

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

ق وَالقُرءَانِ المَجِيدِ1Qaf. By the glorious Recitation!
بَل عَجِبُواْ أَن جَاءَهُم مُّنذِر مِّنهُم فَقَالَ الكَٰفِرُونَ هَٰذَا شَيءٌ عَجِيبٌ2Nay, they marvel that there hath come unto them a warner from among themselves, and those who conceal say: "This is a wondrous thing!
أَءِذَا مِتنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابا ذَٰلِكَ رَجعُ بَعِيد3When we are dead and become dust? That is a return far-fetched."
قَد عَلِمنَا مَا تَنقُصُ الأَرضُ مِنهُم وَعِندَنَا كِتَٰبٌ حَفِيظُ4We know what the earth diminisheth of them, and with Us is a Book that preserveth.
بَل كَذَّبُواْ بِالحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُم فَهُم فِي أَمر مَّرِيجٍ5Nay, but they denied the Truth when it came unto them, and they are in a confused affair.
أَفَلَم يَنظُرُواْ إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَوقَهُم كَيفَ بَنَينَٰهَا وَزَيَّنَّـٰهَا وَمَا لَهَا مِن فُرُوج6Have they not looked at the heaven above them, how We built it and adorned it, and it hath no rifts?386
وَالأَرضَ مَدَدنَٰهَا وَأَلقَينَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِيَ وَأَنبَتنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوجِ بَهِيج7And the earth, We spread it out and cast therein firm mountains, and caused to grow therein of every beauteous pair,
تَبصِرَة وَذِكرَىٰ لِكُلِّ عَبد مُّنِيب8as a sight and a reminder for every servant who turneth in repentance.
وَنَزَّلنَا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاء مُّبَٰرَكا فَأَنبَتنَا بِهِ جَنَّـٰت وَحَبَّ الحَصِيدِ9And We sent down from the heaven blessed water, and caused to grow thereby gardens and the grain of the harvest,
وَالنَّخلَ بَاسِقَٰت لَّهَا طَلع نَّضِيد10and tall palm-trees with ranged clusters,
رِّزقا لِّلعِبَادِ وَأَحيَينَا بِهِ بَلدَة مَّيتا كَذَٰلِكَ الخُرُوجُ11a provision for the servants; and We revived thereby a dead land. Thus shall be the coming forth.
كَذَّبَت قَبلَهُم قَومُ نُوح وَأَصحَٰبُ الرَّسِّ وَثَمُودُ12Before them denied the people of Noah, and the companions of the Well, and Thamud,
وَعَاد وَفِرعَونُ وَإِخوَٰنُ لُوط13and Ad, and Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,
وَأَصحَٰبُ الأَيكَةِ وَقَومُ تُبَّع كُلّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ14and the companions of the Thicket, and the people of Tubba. Each one denied the messengers, so My threat was fulfilled.
أَفَعَيِينَا بِالخَلقِ الأَوَّلِ بَل هُم فِي لَبس مِّن خَلق جَدِيد15Were We then wearied by the first creation? Nay, but they are in confusion about a new creation.
وَلَقَد خَلَقنَا الإِنسَٰنَ وَنَعلَمُ مَا تُوَسوِسُ بِهِ نَفسُهُ وَنَحنُ أَقرَبُ إِلَيهِ مِن حَبلِ الوَرِيدِ16And verily, We created man, and We know what his soul whispereth to him, and We are nearer to him than his jugular vein,387
إِذ يَتَلَقَّى المُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ اليَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمَالِ قَعِيد17when the two receivers receive, seated on the right and on the left.
مَّا يَلفِظُ مِن قَولٍ إِلَّا لَدَيهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيد18He uttereth not a word but there is with him a watcher, ready.
وَجَاءَت سَكرَةُ المَوتِ بِالحَقِّ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنتَ مِنهُ تَحِيدُ19And the stupor of death cometh in truth: "This is that from which thou didst shrink."
وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ذَٰلِكَ يَومُ الوَعِيدِ20And the Trumpet is blown: "This is the Day of the Threat."
وَجَاءَت كُلُّ نَفس مَّعَهَا سَائِق وَشَهِيد21And every soul cometh, with it a driver and a witness.
لَّقَد كُنتَ فِي غَفلَة مِّن هَٰذَا فَكَشَفنَا عَنكَ غِطَاءَكَ فَبَصَرُكَ اليَومَ حَدِيد22"Thou wast indeed heedless of this; now We have removed from thee thy covering, so thy sight today is sharp."
وَقَالَ قَرِينُهُ هَٰذَا مَا لَدَيَّ عَتِيدٌ23And his companion saith: "This is what is with me, ready."
أَلقِيَا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيد24"Cast into Hell every stubborn one who concealeth,
مَّنَّاع لِّلخَيرِ مُعتَد مُّرِيبٍ25hinderer of good, transgressor, doubter,
الَّذِي جَعَلَ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ فَأَلقِيَاهُ فِي العَذَابِ الشَّدِيدِ26who set up with God another god; so cast him into the severe chastisement."
قَالَ قَرِينُهُ رَبَّنَا مَا أَطغَيتُهُ وَلَٰكِن كَانَ فِي ضَلَٰلِ بَعِيد27His companion saith: "Our Lord! I led him not into transgression, but he was in far error."
قَالَ لَا تَختَصِمُواْ لَدَيَّ وَقَد قَدَّمتُ إِلَيكُم بِالوَعِيدِ28He saith: "Dispute not in My presence; I had already sent unto you the threat.
مَا يُبَدَّلُ القَولُ لَدَيَّ وَمَا أَنَا بِظَلَّـٰم لِّلعَبِيدِ29The word is not changed with Me, and I am not unjust unto the servants."
يَومَ نَقُولُ لِجَهَنَّمَ هَلِ امتَلَأتِ وَتَقُولُ هَل مِن مَّزِيد30On the Day when We say unto Hell: "Art thou filled?" and it saith: "Is there yet more?"
وَأُزلِفَتِ الجَنَّةُ لِلمُتَّقِينَ غَيرَ بَعِيدٍ31And the Garden is brought nigh unto the pious, not far off.
هَٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ حَفِيظ32"This is what ye were promised, for every one who turneth in repentance, who keepeth watch,
مَّن خَشِيَ الرَّحمَٰنَ بِالغَيبِ وَجَاءَ بِقَلب مُّنِيبٍ33who feareth the All-Merciful in the unseen and cometh with a heart turning in repentance.
ادخُلُوهَا بِسَلَٰم ذَٰلِكَ يَومُ الخُلُودِ34Enter it in peace; that is the Day of Eternity."
لَهُم مَّا يَشَاءُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَينَا مَزِيد35Theirs is what they will therein, and with Us is yet more.
وَكَم أَهلَكنَا قَبلَهُم مِّن قَرنٍ هُم أَشَدُّ مِنهُم بَطشا فَنَقَّبُواْ فِي البِلَٰدِ هَل مِن مَّحِيصٍ36And how many a generation We destroyed before them, mightier than them in prowess, and they searched about in the lands: is there any refuge?
إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكرَىٰ لِمَن كَانَ لَهُ قَلبٌ أَو أَلقَى السَّمعَ وَهُوَ شَهِيد37Verily, in that is a reminder for him who hath a heart or giveth ear and is a witness.
وَلَقَد خَلَقنَا السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ وَمَا بَينَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّام وَمَا مَسَّنَا مِن لُّغُوب38And verily, We created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days, and no weariness touched Us.
فَاصبِر عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّح بِحَمدِ رَبِّكَ قَبلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمسِ وَقَبلَ الغُرُوبِ39So be patient at what they say, and glorify the praise of thy Lord before the rising of the sun and before the setting,
وَمِنَ الَّيلِ فَسَبِّحهُ وَأَدبَٰرَ السُّجُودِ40and in the night glorify Him, and after the prostrations.
وَاستَمِع يَومَ يُنَادِ المُنَادِ مِن مَّكَان قَرِيب41And hearken on the Day when the Caller shall call from a near place,
يَومَ يَسمَعُونَ الصَّيحَةَ بِالحَقِّ ذَٰلِكَ يَومُ الخُرُوجِ42the Day when they shall hear the Shout in truth. That is the Day of Coming Forth.
إِنَّا نَحنُ نُحيِ وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَينَا المَصِيرُ43Verily, We, it is We Who give life and cause death, and unto Us is the journeying,
يَومَ تَشَقَّقُ الأَرضُ عَنهُم سِرَاعا ذَٰلِكَ حَشرٌ عَلَينَا يَسِير44on the Day when the earth shall be rent asunder from them, hastening forth. That is a gathering easy for Us.
نَّحنُ أَعلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ وَمَا أَنتَ عَلَيهِم بِجَبَّار فَذَكِّر بِالقُرءَانِ مَن يَخَافُ وَعِيدِ45We know best what they say, and thou art not a tyrant over them. So remind by the Recitation him who feareth My threat.

386 Verse 6: "how We built it and adorned it, and it hath no rifts ()." -- means to open, to crack, to split. The heaven is seamless — no cracks, no flaws. This connects to 67:3 where the same challenge is issued: "seest thou any flaw ()?" The perfection of the heavens is a sign: if the cosmos has no...

387 Verse 16: "We are nearer to him than his jugular vein ( )." -- (to be near) describes God's proximity as more intimate than the body's own circulatory system. The jugular vein carries blood to the brain — the seat of consciousness. God is nearer than one's own awareness of self. This is the Qur'a...

Surah 51: Adh-Dhariyat — The Scatterers

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالذَّـٰرِيَٰتِ ذَروا1By the scatterers that scatter,388
فَالحَٰمِلَٰتِ وِقرا2then the bearers of burdens,
فَالجَٰرِيَٰتِ يُسرا3then the runners with ease,
فَالمُقَسِّمَٰتِ أَمرًا4then the apportioners of the command!
إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَصَادِق5Verily, that which ye are promised is true,
وَإِنَّ الدِّينَ لَوَٰقِع6and verily, the Judgement shall come to pass.
وَالسَّمَاءِ ذَاتِ الحُبُكِ7By the heaven of pathways,
إِنَّكُم لَفِي قَول مُّختَلِف8verily, ye are in discordant speech;
يُؤفَكُ عَنهُ مَن أُفِكَ9turned away therefrom is he who is turned away.
قُتِلَ الخَرَّـٰصُونَ10Perish the conjecturers,
الَّذِينَ هُم فِي غَمرَة سَاهُونَ11who are heedless in a flood of confusion.
يَسـَلُونَ أَيَّانَ يَومُ الدِّينِ12They ask: "When is the Day of Judgement?"
يَومَ هُم عَلَى النَّارِ يُفتَنُونَ13The Day when they are tried over the Fire:
ذُوقُواْ فِتنَتَكُم هَٰذَا الَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِ تَستَعجِلُونَ14"Taste your trial! This is that which ye sought to hasten."
إِنَّ المُتَّقِينَ فِي جَنَّـٰت وَعُيُونٍ15Verily, the pious are amid Gardens and springs,
ءَاخِذِينَ مَا ءَاتَىٰهُم رَبُّهُم إِنَّهُم كَانُواْ قَبلَ ذَٰلِكَ مُحسِنِينَ16receiving what their Lord giveth them; verily, they were before that doers of good.
كَانُواْ قَلِيلا مِّنَ الَّيلِ مَا يَهجَعُونَ17They used to sleep but little of the night,
وَبِالأَسحَارِ هُم يَستَغفِرُونَ18and in the hours before dawn they would seek forgiveness,
وَفِي أَموَٰلِهِم حَقّ لِّلسَّائِلِ وَالمَحرُومِ19and in their wealth was a right for the beggar and the deprived.
وَفِي الأَرضِ ءَايَٰت لِّلمُوقِنِينَ20And in the earth are signs for those who are certain,
وَفِي أَنفُسِكُم أَفَلَا تُبصِرُونَ21and in your own selves; will ye not then see?
وَفِي السَّمَاءِ رِزقُكُم وَمَا تُوعَدُونَ22And in the heaven is your provision and that which ye are promised.
فَوَرَبِّ السَّمَاءِ وَالأَرضِ إِنَّهُ لَحَقّ مِّثلَ مَا أَنَّكُم تَنطِقُونَ23So by the Lord of the heaven and the earth, verily, it is the truth, even as ye speak.
هَل أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ضَيفِ إِبرَٰهِيمَ المُكرَمِينَ24Hath the story of the honoured guests of Abraham reached thee?
إِذ دَخَلُواْ عَلَيهِ فَقَالُواْ سَلَٰما قَالَ سَلَٰم قَوم مُّنكَرُونَ25When they entered upon him and said: "Peace!" he said: "Peace! A people unknown."
فَرَاغَ إِلَىٰ أَهلِهِ فَجَاءَ بِعِجل سَمِين26Then he turned aside unto his family and brought a fatted calf
فَقَرَّبَهُ إِلَيهِم قَالَ أَلَا تَأكُلُونَ27and set it before them; he said: "Will ye not eat?"
فَأَوجَسَ مِنهُم خِيفَة قَالُواْ لَا تَخَف وَبَشَّرُوهُ بِغُلَٰمٍ عَلِيم28Then he conceived a fear of them. They said: "Fear not." And they gave him glad tidings of a knowing son.
فَأَقبَلَتِ امرَأَتُهُ فِي صَرَّة فَصَكَّت وَجهَهَا وَقَالَت عَجُوزٌ عَقِيم29Then his wife came forward with a cry and smote her face and said: "A barren old woman!"
قَالُواْ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ إِنَّهُ هُوَ الحَكِيمُ العَلِيمُ30They said: "Even so saith thy Lord; verily, He is the Wise, the Knowing."
قَالَ فَمَا خَطبُكُم أَيُّهَا المُرسَلُونَ31He said: "Then what is your errand, O ye sent ones?"
قَالُواْ إِنَّا أُرسِلنَا إِلَىٰ قَوم مُّجرِمِينَ32They said: "Verily, we have been sent unto a guilty people,
لِنُرسِلَ عَلَيهِم حِجَارَة مِّن طِين33that we may send upon them stones of clay,
مُّسَوَّمَةً عِندَ رَبِّكَ لِلمُسرِفِينَ34marked with thy Lord for the prodigal."
فَأَخرَجنَا مَن كَانَ فِيهَا مِنَ المُؤمِنِينَ35So We brought forth such believers as were therein,
فَمَا وَجَدنَا فِيهَا غَيرَ بَيت مِّنَ المُسلِمِينَ36but We found therein but one house of those who had devoted.
وَتَرَكنَا فِيهَا ءَايَة لِّلَّذِينَ يَخَافُونَ العَذَابَ الأَلِيمَ37And We left therein a sign for those who fear the painful chastisement.
وَفِي مُوسَىٰ إِذ أَرسَلنَٰهُ إِلَىٰ فِرعَونَ بِسُلطَٰن مُّبِين38And in Moses, when We sent him unto Pharaoh with a clear authority.
فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكنِهِ وَقَالَ سَٰحِرٌ أَو مَجنُون39But he turned away with his court and said: "A sorcerer or one possessed."
فَأَخَذنَٰهُ وَجُنُودَهُ فَنَبَذنَٰهُم فِي اليَمِّ وَهُوَ مُلِيم40So We seized him and his hosts and cast them into the sea, and he was blameworthy.
وَفِي عَادٍ إِذ أَرسَلنَا عَلَيهِمُ الرِّيحَ العَقِيمَ41And in Ad, when We sent upon them the barren wind;
مَا تَذَرُ مِن شَيءٍ أَتَت عَلَيهِ إِلَّا جَعَلَتهُ كَالرَّمِيمِ42it left naught that it came upon, but it made it as decayed.
وَفِي ثَمُودَ إِذ قِيلَ لَهُم تَمَتَّعُواْ حَتَّىٰ حِين43And in Thamud, when it was said unto them: "Take your ease awhile."
فَعَتَواْ عَن أَمرِ رَبِّهِم فَأَخَذَتهُمُ الصَّـٰعِقَةُ وَهُم يَنظُرُونَ44But they rebelled against the command of their Lord, so the thunderbolt seized them while they looked on.
فَمَا استَطَٰعُواْ مِن قِيَام وَمَا كَانُواْ مُنتَصِرِينَ45And they were not able to rise, nor could they help themselves.
وَقَومَ نُوح مِّن قَبلُ إِنَّهُم كَانُواْ قَوما فَٰسِقِينَ46And the people of Noah before; verily, they were a wicked people.
وَالسَّمَاءَ بَنَينَٰهَا بِأَييْد وَإِنَّا لَمُوسِعُونَ47And the heaven, We built it with might, and verily, We are the expanders.389
وَالأَرضَ فَرَشنَٰهَا فَنِعمَ المَٰهِدُونَ48And the earth, We spread it; and how excellent are the spreaders!
وَمِن كُلِّ شَيءٍ خَلَقنَا زَوجَينِ لَعَلَّكُم تَذَكَّرُونَ49And of all things We created pairs, that haply ye may reflect.
فَفِرُّواْ إِلَى اللَّهِ إِنِّي لَكُم مِّنهُ نَذِير مُّبِين50So flee unto God; verily, I am unto you from Him a clear warner.
وَلَا تَجعَلُواْ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا ءَاخَرَ إِنِّي لَكُم مِّنهُ نَذِير مُّبِين51And set not up with God another god; verily, I am unto you from Him a clear warner.
كَذَٰلِكَ مَا أَتَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبلِهِم مِّن رَّسُولٍ إِلَّا قَالُواْ سَاحِرٌ أَو مَجنُونٌ52Even so, there came not unto those before them any messenger but they said: "A sorcerer or one possessed!"
أَتَوَاصَواْ بِهِ بَل هُم قَوم طَاغُونَ53Have they bequeathed it to one another? Nay, they are a transgressing people.
فَتَوَلَّ عَنهُم فَمَا أَنتَ بِمَلُوم54So turn away from them, for thou art not blameworthy.
وَذَكِّر فَإِنَّ الذِّكرَىٰ تَنفَعُ المُؤمِنِينَ55And remind, for verily the reminder profiteth the believers.
وَمَا خَلَقتُ الجِنَّ وَالإِنسَ إِلَّا لِيَعبُدُونِ56And I created not the spectres and humankind save that they might worship Me.390
مَا أُرِيدُ مِنهُم مِّن رِّزق وَمَا أُرِيدُ أَن يُطعِمُونِ57I desire not from them any provision, nor do I desire that they should feed Me.
إِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الرَّزَّاقُ ذُو القُوَّةِ المَتِينُ58Verily, God, He is the Provider, the Lord of Might, the Firm.
فَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُواْ ذَنُوبا مِّثلَ ذَنُوبِ أَصحَٰبِهِم فَلَا يَستَعجِلُونِ59And verily, for those who do wrong is a portion like the portion of their fellows, so let them not hasten Me.
فَوَيل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن يَومِهِمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ60And woe unto those who conceal, from their Day which they are promised.

388 Verse 1: "By the scatterers that scatter ( )." -- means to scatter, to winnow, to disperse in the wind. The opening oath invokes winds that scatter — the same natural force that destroyed Ad (v.41, "the barren wind"). The scatterers are both creative (spreading seeds, rain) and destructive (winno...

389 Verse 47: "the heaven, We built it with might, and verily, We are the expanders ()." -- means to expand, to be spacious, to encompass. Read in light of modern cosmology, this describes the expanding universe — the heavens are not static but continuously widening. The participle (expanders) is act...
