The distribution rules (v.7: "that it become not a thing taken in turns among the rich of you") establish economic justice: wealth must circulate, not accumul...

457 Verse 21 ("Had We sent down this Recitation upon a mountain, thou wouldst have seen it humbled, rent asunder") connects to 52:1 (the Mount as oath-object) and 7:143 (the mountain crumbles when God manifests). Mountains break under revelation; hearts should not be harder than mountains. Verse 9's ...

Surah 60: Al-Mumtahanah — The Examined

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَتَّخِذُواْ عَدُوِّي وَعَدُوَّكُم أَولِيَاءَ تُلقُونَ إِلَيهِم بِالمَوَدَّةِ وَقَد كَفَرُواْ بِمَا جَاءَكُم مِّنَ الحَقِّ يُخرِجُونَ الرَّسُولَ وَإِيَّاكُم أَن تُؤمِنُواْ بِاللَّهِ رَبِّكُم إِن كُنتُم خَرَجتُم جِهَٰدا فِي سَبِيلِي وَابتِغَاءَ مَرضَاتِي تُسِرُّونَ إِلَيهِم بِالمَوَدَّةِ وَأَنَا أَعلَمُ بِمَا أَخفَيتُم وَمَا أَعلَنتُم وَمَن يَفعَلهُ مِنكُم فَقَد ضَلَّ سَوَاءَ السَّبِيلِ1O ye who believe! Take not My enemy and your enemy as friends, offering them affection, when they have concealed what hath come unto you of the Truth, driving out the Messenger and you because ye believe in God, your Lord. If ye have gone forth to strive in My way and to seek My good pleasure, ye show them secret affection, but I am most knowing of what ye conceal and what ye proclaim. And whosoever among you doeth that hath verily strayed from the right path.
إِن يَثقَفُوكُم يَكُونُواْ لَكُم أَعدَاء وَيَبسُطُواْ إِلَيكُم أَيدِيَهُم وَأَلسِنَتَهُم بِالسُّوءِ وَوَدُّواْ لَو تَكفُرُونَ2If they gain the upper hand over you, they will be enemies unto you, and will stretch out against you their hands and their tongues with evil; and they wish that ye would conceal.
لَن تَنفَعَكُم أَرحَامُكُم وَلَا أَولَٰدُكُم يَومَ القِيَٰمَةِ يَفصِلُ بَينَكُم وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ بَصِير3Your kinsmen and your children shall avail you naught. On the Day of Resurrection He shall separate between you; and God seeth well what ye do.
قَد كَانَت لَكُم أُسوَةٌ حَسَنَة فِي إِبرَٰهِيمَ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ إِذ قَالُواْ لِقَومِهِم إِنَّا بُرَءَـٰؤُاْ مِنكُم وَمِمَّا تَعبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ كَفَرنَا بِكُم وَبَدَا بَينَنَا وَبَينَكُمُ العَدَٰوَةُ وَالبَغضَاءُ أَبَدًا حَتَّىٰ تُؤمِنُواْ بِاللَّهِ وَحدَهُ إِلَّا قَولَ إِبرَٰهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ لَأَستَغفِرَنَّ لَكَ وَمَا أَملِكُ لَكَ مِنَ اللَّهِ مِن شَيء رَّبَّنَا عَلَيكَ تَوَكَّلنَا وَإِلَيكَ أَنَبنَا وَإِلَيكَ المَصِيرُ4There hath been for you a goodly example in Abraham and those with him, when they said unto their people: "Verily, we are quit of you and of that which ye worship besides God. We have concealed your ways, and there hath arisen between us and you enmity and hatred forever, until ye believe in God alone" — save the saying of Abraham unto his father: "I shall surely ask forgiveness for thee, though I have no power to do aught for thee against God." "Our Lord! In Thee we put our trust, and unto Thee we turn in repentance, and unto Thee is the journeying.458
رَبَّنَا لَا تَجعَلنَا فِتنَة لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَاغفِر لَنَا رَبَّنَا إِنَّكَ أَنتَ العَزِيزُ الحَكِيمُ5Our Lord! Make us not a trial for those who conceal, and forgive us, our Lord; verily, Thou art the Mighty, the Wise."
لَقَد كَانَ لَكُم فِيهِم أُسوَةٌ حَسَنَة لِّمَن كَانَ يَرجُواْ اللَّهَ وَاليَومَ الأخِرَ وَمَن يَتَوَلَّ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ الغَنِيُّ الحَمِيدُ6Verily, there hath been for you in them a goodly example, for him who hopeth in God and the Last Day. And whosoever turneth away, then verily God, He is the Self-Sufficient, the Praiseworthy.
عَسَى اللَّهُ أَن يَجعَلَ بَينَكُم وَبَينَ الَّذِينَ عَادَيتُم مِّنهُم مَّوَدَّة وَاللَّهُ قَدِير وَاللَّهُ غَفُور رَّحِيم7It may be that God will place affection between you and those of them with whom ye are at enmity; and God is Powerful; and God is Forgiving, Merciful.459
لَّا يَنهَىٰكُمُ اللَّهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ لَم يُقَٰتِلُوكُم فِي الدِّينِ وَلَم يُخرِجُوكُم مِّن دِيَٰرِكُم أَن تَبَرُّوهُم وَتُقسِطُواْ إِلَيهِم إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ المُقسِطِينَ8God forbiddeth you not, regarding those who fought you not on account of religion nor drove you from your homes, that ye should show them kindness and deal justly with them; verily, God loveth the just.460
إِنَّمَا يَنهَىٰكُمُ اللَّهُ عَنِ الَّذِينَ قَٰتَلُوكُم فِي الدِّينِ وَأَخرَجُوكُم مِّن دِيَٰرِكُم وَظَٰهَرُواْ عَلَىٰ إِخرَاجِكُم أَن تَوَلَّوهُم وَمَن يَتَوَلَّهُم فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الظَّـٰلِمُونَ9God only forbiddeth you, regarding those who fought you on account of religion and drove you from your homes and aided in your expulsion, that ye should befriend them. And whosoever befriendeth them, those are the wrongdoers.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِذَا جَاءَكُمُ المُؤمِنَٰتُ مُهَٰجِرَٰت فَامتَحِنُوهُنَّ اللَّهُ أَعلَمُ بِإِيمَٰنِهِنَّ فَإِن عَلِمتُمُوهُنَّ مُؤمِنَٰت فَلَا تَرجِعُوهُنَّ إِلَى الكُفَّارِ لَا هُنَّ حِلّ لَّهُم وَلَا هُم يَحِلُّونَ لَهُنَّ وَءَاتُوهُم مَّا أَنفَقُواْ وَلَا جُنَاحَ عَلَيكُم أَن تَنكِحُوهُنَّ إِذَا ءَاتَيتُمُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَلَا تُمسِكُواْ بِعِصَمِ الكَوَافِرِ وَسـَلُواْ مَا أَنفَقتُم وَليَسـَلُواْ مَا أَنفَقُواْ ذَٰلِكُم حُكمُ اللَّهِ يَحكُمُ بَينَكُم وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمٌ حَكِيم10O ye who believe! When believing women come unto you as emigrants, examine them. God is most knowing of their faith. Then if ye know them to be believers, return them not unto those who conceal. They are not lawful for them, nor are they lawful for them. And give them what they have spent. And there is no blame upon you to marry them when ye have given them their dues. And hold not to the bonds of the women who conceal, and ask for what ye have spent, and let them ask for what they have spent. That is the judgement of God; He judgeth between you; and God is Knowing, Wise.461
وَإِن فَاتَكُم شَيء مِّن أَزوَٰجِكُم إِلَى الكُفَّارِ فَعَاقَبتُم فَـَاتُواْ الَّذِينَ ذَهَبَت أَزوَٰجُهُم مِّثلَ مَا أَنفَقُواْ وَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ الَّذِي أَنتُم بِهِ مُؤمِنُونَ11And if aught of your wives hath escaped you unto those who conceal, and then ye retaliate, then give those whose wives have gone the like of what they have spent; and fear God, in Whom ye believe.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا جَاءَكَ المُؤمِنَٰتُ يُبَايِعنَكَ عَلَىٰ أَن لَّا يُشرِكنَ بِاللَّهِ شَيـا وَلَا يَسرِقنَ وَلَا يَزنِينَ وَلَا يَقتُلنَ أَولَٰدَهُنَّ وَلَا يَأتِينَ بِبُهتَٰن يَفتَرِينَهُ بَينَ أَيدِيهِنَّ وَأَرجُلِهِنَّ وَلَا يَعصِينَكَ فِي مَعرُوف فَبَايِعهُنَّ وَاستَغفِر لَهُنَّ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُور رَّحِيم12O Prophet! When believing women come unto thee, pledging allegiance unto thee that they will not associate aught with God, nor steal, nor commit adultery, nor slay their children, nor bring a slander which they have devised between their hands and their feet, nor disobey thee in what is right, then accept their allegiance and ask forgiveness of God for them; verily, God is Forgiving, Merciful.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تَتَوَلَّواْ قَومًا غَضِبَ اللَّهُ عَلَيهِم قَد يَئِسُواْ مِنَ الأخِرَةِ كَمَا يَئِسَ الكُفَّارُ مِن أَصحَٰبِ القُبُورِ13O ye who believe! Befriend not a people with whom God is wroth. They have despaired of the Hereafter, even as those who conceal have despaired of the companions of the graves.

458 Verse 4: Abraham says to his people "We have covered over your ways ( )." Here -- is used by the righteous — Abraham "covers over" the false ways of his people, rejecting and concealing them. 's versatility is on display: covering over can be the act of the wicked (covering truth) or the righteou...

459 Verse 7 ("God will place affection between you and those of them with whom ye are at enmity") is a remarkable promise of future reconciliation, connecting to 49:10 ("the believers are but brothers") — enmity is temporal; brotherhood is the divine intention. The examination of women emigrants (v.1...

460 Verses 1, 8–9 carefully distinguish between forbidden and permitted friendship. -- (to be near, to protect, to befriend) is not blanketly prohibited — only alliance with those who actively fight believers on account of religion. Verse 8 explicitly permits kindness to non-combatants: "God forbidde...

461 The surah's title comes from the root -- via the passive participle — "the examined one" (feminine). Verse 10: "When believing women come unto you as emigrants, examine them ()." means to test, to try by fire, to assay. The examination of emigrant women is a test of sincerity — their faith must b...

Surah 61: As-Saff — The Row

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

سَبَّحَ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرضِ وَهُوَ العَزِيزُ الحَكِيمُ1Whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth glorifieth God, and He is the Mighty, the Wise.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لِمَ تَقُولُونَ مَا لَا تَفعَلُونَ2O ye who believe! Wherefore say ye that which ye do not?
كَبُرَ مَقتًا عِندَ اللَّهِ أَن تَقُولُواْ مَا لَا تَفعَلُونَ3Grievously hateful is it in the sight of God that ye say what ye do not.
إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَٰتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنيَٰن مَّرصُوص4Verily, God loveth those who fight in His way in ranks, as though they were a building well-compacted.462
وَإِذ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَومِهِ يَٰقَومِ لِمَ تُؤذُونَنِي وَقَد تَّعلَمُونَ أَنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيكُم فَلَمَّا زَاغُواْ أَزَاغَ اللَّهُ قُلُوبَهُم وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهدِي القَومَ الفَٰسِقِينَ5And when Moses said unto his people: "O my people! Why do ye afflict me, when ye know full well that I am the Messenger of God unto you?" Then when they swerved, God caused their hearts to swerve; and God guideth not the people of wickedness.
وَإِذ قَالَ عِيسَى ابنُ مَريَمَ يَٰبَنِي إِسرَـٰءِيلَ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيكُم مُّصَدِّقا لِّمَا بَينَ يَدَيَّ مِنَ التَّورَىٰةِ وَمُبَشِّرَا بِرَسُول يَأتِي مِن بَعدِي اسمُهُ أَحمَدُ فَلَمَّا جَاءَهُم بِالبَيِّنَٰتِ قَالُواْ هَٰذَا سِحر مُّبِين6And when Jesus, son of Mary, said: "O Children of Israel! Verily, I am the Messenger of God unto you, confirming that which was before me of the Torah, and bearing glad tidings of a Messenger who cometh after me, whose name shall be Ahmad." Then when he came unto them with clear proofs, they said: "This is manifest sorcery."463
وَمَن أَظلَمُ مِمَّنِ افتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ الكَذِبَ وَهُوَ يُدعَىٰ إِلَى الإِسلَٰمِ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهدِي القَومَ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ7And who is more unjust than he who inventeth a lie against God, while he is called unto Devotion? And God guideth not the people of wrongdoing.
يُرِيدُونَ لِيُطفِـُواْ نُورَ اللَّهِ بِأَفوَٰهِهِم وَاللَّهُ مُتِمُّ نُورِهِ وَلَو كَرِهَ الكَٰفِرُونَ8They desire to extinguish the light of God with their mouths, but God shall perfect His light, though those who have concealed be averse.464
هُوَ الَّذِي أَرسَلَ رَسُولَهُ بِالهُدَىٰ وَدِينِ الحَقِّ لِيُظهِرَهُ عَلَى الدِّينِ كُلِّهِ وَلَو كَرِهَ المُشرِكُونَ9He it is Who hath sent His Messenger with the guidance and the religion of truth, that He may cause it to prevail over all religion, though those who associate be averse.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ هَل أَدُلُّكُم عَلَىٰ تِجَٰرَة تُنجِيكُم مِّن عَذَابٍ أَلِيم10O ye who believe! Shall I direct you to a commerce that shall deliver you from a painful chastisement?
تُؤمِنُونَ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَتُجَٰهِدُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ بِأَموَٰلِكُم وَأَنفُسِكُم ذَٰلِكُم خَير لَّكُم إِن كُنتُم تَعلَمُونَ11Ye shall believe in God and His Messenger, and strive in the way of God with your wealth and your souls. That is better for you, if ye but knew.
يَغفِر لَكُم ذُنُوبَكُم وَيُدخِلكُم جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ وَمَسَٰكِنَ طَيِّبَة فِي جَنَّـٰتِ عَدن ذَٰلِكَ الفَوزُ العَظِيمُ12He will forgive you your sins and admit you into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, and goodly dwellings in Gardens of Eternity. That is the supreme triumph.
وَأُخرَىٰ تُحِبُّونَهَا نَصر مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَفَتح قَرِيب وَبَشِّرِ المُؤمِنِينَ13And another thing that ye love: succour from God and a victory near at hand. And give glad tidings unto the believers.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ كُونُواْ أَنصَارَ اللَّهِ كَمَا قَالَ عِيسَى ابنُ مَريَمَ لِلحَوَارِيِّـنَ مَن أَنصَارِي إِلَى اللَّهِ قَالَ الحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحنُ أَنصَارُ اللَّهِ فَـَامَنَت طَّائِفَة مِّن بَنِي إِسرَـٰءِيلَ وَكَفَرَت طَّائِفَة فَأَيَّدنَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ عَلَىٰ عَدُوِّهِم فَأَصبَحُواْ ظَٰهِرِينَ14O ye who believe! Be helpers of God, even as Jesus, son of Mary, said unto the Devoted: "Who are my helpers unto God?" The Returners said: "We are the helpers of God." Then a party of the Children of Israel believed and a party concealed. So We strengthened those who believed against their enemy, and they became the uppermost.465

462 Verse 4: "God loveth those who fight in His way in ranks, as though they were a building well-compacted." -- (to line up, to arrange) gives the surah its name. The image is architectural: faith as a structure where each element supports the others. The is not merely military formation but ordered...

463 Verse 6 contains Jesus's prophecy: "bearing glad tidings of a Messenger who cometh after me, whose name shall be Ahmad." -- yields both (Ahmad, "most praised") and (Muhammad, "repeatedly praised"). The same root gives (al-ḥamd, "the praise") in the Fatiha (1:2). Jesus announces not merely a name ...

464 Verse 8 ("They desire to extinguish the light of God with their mouths") connects to 57:12–13 where light becomes the visible substance dividing believers from hypocrites. The attempt to extinguish light is the attempt to extinguish the ẓāhir of God.

465 Verse 14 uses (al-ḥawāriyyīn) — the disciples of Jesus — from the same root -- as the (ḥūr) of 55:72. Jesus says to the Devoted: "Who are my helpers unto God?" This is the same community as the Devoted of the hidden Realms. meaning "to return" frames the disciples as those who return to God — not...

Surah 62: Al-Jumu'ah — The Congregation

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرضِ المَلِكِ القُدُّوسِ العَزِيزِ الحَكِيمِ1Whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth glorifieth God, the Sovereign, the Holy, the Mighty, the Wise.
هُوَ الَّذِي بَعَثَ فِي الأُمِّيِّـنَ رَسُولا مِّنهُم يَتلُواْ عَلَيهِم ءَايَٰتِهِ وَيُزَكِّيهِم وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ الكِتَٰبَ وَالحِكمَةَ وَإِن كَانُواْ مِن قَبلُ لَفِي ضَلَٰل مُّبِين2He it is Who hath raised among the unlettered ones a Messenger from among themselves, reciting unto them His signs, and purifying them, and teaching them the Book and the Wisdom, though they were before in manifest error.466
وَءَاخَرِينَ مِنهُم لَمَّا يَلحَقُواْ بِهِم وَهُوَ العَزِيزُ الحَكِيمُ3And others among them who have not yet joined them; and He is the Mighty, the Wise.467
ذَٰلِكَ فَضلُ اللَّهِ يُؤتِيهِ مَن يَشَاءُ وَاللَّهُ ذُو الفَضلِ العَظِيمِ4That is the bounty of God; He bestoweth it upon whom He willeth, and God is the Possessor of great bounty.
مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ حُمِّلُواْ التَّورَىٰةَ ثُمَّ لَم يَحمِلُوهَا كَمَثَلِ الحِمَارِ يَحمِلُ أَسفَارَا بِئسَ مَثَلُ القَومِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُواْ بِـَايَٰتِ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ لَا يَهدِي القَومَ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ5The likeness of those who were laden with the Torah, then bore it not, is as the likeness of a donkey bearing books. Wretched is the likeness of the people who deny the signs of God; and God guideth not the people of wrongdoing.468
قُل يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ هَادُواْ إِن زَعَمتُم أَنَّكُم أَولِيَاءُ لِلَّهِ مِن دُونِ النَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُاْ المَوتَ إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ6Say: "O ye who are of Jewry! If ye claim that ye are the friends of God, apart from other people, then wish for death, if ye be truthful."
وَلَا يَتَمَنَّونَهُ أَبَدَا بِمَا قَدَّمَت أَيدِيهِم وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمُ بِالظَّـٰلِمِينَ7But they will never wish for it, because of what their hands have sent before them; and God is Knowing of the wrongdoers.
قُل إِنَّ المَوتَ الَّذِي تَفِرُّونَ مِنهُ فَإِنَّهُ مُلَٰقِيكُم ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَٰلِمِ الغَيبِ وَالشَّهَٰدَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُم تَعمَلُونَ8Say: "Verily, the death from which ye flee, verily it shall meet you; then ye shall be returned unto the Knower of the unseen and the seen, and He shall inform you of what ye used to do."
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِذَا نُودِيَ لِلصَّلَوٰةِ مِن يَومِ الجُمُعَةِ فَاسعَواْ إِلَىٰ ذِكرِ اللَّهِ وَذَرُواْ البَيعَ ذَٰلِكُم خَير لَّكُم إِن كُنتُم تَعلَمُونَ9O ye who believe! When the call is made for prayer on the Day of Congregation, hasten unto the remembrance of God and leave off trading. That is better for you, if ye but knew.469
فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ الصَّلَوٰةُ فَانتَشِرُواْ فِي الأَرضِ وَابتَغُواْ مِن فَضلِ اللَّهِ وَاذكُرُواْ اللَّهَ كَثِيرا لَّعَلَّكُم تُفلِحُونَ10And when the prayer is ended, then disperse in the earth and seek of the bounty of God, and remember God much, that haply ye may prosper.
وَإِذَا رَأَواْ تِجَٰرَةً أَو لَهوًا انفَضُّواْ إِلَيهَا وَتَرَكُوكَ قَائِما قُل مَا عِندَ اللَّهِ خَير مِّنَ اللَّهوِ وَمِنَ التِّجَٰرَةِ وَاللَّهُ خَيرُ الرَّـٰزِقِينَ11And when they see merchandise or amusement, they break away unto it and leave thee standing. Say: "That which is with God is better than amusement and merchandise; and God is the best of providers."

466 Verse 2: "He it is Who hath raised among the unlettered ones () a Messenger from among themselves." '-m-m yields (unlettered, of the mother-community). The unlettered are not merely illiterate but those without a prior scripture — the mother-nation () in its original state. The Messenger is from ...

467 Verse 3 ("And others among them who have not yet joined them") is traditionally read as a prophecy of later communities who will join the Prophet's community. In the Bahá'í reading, this includes all future believers — the congregation is not closed. The donkey simile (v.5) connects to 31:19 (the...

468 Verse 5: "those who were laden with the Torah, then bore it not ( ), is as the likeness of a donkey bearing () books." -- (to carry, to bear) appears twice: they were burdened with scripture but did not truly carry it. The donkey bears books without comprehending them — a devastating image of kno...

469 Verse 9: "When the call is made for prayer on the Day of Congregation ( ), hasten unto the remembrance of God." -- means to gather, to collect, to assemble. The Jumu'ah (Friday) prayer is the weekly gathering — the community's collective act of remembrance. connects to -- (gathering, 59:2) — the ...

Surah 63: Al-Munafiqun — The Hypocrites

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا جَاءَكَ المُنَٰفِقُونَ قَالُواْ نَشهَدُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُ اللَّهِ وَاللَّهُ يَعلَمُ إِنَّكَ لَرَسُولُهُ وَاللَّهُ يَشهَدُ إِنَّ المُنَٰفِقِينَ لَكَٰذِبُونَ1When the hypocrites come unto thee, they say: "We bear witness that thou art indeed the Messenger of God." And God knoweth that thou art indeed His Messenger, and God beareth witness that the hypocrites are liars.
اتَّخَذُواْ أَيمَٰنَهُم جُنَّة فَصَدُّواْ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ إِنَّهُم سَاءَ مَا كَانُواْ يَعمَلُونَ2They have taken their oaths as a shield, and they turn away from the way of God. Verily, evil is that which they have been doing.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُم ءَامَنُواْ ثُمَّ كَفَرُواْ فَطُبِعَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم فَهُم لَا يَفقَهُونَ3That is because they believed, then concealed, so a seal was set upon their hearts, and they comprehend not.470
وَإِذَا رَأَيتَهُم تُعجِبُكَ أَجسَامُهُم وَإِن يَقُولُواْ تَسمَع لِقَولِهِم كَأَنَّهُم خُشُب مُّسَنَّدَة يَحسَبُونَ كُلَّ صَيحَةٍ عَلَيهِم هُمُ العَدُوُّ فَاحذَرهُم قَٰتَلَهُمُ اللَّهُ أَنَّىٰ يُؤفَكُونَ4And when thou seest them, their bodies please thee; and if they speak, thou listenest unto their speech. They are as propped-up timbers. They deem every shout to be against them. They are the enemy, so beware of them. God confound them! How are they turned away!471
وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُم تَعَالَواْ يَستَغفِر لَكُم رَسُولُ اللَّهِ لَوَّواْ رُءُوسَهُم وَرَأَيتَهُم يَصُدُّونَ وَهُم مُّستَكبِرُونَ5And when it is said unto them: "Come, the Messenger of God will ask forgiveness for you," they turn their heads aside, and thou seest them turning away in their arrogance.
سَوَاءٌ عَلَيهِم أَستَغفَرتَ لَهُم أَم لَم تَستَغفِر لَهُم لَن يَغفِرَ اللَّهُ لَهُم إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَهدِي القَومَ الفَٰسِقِينَ6It is alike for them whether thou askest forgiveness for them or askest not forgiveness for them; God will never forgive them. Verily, God guideth not the people of wickedness.
هُمُ الَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ لَا تُنفِقُواْ عَلَىٰ مَن عِندَ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ حَتَّىٰ يَنفَضُّواْ وَلِلَّهِ خَزَائِنُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَلَٰكِنَّ المُنَٰفِقِينَ لَا يَفقَهُونَ7They it is who say: "Spend not upon those who are with the Messenger of God, that they may disperse." And unto God belong the treasuries of the heavens and the earth, but the hypocrites comprehend not.
يَقُولُونَ لَئِن رَّجَعنَا إِلَى المَدِينَةِ لَيُخرِجَنَّ الأَعَزُّ مِنهَا الأَذَلَّ وَلِلَّهِ العِزَّةُ وَلِرَسُولِهِ وَلِلمُؤمِنِينَ وَلَٰكِنَّ المُنَٰفِقِينَ لَا يَعلَمُونَ8They say: "If we return unto Medina, the mightier shall surely expel the meaner therefrom." But unto God belongeth the might, and unto His Messenger, and unto the believers; but the hypocrites know not.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ لَا تُلهِكُم أَموَٰلُكُم وَلَا أَولَٰدُكُم عَن ذِكرِ اللَّهِ وَمَن يَفعَل ذَٰلِكَ فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الخَٰسِرُونَ9O ye who believe! Let not your wealth nor your children divert you from the remembrance of God. And whosoever doeth that, those are the losers.472
وَأَنفِقُواْ مِن مَّا رَزَقنَٰكُم مِّن قَبلِ أَن يَأتِيَ أَحَدَكُمُ المَوتُ فَيَقُولَ رَبِّ لَولَا أَخَّرتَنِي إِلَىٰ أَجَل قَرِيب فَأَصَّدَّقَ وَأَكُن مِّنَ الصَّـٰلِحِينَ10And spend of that wherewith We have provided you before death cometh upon one of you, and he saith: "My Lord! If only Thou wouldst delay me unto a near term, that I might give in charity and be among the righteous."
وَلَن يُؤَخِّرَ اللَّهُ نَفسًا إِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهَا وَاللَّهُ خَبِيرُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ11And God will never delay a soul when its term hath come; and God is Aware of what ye do.

470 Verse 3: "they believed, then covered over, so a seal was set upon their hearts ( )." -- (to stamp, to seal, to print) is closely related to -- (to seal). The seal on the heart is the consequence of covering over after believing — not an arbitrary divine act but the natural result of choosing con...

471 -- means to spend (), to dig a tunnel (), and to be a hypocrite (). The connection is revealing: the hypocrite is one who has a tunnel — an escape route, a secret passage between two faces. The has two openings: one facing the believers, one facing the enemy. Verse 4: "They are as propped-up timb...

472 Verse 9: "Let not your wealth nor your children divert you from the remembrance of God ( )." -- (to remember, to mention) names the antidote to hypocrisy: remembrance. Wealth and children are the tunnel through which the hypocrite escapes from God's presence. Remembrance closes the tunnel — it ma...

Surah 64: At-Taghabun — Mutual Cheating

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِي الأَرضِ لَهُ المُلكُ وَلَهُ الحَمدُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِيرٌ1Whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth glorifieth God; unto Him belongeth the dominion, and unto Him belongeth the praise, and He is over all things Powerful.
هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُم فَمِنكُم كَافِر وَمِنكُم مُّؤمِن وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ2He it is Who created you, and among you is one who concealeth and among you is one who believeth; and God seeth well what ye do.
خَلَقَ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضَ بِالحَقِّ وَصَوَّرَكُم فَأَحسَنَ صُوَرَكُم وَإِلَيهِ المَصِيرُ3He created the heavens and the earth in truth, and He fashioned you and made goodly your forms; and unto Him is the journeying.
يَعلَمُ مَا فِي السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَيَعلَمُ مَا تُسِرُّونَ وَمَا تُعلِنُونَ وَاللَّهُ عَلِيمُ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ4He knoweth what is in the heavens and the earth, and He knoweth what ye conceal and what ye proclaim; and God is Knowing of what is in the breasts.
أَلَم يَأتِكُم نَبَؤُاْ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن قَبلُ فَذَاقُواْ وَبَالَ أَمرِهِم وَلَهُم عَذَابٌ أَلِيم5Hath there not come unto you the tidings of those who concealed before? They tasted the evil consequence of their affair, and for them is a painful chastisement.
ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُ كَانَت تَّأتِيهِم رُسُلُهُم بِالبَيِّنَٰتِ فَقَالُواْ أَبَشَر يَهدُونَنَا فَكَفَرُواْ وَتَوَلَّواْ وَّاستَغنَى اللَّهُ وَاللَّهُ غَنِيٌّ حَمِيد6That is because their messengers used to come unto them with clear proofs, but they said: "Shall mortals guide us?" So they concealed and turned away, and God was Self-Sufficient; and God is Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.
زَعَمَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ أَن لَّن يُبعَثُواْ قُل بَلَىٰ وَرَبِّي لَتُبعَثُنَّ ثُمَّ لَتُنَبَّؤُنَّ بِمَا عَمِلتُم وَذَٰلِكَ عَلَى اللَّهِ يَسِير7Those who have concealed claim that they will never be raised. Say: "Yea, by my Lord! Ye shall indeed be raised, then ye shall indeed be informed of what ye did; and that is easy for God."
فَـَامِنُواْ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ وَالنُّورِ الَّذِي أَنزَلنَا وَاللَّهُ بِمَا تَعمَلُونَ خَبِير8So believe in God and His Messenger, and the Light which We have sent down; and God is Aware of what ye do.473
يَومَ يَجمَعُكُم لِيَومِ الجَمعِ ذَٰلِكَ يَومُ التَّغَابُنِ وَمَن يُؤمِن بِاللَّهِ وَيَعمَل صَٰلِحا يُكَفِّر عَنهُ سَيِّـَاتِهِ وَيُدخِلهُ جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدا ذَٰلِكَ الفَوزُ العَظِيمُ9The Day when He shall gather you for the Day of Gathering, that shall be the Day of Mutual Cheating. And whosoever believeth in God and worketh righteousness, He shall remit from him his evil deeds and admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein forever. That is the supreme triumph.474
وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَكَذَّبُواْ بِـَايَٰتِنَا أُوْلَـٰئِكَ أَصحَٰبُ النَّارِ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَبِئسَ المَصِيرُ10And those who have concealed and denied Our signs, those are the companions of the Fire, to abide therein; and wretched is the journeying.
مَا أَصَابَ مِن مُّصِيبَةٍ إِلَّا بِإِذنِ اللَّهِ وَمَن يُؤمِن بِاللَّهِ يَهدِ قَلبَهُ وَاللَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عَلِيم11No affliction befalleth save by the leave of God. And whosoever believeth in God, He guideth his heart; and God is of all things Knowing.475
وَأَطِيعُواْ اللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُواْ الرَّسُولَ فَإِن تَوَلَّيتُم فَإِنَّمَا عَلَىٰ رَسُولِنَا البَلَٰغُ المُبِينُ12And obey God and obey the Messenger. But if ye turn away, then upon Our Messenger is only the clear conveyance.
اللَّهُ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَعَلَى اللَّهِ فَليَتَوَكَّلِ المُؤمِنُونَ13God! There is no god but He. And upon God let the believers put their trust.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِنَّ مِن أَزوَٰجِكُم وَأَولَٰدِكُم عَدُوّا لَّكُم فَاحذَرُوهُم وَإِن تَعفُواْ وَتَصفَحُواْ وَتَغفِرُواْ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُور رَّحِيمٌ14O ye who believe! Verily, among your spouses and your children is an enemy unto you, so beware of them. And if ye pardon and overlook and forgive, then verily, God is Forgiving, Merciful.476
إِنَّمَا أَموَٰلُكُم وَأَولَٰدُكُم فِتنَة وَاللَّهُ عِندَهُ أَجرٌ عَظِيم15Your wealth and your children are but a trial; and God, with Him is a great reward.
فَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ مَا استَطَعتُم وَاسمَعُواْ وَأَطِيعُواْ وَأَنفِقُواْ خَيرا لِّأَنفُسِكُم وَمَن يُوقَ شُحَّ نَفسِهِ فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ المُفلِحُونَ16So fear God as much as ye are able, and hearken, and obey, and spend; it is better for your souls. And whosoever is shielded from the avarice of his own soul, those are the successful.
إِن تُقرِضُواْ اللَّهَ قَرضًا حَسَنا يُضَٰعِفهُ لَكُم وَيَغفِر لَكُم وَاللَّهُ شَكُورٌ حَلِيمٌ17If ye lend unto God a goodly loan, He will multiply it for you and forgive you; and God is Grateful, Forbearing,
عَٰلِمُ الغَيبِ وَالشَّهَٰدَةِ العَزِيزُ الحَكِيمُ18Knower of the unseen and the seen, the Mighty, the Wise.

473 Verse 8: "believe in God and His Messenger, and the Light () which We have sent down." The Light is sent down alongside the Messenger — it is not metaphorical but a distinct thing. -- here connects to 57:12–13 (light running before the believers) and 57:28 ("a light whereby ye walk"). Light as di...

474 Verse 9: "the Day of Gathering, that shall be the Day of Mutual Cheating ()." -- means to cheat, to shortchange, to deceive in a transaction. The Form VI () is reciprocal: mutual cheating. On that Day, everyone discovers they have been shortchanged — the coverers cheated themselves of faith, and ...

475 Verse 11 ("No affliction befalleth save by the leave of God") echoes 57:22 ("No affliction befalleth in the earth or in your souls but it is in a Book"). Affliction within divine measure spans both surahs.

476 Verse 14: "among your spouses and your children is an enemy () unto you." -- means to be hostile, to transgress, to run past. Family as potential enemy — not through malice but through distraction. The verse immediately softens: "if ye pardon and overlook and forgive." The enemy is not to be foug...

Surah 65: At-Talaq — Divorce

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ إِذَا طَلَّقتُمُ النِّسَاءَ فَطَلِّقُوهُنَّ لِعِدَّتِهِنَّ وَأَحصُواْ العِدَّةَ وَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ رَبَّكُم لَا تُخرِجُوهُنَّ مِن بُيُوتِهِنَّ وَلَا يَخرُجنَ إِلَّا أَن يَأتِينَ بِفَٰحِشَة مُّبَيِّنَة وَتِلكَ حُدُودُ اللَّهِ وَمَن يَتَعَدَّ حُدُودَ اللَّهِ فَقَد ظَلَمَ نَفسَهُ لَا تَدرِي لَعَلَّ اللَّهَ يُحدِثُ بَعدَ ذَٰلِكَ أَمرا1O Prophet! When ye divorce women, divorce them for their waiting period, and reckon the waiting period; and fear God, your Lord. Expel them not from their houses, nor let them go forth, unless they commit a manifest indecency. And those are the limits of God. And whosoever transgresseth the limits of God hath verily wronged his own soul. Thou knowest not; it may be that God will bring to pass after that some new thing.477
فَإِذَا بَلَغنَ أَجَلَهُنَّ فَأَمسِكُوهُنَّ بِمَعرُوفٍ أَو فَارِقُوهُنَّ بِمَعرُوف وَأَشهِدُواْ ذَوَي عَدل مِّنكُم وَأَقِيمُواْ الشَّهَٰدَةَ لِلَّهِ ذَٰلِكُم يُوعَظُ بِهِ مَن كَانَ يُؤمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَاليَومِ الأخِرِ وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجعَل لَّهُ مَخرَجا2Then when they have reached their term, retain them honourably or part from them honourably, and call to witness two just men among you, and establish the testimony for God. Thus is exhorted he who believeth in God and the Last Day. And whosoever feareth God, He will make for him a way out,
وَيَرزُقهُ مِن حَيثُ لَا يَحتَسِبُ وَمَن يَتَوَكَّل عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسبُهُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ بَٰلِغُ أَمرِهِ قَد جَعَلَ اللَّهُ لِكُلِّ شَيء قَدرا3and will provide for him from whence he reckoneth not. And whosoever putteth his trust in God, He is sufficient for him. Verily, God shall attain His purpose. God hath set for everything a measure.
وَالَّـٰـِي يَئِسنَ مِنَ المَحِيضِ مِن نِّسَائِكُم إِنِ ارتَبتُم فَعِدَّتُهُنَّ ثَلَٰثَةُ أَشهُر وَالَّـٰـِي لَم يَحِضنَ وَأُوْلَٰتُ الأَحمَالِ أَجَلُهُنَّ أَن يَضَعنَ حَملَهُنَّ وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يَجعَل لَّهُ مِن أَمرِهِ يُسرا4And those of your women who have despaired of menstruation, if ye be in doubt, their waiting period shall be three months, and those who have not menstruated. And for those who are with child, their term shall be until they bring forth their burden. And whosoever feareth God, He will make his affair easy for him.
ذَٰلِكَ أَمرُ اللَّهِ أَنزَلَهُ إِلَيكُم وَمَن يَتَّقِ اللَّهَ يُكَفِّر عَنهُ سَيِّـَاتِهِ وَيُعظِم لَهُ أَجرًا5That is the command of God which He hath sent down unto you. And whosoever feareth God, He will remit from him his evil deeds and magnify for him his reward.
أَسكِنُوهُنَّ مِن حَيثُ سَكَنتُم مِّن وُجدِكُم وَلَا تُضَارُّوهُنَّ لِتُضَيِّقُواْ عَلَيهِنَّ وَإِن كُنَّ أُوْلَٰتِ حَمل فَأَنفِقُواْ عَلَيهِنَّ حَتَّىٰ يَضَعنَ حَملَهُنَّ فَإِن أَرضَعنَ لَكُم فَـَاتُوهُنَّ أُجُورَهُنَّ وَأتَمِرُواْ بَينَكُم بِمَعرُوف وَإِن تَعَاسَرتُم فَسَتُرضِعُ لَهُ أُخرَىٰ6Lodge them where ye dwell, according to your means, and harass them not so as to straiten them. And if they be with child, spend upon them until they bring forth their burden. Then if they suckle for you, give them their wages, and consult together honourably. And if ye find it difficult, then another woman shall suckle for him.
لِيُنفِق ذُو سَعَة مِّن سَعَتِهِ وَمَن قُدِرَ عَلَيهِ رِزقُهُ فَليُنفِق مِمَّا ءَاتَىٰهُ اللَّهُ لَا يُكَلِّفُ اللَّهُ نَفسًا إِلَّا مَا ءَاتَىٰهَا سَيَجعَلُ اللَّهُ بَعدَ عُسر يُسرا7Let him of abundance spend of his abundance, and he whose provision is straitened, let him spend of that which God hath given him. God burdeneth not a soul beyond that which He hath given it. God shall bring about ease after hardship.
وَكَأَيِّن مِّن قَريَةٍ عَتَت عَن أَمرِ رَبِّهَا وَرُسُلِهِ فَحَاسَبنَٰهَا حِسَابا شَدِيدا وَعَذَّبنَٰهَا عَذَابا نُّكرا8And how many a township rebelled against the command of its Lord and His messengers, so We reckoned with it a severe reckoning and chastised it with a dire chastisement.
فَذَاقَت وَبَالَ أَمرِهَا وَكَانَ عَٰقِبَةُ أَمرِهَا خُسرًا9So it tasted the evil consequence of its affair, and the end of its affair was loss.
أَعَدَّ اللَّهُ لَهُم عَذَابا شَدِيدا فَاتَّقُواْ اللَّهَ يَـٰأُوْلِي الأَلبَٰبِ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ قَد أَنزَلَ اللَّهُ إِلَيكُم ذِكرا10God hath prepared for them a severe chastisement; so fear God, O ye of understanding who believe! God hath indeed sent down unto you a Reminder,
رَّسُولا يَتلُواْ عَلَيكُم ءَايَٰتِ اللَّهِ مُبَيِّنَٰت لِّيُخرِجَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ مِنَ الظُّلُمَٰتِ إِلَى النُّورِ وَمَن يُؤمِن بِاللَّهِ وَيَعمَل صَٰلِحا يُدخِلهُ جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدا قَد أَحسَنَ اللَّهُ لَهُ رِزقًا11a Messenger reciting unto you the signs of God made clear, that He may bring forth those who believe and work righteous deeds from the darkness unto the light. And whosoever believeth in God and worketh righteousness, He will admit him into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, to abide therein forever. God hath indeed made goodly provision for him.
اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبعَ سَمَٰوَٰت وَمِنَ الأَرضِ مِثلَهُنَّ يَتَنَزَّلُ الأَمرُ بَينَهُنَّ لِتَعلَمُواْ أَنَّ اللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِير وَأَنَّ اللَّهَ قَد أَحَاطَ بِكُلِّ شَيءٍ عِلمَا12God it is Who created seven heavens, and of the earth the like thereof. The command descendeth between them, that ye may know that God is over all things Powerful, and that God encompasseth all things in knowledge.478

477 The phrase "whosoever feareth God" ( ) appears five times in this short surah (vv.2, 3, 4, 5, and implied in v.1). -- means to guard, to shield, to be aware. The surah frames divorce law entirely within taqwa: every legal instruction is followed by a promise for those who are God-aware. Taqwa is ...

478 Verse 12: "God it is Who created seven heavens, and of the earth the like thereof." Seven heavens and seven earths — the cosmic structure mirrors itself above and below. "The command descendeth between them" — revelation moves between the layers. This is the surah's unexpected cosmic coda: a dome...

Surah 66: At-Tahrim — The Forbidding

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ لِمَ تُحَرِّمُ مَا أَحَلَّ اللَّهُ لَكَ تَبتَغِي مَرضَاتَ أَزوَٰجِكَ وَاللَّهُ غَفُور رَّحِيم1O Prophet! Why forbiddest thou that which God hath made lawful unto thee, seeking the pleasure of thy wives? And God is Forgiving, Merciful.479
قَد فَرَضَ اللَّهُ لَكُم تَحِلَّةَ أَيمَٰنِكُم وَاللَّهُ مَولَىٰكُم وَهُوَ العَلِيمُ الحَكِيمُ2God hath indeed ordained for you the absolution of your oaths; and God is your Master, and He is the Knowing, the Wise.
وَإِذ أَسَرَّ النَّبِيُّ إِلَىٰ بَعضِ أَزوَٰجِهِ حَدِيثا فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَت بِهِ وَأَظهَرَهُ اللَّهُ عَلَيهِ عَرَّفَ بَعضَهُ وَأَعرَضَ عَن بَعض فَلَمَّا نَبَّأَهَا بِهِ قَالَت مَن أَنبَأَكَ هَٰذَا قَالَ نَبَّأَنِيَ العَلِيمُ الخَبِيرُ3And when the Prophet confided unto one of his wives a certain matter, then when she told of it, and God made it known unto him, he made known part thereof and passed over part. Then when he informed her of it, she said: "Who told thee this?" He said: "The Knowing, the Aware told me."
إِن تَتُوبَا إِلَى اللَّهِ فَقَد صَغَت قُلُوبُكُمَا وَإِن تَظَٰهَرَا عَلَيهِ فَإِنَّ اللَّهَ هُوَ مَولَىٰهُ وَجِبرِيلُ وَصَٰلِحُ المُؤمِنِينَ وَالمَلَـٰئِكَةُ بَعدَ ذَٰلِكَ ظَهِيرٌ4If ye two turn in repentance unto God, then indeed your hearts have inclined; but if ye band together against him, then verily, God is his Protector, and Gabriel, and the righteous among the believers; and the angels after that are his helpers.
عَسَىٰ رَبُّهُ إِن طَلَّقَكُنَّ أَن يُبدِلَهُ أَزوَٰجًا خَيرا مِّنكُنَّ مُسلِمَٰت مُّؤمِنَٰت قَٰنِتَٰت تَـٰئِبَٰتٍ عَٰبِدَٰت سَـٰئِحَٰت ثَيِّبَٰت وَأَبكَارا5It may be that his Lord, if he divorce you, will give him in exchange wives better than you, devoted, believing, devout, repentant, worshipping, given to fasting, both previously married and virgins.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ قُواْ أَنفُسَكُم وَأَهلِيكُم نَارا وَقُودُهَا النَّاسُ وَالحِجَارَةُ عَلَيهَا مَلَـٰئِكَةٌ غِلَاظ شِدَاد لَّا يَعصُونَ اللَّهَ مَا أَمَرَهُم وَيَفعَلُونَ مَا يُؤمَرُونَ6O ye who believe! Guard yourselves and your families against a Fire whose fuel is people and stones, over which are angels fierce and stern, who disobey not God in what He commandeth them and do what they are commanded.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَا تَعتَذِرُواْ اليَومَ إِنَّمَا تُجزَونَ مَا كُنتُم تَعمَلُونَ7O ye who have concealed! Make no excuses this day. Ye are only recompensed for what ye used to do.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ تُوبُواْ إِلَى اللَّهِ تَوبَة نَّصُوحًا عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُم أَن يُكَفِّرَ عَنكُم سَيِّـَاتِكُم وَيُدخِلَكُم جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ يَومَ لَا يُخزِي اللَّهُ النَّبِيَّ وَالَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ مَعَهُ نُورُهُم يَسعَىٰ بَينَ أَيدِيهِم وَبِأَيمَٰنِهِم يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا أَتمِم لَنَا نُورَنَا وَاغفِر لَنَا إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِير8O ye who believe! Turn unto God in sincere repentance. It may be that your Lord will remit from you your evil deeds and admit you into Gardens beneath which rivers flow, on the Day when God will not abase the Prophet and those who believe with him. Their light runneth before them and on their right hands; they say: "Our Lord! Perfect for us our light and forgive us; verily, Thou art over all things Powerful."480
يَـٰأَيُّهَا النَّبِيُّ جَٰهِدِ الكُفَّارَ وَالمُنَٰفِقِينَ وَاغلُظ عَلَيهِم وَمَأوَىٰهُم جَهَنَّمُ وَبِئسَ المَصِيرُ9O Prophet! Strive against those who conceal and the hypocrites, and be stern with them; and their abode is Hell, and wretched is the journeying.
ضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلا لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ امرَأَتَ نُوح وَامرَأَتَ لُوط كَانَتَا تَحتَ عَبدَينِ مِن عِبَادِنَا صَٰلِحَينِ فَخَانَتَاهُمَا فَلَم يُغنِيَا عَنهُمَا مِنَ اللَّهِ شَيـا وَقِيلَ ادخُلَا النَّارَ مَعَ الدَّـٰخِلِينَ10God hath struck a likeness for those who have concealed: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two servants of Our servants, righteous, but they betrayed them, so they availed them naught against God, and it was said: "Enter ye two the Fire with those who enter."
وَضَرَبَ اللَّهُ مَثَلا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ امرَأَتَ فِرعَونَ إِذ قَالَت رَبِّ ابنِ لِي عِندَكَ بَيتا فِي الجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِي مِن فِرعَونَ وَعَمَلِهِ وَنَجِّنِي مِنَ القَومِ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ11And God hath struck a likeness for those who believe: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said: "My Lord! Build for me a house with Thee in the Garden, and deliver me from Pharaoh and his work, and deliver me from the people of wrongdoing."
وَمَريَمَ ابنَتَ عِمرَٰنَ الَّتِي أَحصَنَت فَرجَهَا فَنَفَخنَا فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِنَا وَصَدَّقَت بِكَلِمَٰتِ رَبِّهَا وَكُتُبِهِ وَكَانَت مِنَ القَٰنِتِينَ12And Mary, daughter of Imran, who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into it of Our Spirit; and she affirmed the words of her Lord and His Books, and she was of the devout.

479 Verse 1: "Why forbiddest thou () that which God hath made lawful unto thee?" -- means both to forbid and to make sacred (the Haram in Mecca is the sacred precinct). The Prophet forbids something lawful to please his wives — and God corrects him. 's duality is significant: the sacred and the forbi...

480 Verse 8: "Turn unto God in sincere repentance ( )." -- means to return, to turn back. (naṣūḥ) from -- means sincere, purified. Sincere repentance is a purified return — not merely stopping sin but returning to the origin. -- connects to -- (to return) — repentance and devotion share the concept o...

Surah 67: Al-Mulk — The Dominion

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

تَبَٰرَكَ الَّذِي بِيَدِهِ المُلكُ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء قَدِيرٌ1Blessed is He in Whose hand is the dominion, and He is over all things Powerful,481
الَّذِي خَلَقَ المَوتَ وَالحَيَوٰةَ لِيَبلُوَكُم أَيُّكُم أَحسَنُ عَمَلا وَهُوَ العَزِيزُ الغَفُورُ2Who created death and life, that He might try you, which of you is best in deed; and He is the Mighty, the Forgiving,
الَّذِي خَلَقَ سَبعَ سَمَٰوَٰت طِبَاقا مَّا تَرَىٰ فِي خَلقِ الرَّحمَٰنِ مِن تَفَٰوُت فَارجِعِ البَصَرَ هَل تَرَىٰ مِن فُطُور3Who created seven heavens in layers. Thou seest not in the creation of the All-Merciful any disparity. So return thy gaze: seest thou any flaw?482
ثُمَّ ارجِعِ البَصَرَ كَرَّتَينِ يَنقَلِب إِلَيكَ البَصَرُ خَاسِئا وَهُوَ حَسِير4Then return thy gaze twice again; thy gaze shall return unto thee humbled, and it is wearied.
وَلَقَد زَيَّنَّا السَّمَاءَ الدُّنيَا بِمَصَٰبِيحَ وَجَعَلنَٰهَا رُجُوما لِّلشَّيَٰطِينِ وَأَعتَدنَا لَهُم عَذَابَ السَّعِيرِ5And verily, We adorned the nearest heaven with lamps, and We made them missiles against the devils, and We have prepared for them the chastisement of the Blaze.
وَلِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ بِرَبِّهِم عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَبِئسَ المَصِيرُ6And for those who have concealed their Lord is the chastisement of Hell; and wretched is the journeying.
إِذَا أُلقُواْ فِيهَا سَمِعُواْ لَهَا شَهِيقا وَهِيَ تَفُورُ7When they are cast therein, they hear from it a gasping while it boileth.
تَكَادُ تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ الغَيظِ كُلَّمَا أُلقِيَ فِيهَا فَوج سَأَلَهُم خَزَنَتُهَا أَلَم يَأتِكُم نَذِير8It wellnigh bursteth with rage. Whensoever a host is cast therein, its keepers ask them: "Did there not come unto you a warner?"
قَالُواْ بَلَىٰ قَد جَاءَنَا نَذِير فَكَذَّبنَا وَقُلنَا مَا نَزَّلَ اللَّهُ مِن شَيءٍ إِن أَنتُم إِلَّا فِي ضَلَٰل كَبِير9They say: "Yea, indeed a warner came unto us, but we denied and said: God hath not sent down anything; ye are in naught but great error."
وَقَالُواْ لَو كُنَّا نَسمَعُ أَو نَعقِلُ مَا كُنَّا فِي أَصحَٰبِ السَّعِيرِ10And they say: "Had we but hearkened or had understanding, we would not be among the companions of the Blaze."
فَاعتَرَفُواْ بِذَنبِهِم فَسُحقا لِّأَصحَٰبِ السَّعِيرِ11So they confess their sin; so perdition for the companions of the Blaze!
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَخشَونَ رَبَّهُم بِالغَيبِ لَهُم مَّغفِرَة وَأَجر كَبِير12Verily, those who fear their Lord in the unseen, for them is forgiveness and a great reward.
وَأَسِرُّواْ قَولَكُم أَوِ اجهَرُواْ بِهِ إِنَّهُ عَلِيمُ بِذَاتِ الصُّدُورِ13And conceal your speech or proclaim it; verily, He is Knowing of what is in the breasts.
أَلَا يَعلَمُ مَن خَلَقَ وَهُوَ اللَّطِيفُ الخَبِيرُ14Shall He not know, He Who created? And He is the Subtle, the Aware.
هُوَ الَّذِي جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الأَرضَ ذَلُولا فَامشُواْ فِي مَنَاكِبِهَا وَكُلُواْ مِن رِّزقِهِ وَإِلَيهِ النُّشُورُ15He it is Who hath made the earth submissive unto you, so walk upon its shoulders and eat of His provision; and unto Him is the resurrection.483
ءَأَمِنتُم مَّن فِي السَّمَاءِ أَن يَخسِفَ بِكُمُ الأَرضَ فَإِذَا هِيَ تَمُورُ16Are ye secure that He Who is in the heaven will not cause the earth to swallow you, when lo! it quaketh?
أَم أَمِنتُم مَّن فِي السَّمَاءِ أَن يُرسِلَ عَلَيكُم حَاصِبا فَسَتَعلَمُونَ كَيفَ نَذِيرِ17Or are ye secure that He Who is in the heaven will not send against you a storm of stones? Then ye shall know how My warning was.
وَلَقَد كَذَّبَ الَّذِينَ مِن قَبلِهِم فَكَيفَ كَانَ نَكِيرِ18And verily, those before them denied; then how was My denial!
أَوَلَم يَرَواْ إِلَى الطَّيرِ فَوقَهُم صَـٰفَّـٰت وَيَقبِضنَ مَا يُمسِكُهُنَّ إِلَّا الرَّحمَٰنُ إِنَّهُ بِكُلِّ شَيءِ بَصِيرٌ19Have they not seen the birds above them, spreading their wings and folding them? None holdeth them save the All-Merciful. Verily, He is of all things Seeing.
أَمَّن هَٰذَا الَّذِي هُوَ جُند لَّكُم يَنصُرُكُم مِّن دُونِ الرَّحمَٰنِ إِنِ الكَٰفِرُونَ إِلَّا فِي غُرُورٍ20Or who is he that is an army for you, to succour you, apart from the All-Merciful? Those who conceal are in naught but delusion.
أَمَّن هَٰذَا الَّذِي يَرزُقُكُم إِن أَمسَكَ رِزقَهُ بَل لَّجُّواْ فِي عُتُوّ وَنُفُورٍ21Or who is he that shall provide for you, if He withholdeth His provision? Nay, but they persist in insolence and aversion.
أَفَمَن يَمشِي مُكِبًّا عَلَىٰ وَجهِهِ أَهدَىٰ أَمَّن يَمشِي سَوِيًّا عَلَىٰ صِرَٰط مُّستَقِيم22Is then he who walketh fallen on his face better guided, or he who walketh upright upon a straight path?
قُل هُوَ الَّذِي أَنشَأَكُم وَجَعَلَ لَكُمُ السَّمعَ وَالأَبصَٰرَ وَالأَفـِدَةَ قَلِيلا مَّا تَشكُرُونَ23Say: "He it is Who brought you into being, and made for you hearing and sight and hearts. Little is it that ye give thanks."
قُل هُوَ الَّذِي ذَرَأَكُم فِي الأَرضِ وَإِلَيهِ تُحشَرُونَ24Say: "He it is Who hath scattered you in the earth, and unto Him ye shall be gathered."
وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَٰذَا الوَعدُ إِن كُنتُم صَٰدِقِينَ25And they say: "When is this promise, if ye be truthful?"
قُل إِنَّمَا العِلمُ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَإِنَّمَا أَنَا نَذِير مُّبِين26Say: "The knowledge is only with God, and I am but a clear warner."
فَلَمَّا رَأَوهُ زُلفَة سِيـَت وُجُوهُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ وَقِيلَ هَٰذَا الَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِ تَدَّعُونَ27Then when they see it nigh, the faces of those who have concealed shall be stricken, and it shall be said: "This is that which ye used to call for."
قُل أَرَءَيتُم إِن أَهلَكَنِيَ اللَّهُ وَمَن مَّعِيَ أَو رَحِمَنَا فَمَن يُجِيرُ الكَٰفِرِينَ مِن عَذَابٍ أَلِيم28Say: "Have ye considered? If God destroyeth me and those who are with me, or hath mercy upon us, then who shall shelter those who conceal from a painful chastisement?"
قُل هُوَ الرَّحمَٰنُ ءَامَنَّا بِهِ وَعَلَيهِ تَوَكَّلنَا فَسَتَعلَمُونَ مَن هُوَ فِي ضَلَٰل مُّبِين29Say: "He is the All-Merciful; we believe in Him and upon Him we put our trust. Then ye shall know who is in manifest error."
قُل أَرَءَيتُم إِن أَصبَحَ مَاؤُكُم غَورا فَمَن يَأتِيكُم بِمَاء مَّعِينِ30Say: "Have ye considered? If your water were to sink away, then who would bring you running water?"

481 Verse 1: "Blessed is He in Whose hand is the dominion ()." -- means to possess, to rule, to own. The dominion is "in His hand" — an anthropomorphic image that conveys directness of control. The same root gives (king), (angel), and (property). God's sovereignty encompasses all three: He is King ov...

482 Verse 3: "Thou seest not in the creation () of the All-Merciful any disparity ()." --- means unevenness, incongruity, disparity. The challenge "seest thou any flaw?" ( ) uses the root -- — cracks, flaws, ruptures. The seven layered heavens are seamless. The double challenge ("return thy gaze... r...

483 Verse 15: "He it is Who hath made the earth submissive () unto you, so walk upon its shoulders." -- means to humble, to tame, to make gentle. The earth is not merely available — it is tamed, made gentle for human habitation. "Walk upon its shoulders ()" — the earth has shoulders, like a beast of ...

Surah 68: Al-Qalam — The Pen

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

ن وَالقَلَمِ وَمَا يَسطُرُونَ1Nun. By the Pen and what they inscribe!484
مَا أَنتَ بِنِعمَةِ رَبِّكَ بِمَجنُون2Thou art not, by the grace of thy Lord, one possessed.
وَإِنَّ لَكَ لَأَجرًا غَيرَ مَمنُون3And verily, for thee is a reward unfailing.
وَإِنَّكَ لَعَلَىٰ خُلُقٍ عَظِيم4And verily, thou art upon a mighty character.485
فَسَتُبصِرُ وَيُبصِرُونَ5So thou shalt see and they shall see,
بِأَييِّكُمُ المَفتُونُ6which of you is the one afflicted.
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِ وَهُوَ أَعلَمُ بِالمُهتَدِينَ7Verily, thy Lord, He is most knowing of who hath strayed from His way, and He is most knowing of the rightly guided.
فَلَا تُطِعِ المُكَذِّبِينَ8So obey not those who deny.
وَدُّواْ لَو تُدهِنُ فَيُدهِنُونَ9They wish that thou shouldst compromise, so they would compromise.
وَلَا تُطِع كُلَّ حَلَّاف مَّهِينٍ10And obey not every contemptible swearer,
هَمَّاز مَّشَّاءِ بِنَمِيم11slanderer, going about with calumny,
مَّنَّاع لِّلخَيرِ مُعتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ12hinderer of good, transgressor, sinner,
عُتُلِّ بَعدَ ذَٰلِكَ زَنِيمٍ13crude, and besides all that, baseborn,
أَن كَانَ ذَا مَال وَبَنِينَ14because he possesseth wealth and sons.
إِذَا تُتلَىٰ عَلَيهِ ءَايَٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَٰطِيرُ الأَوَّلِينَ15When Our signs are recited unto him, he saith: "Tales of the ancients!"
سَنَسِمُهُ عَلَى الخُرطُومِ16We shall brand him upon the snout.
إِنَّا بَلَونَٰهُم كَمَا بَلَونَا أَصحَٰبَ الجَنَّةِ إِذ أَقسَمُواْ لَيَصرِمُنَّهَا مُصبِحِينَ17Verily, We have tried them even as We tried the companions of the garden, when they swore they would surely reap it in the morning,
وَلَا يَستَثنُونَ18and made no exception.
فَطَافَ عَلَيهَا طَائِف مِّن رَّبِّكَ وَهُم نَائِمُونَ19Then a visitation from thy Lord came upon it while they slept,
فَأَصبَحَت كَالصَّرِيمِ20and in the morning it was as if reaped.486
فَتَنَادَواْ مُصبِحِينَ21And they called out one to another in the morning:
أَنِ اغدُواْ عَلَىٰ حَرثِكُم إِن كُنتُم صَٰرِمِينَ22"Go early to your tilth, if ye would be reapers."
فَانطَلَقُواْ وَهُم يَتَخَٰفَتُونَ23So they went off, whispering together:
أَن لَّا يَدخُلَنَّهَا اليَومَ عَلَيكُم مِّسكِين24"Let no poor man enter it today against you."
وَغَدَواْ عَلَىٰ حَرد قَٰدِرِينَ25And they went early with determined purpose, able.
فَلَمَّا رَأَوهَا قَالُواْ إِنَّا لَضَالُّونَ26But when they saw it, they said: "Verily, we have gone astray!
بَل نَحنُ مَحرُومُونَ27Nay, we are deprived!"
قَالَ أَوسَطُهُم أَلَم أَقُل لَّكُم لَولَا تُسَبِّحُونَ28The most moderate of them said: "Did I not say unto you: Why do ye not glorify?"
قَالُواْ سُبحَٰنَ رَبِّنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَٰلِمِينَ29They said: "Glory be to our Lord! Verily, we were wrongdoers."
فَأَقبَلَ بَعضُهُم عَلَىٰ بَعض يَتَلَٰوَمُونَ30Then some of them turned upon others, blaming one another.
قَالُواْ يَٰوَيلَنَا إِنَّا كُنَّا طَٰغِينَ31They said: "Woe unto us! Verily, we were transgressors.
عَسَىٰ رَبُّنَا أَن يُبدِلَنَا خَيرا مِّنهَا إِنَّا إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا رَٰغِبُونَ32It may be that our Lord will give us in exchange better than it; verily, unto our Lord we turn in hope."
كَذَٰلِكَ العَذَابُ وَلَعَذَابُ الأخِرَةِ أَكبَرُ لَو كَانُواْ يَعلَمُونَ33Such is the chastisement; and the chastisement of the Hereafter is greater, if they but knew.
إِنَّ لِلمُتَّقِينَ عِندَ رَبِّهِم جَنَّـٰتِ النَّعِيمِ34Verily, for the pious are Gardens of Delight with their Lord.
أَفَنَجعَلُ المُسلِمِينَ كَالمُجرِمِينَ35Shall We then treat the Devoted as the guilty?
مَا لَكُم كَيفَ تَحكُمُونَ36What aileth you? How do ye judge?
أَم لَكُم كِتَٰب فِيهِ تَدرُسُونَ37Or have ye a book wherein ye study,
إِنَّ لَكُم فِيهِ لَمَا تَخَيَّرُونَ38that verily therein is for you whatsoever ye choose?
أَم لَكُم أَيمَٰنٌ عَلَينَا بَٰلِغَةٌ إِلَىٰ يَومِ القِيَٰمَةِ إِنَّ لَكُم لَمَا تَحكُمُونَ39Or have ye oaths binding upon Us, reaching to the Day of Resurrection, that verily for you is whatsoever ye judge?
سَلهُم أَيُّهُم بِذَٰلِكَ زَعِيمٌ40Ask them, which of them will vouch for that.
أَم لَهُم شُرَكَاءُ فَليَأتُواْ بِشُرَكَائِهِم إِن كَانُواْ صَٰدِقِينَ41Or have they associates? Then let them bring their associates, if they be truthful.
يَومَ يُكشَفُ عَن سَاق وَيُدعَونَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ فَلَا يَستَطِيعُونَ42On the Day when the leg shall be bared, and they are called to prostration, but they shall not be able,
خَٰشِعَةً أَبصَٰرُهُم تَرهَقُهُم ذِلَّة وَقَد كَانُواْ يُدعَونَ إِلَى السُّجُودِ وَهُم سَٰلِمُونَ43their eyes downcast, abasement covering them; and they had been called to prostration while they were whole.
فَذَرنِي وَمَن يُكَذِّبُ بِهَٰذَا الحَدِيثِ سَنَستَدرِجُهُم مِّن حَيثُ لَا يَعلَمُونَ44So leave Me with him who denieth this discourse. We shall lead them on by degrees from whence they know not.
وَأُملِي لَهُم إِنَّ كَيدِي مَتِينٌ45And I grant them respite; verily, My stratagem is firm.
أَم تَسـَلُهُم أَجرا فَهُم مِّن مَّغرَم مُّثقَلُونَ46Or dost thou ask of them a wage, so they are burdened with debt?
أَم عِندَهُمُ الغَيبُ فَهُم يَكتُبُونَ47Or is the unseen with them, so they write?
فَاصبِر لِحُكمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تَكُن كَصَاحِبِ الحُوتِ إِذ نَادَىٰ وَهُوَ مَكظُوم48So be patient for the judgement of thy Lord, and be not as the companion of the whale, when he cried out while he was stifled.
لَّولَا أَن تَدَٰرَكَهُ نِعمَة مِّن رَّبِّهِ لَنُبِذَ بِالعَرَاءِ وَهُوَ مَذمُوم49Had not a grace from his Lord reached him, he would have been cast upon the barren shore, blameworthy.
فَاجتَبَٰهُ رَبُّهُ فَجَعَلَهُ مِنَ الصَّـٰلِحِينَ50But his Lord chose him and made him of the righteous.
وَإِن يَكَادُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لَيُزلِقُونَكَ بِأَبصَٰرِهِم لَمَّا سَمِعُواْ الذِّكرَ وَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّهُ لَمَجنُون51And verily, those who have concealed would wellnigh cause thee to slip with their glances when they hear the Reminder, and they say: "Verily, he is one possessed."
وَمَا هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكر لِّلعَٰلَمِينَ52And it is naught but a Reminder unto the worlds.

484 Verse 1: "Nun. By the Pen () and what they inscribe!" -- means to cut, to trim, to write. The pen is what has been cut to a point — shaped for its purpose. God swears by the instrument of writing, elevating inscription to cosmic significance. "What they inscribe" (, root --, to write in lines) — ...

485 Verse 4: "thou art upon a mighty character ( )." -- means both to create and to have a character/nature. The Prophet's character IS his creation — his nature is what God made him. The word (khuluq, character) shares its root with (khalq, creation). The finest character is the most authentic creat...

486 Verses 17–33 tell the parable of the garden-owners who planned to harvest without sharing with the poor. -- (to till, to cultivate) frames the story. Their garden is destroyed overnight — "in the morning it was as if reaped" (v.20). The garden that excludes the poor self-destructs. This is a j-n-...

Surah 69: Al-Haqqah — The Reality

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

الحَاقَّةُ1The Reality!
مَا الحَاقَّةُ2What is the Reality?
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا الحَاقَّةُ3And what shall make thee know what the Reality is?
كَذَّبَت ثَمُودُ وَعَادُ بِالقَارِعَةِ4Thamud and Ad denied the Striking Hour.
فَأَمَّا ثَمُودُ فَأُهلِكُواْ بِالطَّاغِيَةِ5As for Thamud, they were destroyed by the overwhelming.
وَأَمَّا عَاد فَأُهلِكُواْ بِرِيح صَرصَرٍ عَاتِيَة6And as for Ad, they were destroyed by a furious, raging wind
سَخَّرَهَا عَلَيهِم سَبعَ لَيَال وَثَمَٰنِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُوما فَتَرَى القَومَ فِيهَا صَرعَىٰ كَأَنَّهُم أَعجَازُ نَخلٍ خَاوِيَة7which He subjected upon them seven nights and eight days in succession; and thou wouldst see the people therein fallen, as though they were hollow trunks of palm trees.
فَهَل تَرَىٰ لَهُم مِّن بَاقِيَة8Then dost thou see any remnant of them?
وَجَاءَ فِرعَونُ وَمَن قَبلَهُ وَالمُؤتَفِكَٰتُ بِالخَاطِئَةِ9And there came Pharaoh, and those before him, and the overturned cities, with sin.
فَعَصَواْ رَسُولَ رَبِّهِم فَأَخَذَهُم أَخذَة رَّابِيَةً10And they disobeyed the messenger of their Lord, so He seized them with a surpassing seizure.
إِنَّا لَمَّا طَغَا المَاءُ حَمَلنَٰكُم فِي الجَارِيَةِ11Verily, when the waters rose beyond measure, We bore you in the running vessel,
لِنَجعَلَهَا لَكُم تَذكِرَة وَتَعِيَهَا أُذُن وَٰعِيَة12that We might make it a reminder for you, and that a retaining ear might retain it.
فَإِذَا نُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ نَفخَة وَٰحِدَة13Then when the Trumpet is blown with a single blast,
وَحُمِلَتِ الأَرضُ وَالجِبَالُ فَدُكَّتَا دَكَّة وَٰحِدَة14and the earth and the mountains are lifted up and crushed with a single crushing,
فَيَومَئِذ وَقَعَتِ الوَاقِعَةُ15then on that Day shall the Event befall,
وَانشَقَّتِ السَّمَاءُ فَهِيَ يَومَئِذ وَاهِيَة16and the heaven shall be rent asunder, for on that Day it shall be frail,
وَالمَلَكُ عَلَىٰ أَرجَائِهَا وَيَحمِلُ عَرشَ رَبِّكَ فَوقَهُم يَومَئِذ ثَمَٰنِيَة17and the angels shall be on its edges; and above them, eight shall bear the Throne of thy Lord on that Day.
يَومَئِذ تُعرَضُونَ لَا تَخفَىٰ مِنكُم خَافِيَة18On that Day ye shall be exposed; no secret of yours shall be hidden.
فَأَمَّا مَن أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ فَيَقُولُ هَاؤُمُ اقرَءُواْ كِتَٰبِيَه19Then as for him who is given his book in his right hand, he shall say: "Here, read my book!
إِنِّي ظَنَنتُ أَنِّي مُلَٰقٍ حِسَابِيَه20Verily, I knew that I should meet my reckoning."
فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَة رَّاضِيَة21So he shall be in a pleasing life,
فِي جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَة22in a lofty Garden,
قُطُوفُهَا دَانِيَة23its clusters nigh to gather.
كُلُواْ وَاشرَبُواْ هَنِيـَا بِمَا أَسلَفتُم فِي الأَيَّامِ الخَالِيَةِ24"Eat and drink in wholesome ease for that which ye sent ahead in the days that are past."
وَأَمَّا مَن أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُ بِشِمَالِهِ فَيَقُولُ يَٰلَيتَنِي لَم أُوتَ كِتَٰبِيَه25But as for him who is given his book in his left hand, he shall say: "Would that I had not been given my book,
وَلَم أَدرِ مَا حِسَابِيَه26and that I had not known my reckoning!
يَٰلَيتَهَا كَانَتِ القَاضِيَةَ27Would that it had been the end!
مَا أَغنَىٰ عَنِّي مَالِيَه28My wealth hath availed me naught.
هَلَكَ عَنِّي سُلطَٰنِيَه29My authority hath perished from me."
خُذُوهُ فَغُلُّوهُ30"Seize him and shackle him!
ثُمَّ الجَحِيمَ صَلُّوهُ31Then into the Blaze cast him!
ثُمَّ فِي سِلسِلَة ذَرعُهَا سَبعُونَ ذِرَاعا فَاسلُكُوهُ32Then in a chain whose length is seventy cubits, insert him!"
إِنَّهُ كَانَ لَا يُؤمِنُ بِاللَّهِ العَظِيمِ33Verily, he used not to believe in God, the Tremendous,
وَلَا يَحُضُّ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ المِسكِينِ34and he urged not the feeding of the indigent.487
فَلَيسَ لَهُ اليَومَ هَٰهُنَا حَمِيم35So this day he hath here no intimate friend,
وَلَا طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِن غِسلِين36nor any food save foul pus,
لَّا يَأكُلُهُ إِلَّا الخَٰطِـُونَ37which none eat save the sinners.
فَلَا أُقسِمُ بِمَا تُبصِرُونَ38So nay! I swear by what ye see
وَمَا لَا تُبصِرُونَ39and what ye see not,
إِنَّهُ لَقَولُ رَسُول كَرِيم40verily, it is the speech of a noble Messenger.
وَمَا هُوَ بِقَولِ شَاعِر قَلِيلا مَّا تُؤمِنُونَ41And it is not the speech of a poet; little do ye believe.
وَلَا بِقَولِ كَاهِن قَلِيلا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ42Nor the speech of a soothsayer; little do ye remember.
تَنزِيل مِّن رَّبِّ العَٰلَمِينَ43A sending down from the Lord of the worlds.
وَلَو تَقَوَّلَ عَلَينَا بَعضَ الأَقَاوِيلِ44And had he fabricated against Us some sayings,
لَأَخَذنَا مِنهُ بِاليَمِينِ45We would have seized him by the right hand,
ثُمَّ لَقَطَعنَا مِنهُ الوَتِينَ46then We would have severed from him the life-vein,
فَمَا مِنكُم مِّن أَحَدٍ عَنهُ حَٰجِزِينَ47and not one of you could have held Us off from him.
وَإِنَّهُ لَتَذكِرَة لِّلمُتَّقِينَ48And verily, it is a reminder for the pious.
وَإِنَّا لَنَعلَمُ أَنَّ مِنكُم مُّكَذِّبِينَ49And verily, We know that among you are deniers.
وَإِنَّهُ لَحَسرَةٌ عَلَى الكَٰفِرِينَ50And verily, it is a grief for those who conceal.
وَإِنَّهُ لَحَقُّ اليَقِينِ51And verily, it is the truth of certainty.
فَسَبِّح بِاسمِ رَبِّكَ العَظِيمِ52So glorify the name of thy Lord, the Tremendous.

487 Verse 34 ("he urged not the feeding of the indigent") connects to 74:44 and 76:8 — failure to feed is the recurring ethical failure that leads to the Fire.

Surah 70: Al-Ma'arij — The Ways of Ascent

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

سَأَلَ سَائِلُ بِعَذَاب وَاقِع1A questioner asked about a chastisement to befall
لِّلكَٰفِرِينَ لَيسَ لَهُ دَافِع2those who conceal; none can repel it,
مِّنَ اللَّهِ ذِي المَعَارِجِ3from God, Lord of the Ways of Ascent.488
تَعرُجُ المَلَـٰئِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ إِلَيهِ فِي يَوم كَانَ مِقدَارُهُ خَمسِينَ أَلفَ سَنَة4The angels and the Spirit ascend unto Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years.489
فَاصبِر صَبرا جَمِيلًا5So be patient with a fair patience.
إِنَّهُم يَرَونَهُ بَعِيدا6Verily, they see it far off,
وَنَرَىٰهُ قَرِيبا7but We see it nigh.
يَومَ تَكُونُ السَّمَاءُ كَالمُهلِ8On the Day when the heaven shall be as molten copper,
وَتَكُونُ الجِبَالُ كَالعِهنِ9and the mountains shall be as tufted wool,
وَلَا يَسـَلُ حَمِيمٌ حَمِيما10and no intimate friend shall ask of an intimate friend,
يُبَصَّرُونَهُم يَوَدُّ المُجرِمُ لَو يَفتَدِي مِن عَذَابِ يَومِئِذِ بِبَنِيهِ11though they are made to see one another. The guilty one would fain ransom himself from the chastisement of that Day by his children,
وَصَٰحِبَتِهِ وَأَخِيهِ12and his spouse and his brother,
وَفَصِيلَتِهِ الَّتِي تُـوِيهِ13and his kin that sheltered him,
وَمَن فِي الأَرضِ جَمِيعا ثُمَّ يُنجِيهِ14and all who are upon the earth, that then it might deliver him.
كَلَّا إِنَّهَا لَظَىٰ15Nay! Verily, it is a Raging Flame,
نَزَّاعَة لِّلشَّوَىٰ16a remover of the extremities,490
تَدعُواْ مَن أَدبَرَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ17calling him who turned his back and went away,
وَجَمَعَ فَأَوعَىٰ18and amassed and then hoarded.
إِنَّ الإِنسَٰنَ خُلِقَ هَلُوعًا19Verily, man was created anxious,491
إِذَا مَسَّهُ الشَّرُّ جَزُوعا20when evil toucheth him, fretful,
وَإِذَا مَسَّهُ الخَيرُ مَنُوعًا21and when good toucheth him, withholding,
إِلَّا المُصَلِّينَ22save those who pray,
الَّذِينَ هُم عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِم دَائِمُونَ23those who are constant at their prayer,
وَالَّذِينَ فِي أَموَٰلِهِم حَقّ مَّعلُوم24and those in whose wealth is a known right
لِّلسَّائِلِ وَالمَحرُومِ25for the beggar and the deprived,
وَالَّذِينَ يُصَدِّقُونَ بِيَومِ الدِّينِ26and those who affirm the Day of Judgement,
وَالَّذِينَ هُم مِّن عَذَابِ رَبِّهِم مُّشفِقُونَ27and those who are fearful of the chastisement of their Lord
إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّهِم غَيرُ مَأمُون28— verily, the chastisement of their Lord is not to be felt secure from —
وَالَّذِينَ هُم لِفُرُوجِهِم حَٰفِظُونَ29and those who guard their chastity,
إِلَّا عَلَىٰ أَزوَٰجِهِم أَو مَا مَلَكَت أَيمَٰنُهُم فَإِنَّهُم غَيرُ مَلُومِينَ30save with their spouses or what their right hands possess, for verily, they are not blameworthy.
فَمَنِ ابتَغَىٰ وَرَاءَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ العَادُونَ31But whosoever seeketh beyond that, those are the transgressors.
وَالَّذِينَ هُم لِأَمَٰنَٰتِهِم وَعَهدِهِم رَٰعُونَ32And those who are faithful to their trusts and their covenant,
وَالَّذِينَ هُم بِشَهَٰدَٰتِهِم قَائِمُونَ33and those who stand by their testimonies,
وَالَّذِينَ هُم عَلَىٰ صَلَاتِهِم يُحَافِظُونَ34and those who guard their prayer.
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ فِي جَنَّـٰت مُّكرَمُونَ35Those shall be in Gardens, honoured.
فَمَالِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ قِبَلَكَ مُهطِعِينَ36What aileth those who conceal, hastening before thee,
عَنِ اليَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمَالِ عِزِينَ37on the right and on the left, in groups?
أَيَطمَعُ كُلُّ امرِي مِّنهُم أَن يُدخَلَ جَنَّةَ نَعِيم38Doth every man among them covet that he should enter a Garden of Delight?
كَلَّا إِنَّا خَلَقنَٰهُم مِّمَّا يَعلَمُونَ39Nay! Verily, We created them of that which they know.
فَلَا أُقسِمُ بِرَبِّ المَشَٰرِقِ وَالمَغَٰرِبِ إِنَّا لَقَٰدِرُونَ40So nay! I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests, verily, We are Able
عَلَىٰ أَن نُّبَدِّلَ خَيرا مِّنهُم وَمَا نَحنُ بِمَسبُوقِينَ41to replace them with better than them, and We are not to be outstripped.
فَذَرهُم يَخُوضُواْ وَيَلعَبُواْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَٰقُواْ يَومَهُمُ الَّذِي يُوعَدُونَ42So leave them to plunge and play until they meet the Day which they are promised,
يَومَ يَخرُجُونَ مِنَ الأَجدَاثِ سِرَاعا كَأَنَّهُم إِلَىٰ نُصُب يُوفِضُونَ43the Day when they shall come forth from the graves in haste, as though they were racing unto a goal,
خَٰشِعَةً أَبصَٰرُهُم تَرهَقُهُم ذِلَّة ذَٰلِكَ اليَومُ الَّذِي كَانُواْ يُوعَدُونَ44their eyes downcast, abasement covering them. That is the Day which they were promised.

488 Verse 3: "God, Lord of the Ways of Ascent ()." -- means to ascend, to climb, to mount upward. The are the ascending stairways — plural, not singular. There are multiple ways of ascent to God, not one. Verse 4: "The angels and the Spirit ascend unto Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousa...

489 The fifty-thousand-year Day (v.4) contrasts with 32:5's thousand-year Day — different scales of divine time, suggesting that different spiritual processes have different durations. Verse 19's created anxiety connects to 76:2 ("We created man from a mixed drop, to try him") — the human is created ...

490 Verse 16: "a remover of the extremities ( )." -- means to pull out, to strip, to remove. The Raging Flame strips away the (extremities, outer skin) — it removes the ẓāhir to expose the bāṭin. Hellfire as the forcible removal of coverings: those who covered over (--) have their coverings burned away.

491 Verse 19: "man was created anxious ()." -- means to be anxious, to be restless, to be impatient. This is a rare word — a hapax legomenon. Anxiety is not an accident but a created condition: God made humans anxious. Verses 20–21 explain: "when evil toucheth him, fretful, and when good toucheth him...

Surah 71: Nuh — Noah

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِنَّا أَرسَلنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَومِهِ أَن أَنذِر قَومَكَ مِن قَبلِ أَن يَأتِيَهُم عَذَابٌ أَلِيم1Verily, We sent Noah unto his people: "Warn thy people before there cometh upon them a painful chastisement."
قَالَ يَٰقَومِ إِنِّي لَكُم نَذِير مُّبِينٌ2He said: "O my people! Verily, I am unto you a clear warner:
أَنِ اعبُدُواْ اللَّهَ وَاتَّقُوهُ وَأَطِيعُونِ3that ye worship God and fear Him and obey me.
يَغفِر لَكُم مِّن ذُنُوبِكُم وَيُؤَخِّركُم إِلَىٰ أَجَل مُّسَمًّى إِنَّ أَجَلَ اللَّهِ إِذَا جَاءَ لَا يُؤَخَّرُ لَو كُنتُم تَعلَمُونَ4He will forgive you of your sins and defer you unto an appointed term. Verily, the term of God, when it cometh, shall not be deferred, if ye but knew."
قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي دَعَوتُ قَومِي لَيلا وَنَهَارا5He said: "My Lord! Verily, I have called my people night and day,
فَلَم يَزِدهُم دُعَاءِي إِلَّا فِرَارا6but my calling hath only increased them in flight.492
وَإِنِّي كُلَّمَا دَعَوتُهُم لِتَغفِرَ لَهُم جَعَلُواْ أَصَٰبِعَهُم فِي ءَاذَانِهِم وَاستَغشَواْ ثِيَابَهُم وَأَصَرُّواْ وَاستَكبَرُواْ استِكبَارا7And verily, whenever I called them, that Thou mightest forgive them, they put their fingers in their ears and wrapped themselves in their garments, and persisted, and were exceedingly proud.493
ثُمَّ إِنِّي دَعَوتُهُم جِهَارا8Then verily, I called them openly;
ثُمَّ إِنِّي أَعلَنتُ لَهُم وَأَسرَرتُ لَهُم إِسرَارا9then verily, I proclaimed unto them and confided unto them in secret,494
فَقُلتُ استَغفِرُواْ رَبَّكُم إِنَّهُ كَانَ غَفَّارا10and I said: 'Seek forgiveness of your Lord; verily, He is ever Forgiving.
يُرسِلِ السَّمَاءَ عَلَيكُم مِّدرَارا11He will send the sky upon you in abundant rain,
وَيُمدِدكُم بِأَموَٰل وَبَنِينَ وَيَجعَل لَّكُم جَنَّـٰت وَيَجعَل لَّكُم أَنهَٰرا12and will aid you with wealth and sons, and will appoint for you gardens, and will appoint for you rivers.
مَّا لَكُم لَا تَرجُونَ لِلَّهِ وَقَارا13What aileth you that ye hope not toward God for dignity,495
وَقَد خَلَقَكُم أَطوَارًا14when He hath created you in stages?
أَلَم تَرَواْ كَيفَ خَلَقَ اللَّهُ سَبعَ سَمَٰوَٰت طِبَاقا15See ye not how God hath created seven heavens in layers,
وَجَعَلَ القَمَرَ فِيهِنَّ نُورا وَجَعَلَ الشَّمسَ سِرَاجا16and hath set the moon therein as a light and hath set the sun as a lamp?
وَاللَّهُ أَنبَتَكُم مِّنَ الأَرضِ نَبَاتا17And God hath caused you to grow from the earth as a growth.
ثُمَّ يُعِيدُكُم فِيهَا وَيُخرِجُكُم إِخرَاجا18Then He will return you into it and bring you forth a bringing forth.
وَاللَّهُ جَعَلَ لَكُمُ الأَرضَ بِسَاطا19And God hath made the earth for you a spreading,
لِّتَسلُكُواْ مِنهَا سُبُلا فِجَاجا20that ye may traverse therein ways, wide valleys.'"
قَالَ نُوح رَّبِّ إِنَّهُم عَصَونِي وَاتَّبَعُواْ مَن لَّم يَزِدهُ مَالُهُ وَوَلَدُهُ إِلَّا خَسَارا21Noah said: "My Lord! Verily, they have disobeyed me and followed him whose wealth and children increase him in naught but loss.
وَمَكَرُواْ مَكرا كُبَّارا22And they have plotted a mighty plot,
وَقَالُواْ لَا تَذَرُنَّ ءَالِهَتَكُم وَلَا تَذَرُنَّ وَدّا وَلَا سُوَاعا وَلَا يَغُوثَ وَيَعُوقَ وَنَسرا23and have said: 'Forsake not your gods! Forsake not Wadd, nor Suwa, nor Yaghuth, and Ya'uq, and Nasr!'
وَقَد أَضَلُّواْ كَثِيرا وَلَا تَزِدِ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا ضَلَٰلا24And indeed they have led many astray; and increase Thou not the wrongdoers save in error."
مِّمَّا خَطِيـَٰتِهِم أُغرِقُواْ فَأُدخِلُواْ نَارا فَلَم يَجِدُواْ لَهُم مِّن دُونِ اللَّهِ أَنصَارا25Because of their sins they were drowned, then made to enter the Fire, and they found for themselves, apart from God, no helpers.496
وَقَالَ نُوح رَّبِّ لَا تَذَر عَلَى الأَرضِ مِنَ الكَٰفِرِينَ دَيَّارًا26And Noah said: "My Lord! Leave not upon the earth any one of those who conceal, dwelling therein.
إِنَّكَ إِن تَذَرهُم يُضِلُّواْ عِبَادَكَ وَلَا يَلِدُواْ إِلَّا فَاجِرا كَفَّارا27Verily, if Thou leavest them, they will lead Thy servants astray and will beget none save wicked ones who conceal.
رَّبِّ اغفِر لِي وَلِوَٰلِدَيَّ وَلِمَن دَخَلَ بَيتِيَ مُؤمِنا وَلِلمُؤمِنِينَ وَالمُؤمِنَٰتِ وَلَا تَزِدِ الظَّـٰلِمِينَ إِلَّا تَبَارَا28My Lord! Forgive me and my parents and him who entereth my house as a believer, and the believing men and the believing women; and increase not the wrongdoers save in ruin."

492 Verses 5–9: Noah's calling is described with escalating intensity: "I have called () my people night and day... I called them openly... I proclaimed unto them and confided unto them in secret." -- means to call, to summon, to invite. Noah exhausts every mode of calling — public and private, day a...

493 Verse 7: "they put their fingers in their ears and wrapped themselves in their garments ( )." -- means to cover, to wrap over. The covering over is literal: they physically cover themselves to block Noah's voice. The garment () becomes a tool of -- — clothing as concealment from truth. This inver...

494 Noah's dual calling (open and secret, v.9) mirrors the ẓāhir/bāṭin structure: revelation operates on both planes. This connects to 58:7 (no secret conference but God is present) — even the secret call is witnessed. Verse 25 ("because of their sins they were drowned, then made to enter the Fire") ...

495 Verse 13: "What aileth you that ye hope not toward God for dignity ()?" -- means gravity, dignity, weight. Conventional translations often render this as "you do not fear God's majesty" — but the Arabic can be read as "you do not hope for dignity from God." Noah is asking: why do you not expect G...

496 Verse 25 ("because of their sins they were drowned, then made to enter the Fire") describes a transition from physical punishment (flood) to spiritual punishment (fire) — water and fire as the two instruments of divine judgement, connecting to 55:15 (spectres from fire) and 55:19 (the two seas).

Surah 72: Al-Jinn — The Spectres

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

قُل أُوحِيَ إِلَيَّ أَنَّهُ استَمَعَ نَفَر مِّنَ الجِنِّ فَقَالُواْ إِنَّا سَمِعنَا قُرءَانًا عَجَبا1Say: "It hath been revealed unto me that a company of the Spectres hearkened, and they said: 'Verily, we have heard a wondrous Recitation.497
يَهدِي إِلَى الرُّشدِ فَـَامَنَّا بِهِ وَلَن نُّشرِكَ بِرَبِّنَا أَحَدا2It guideth unto right conduct, so we have believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord.
وَأَنَّهُ تَعَٰلَىٰ جَدُّ رَبِّنَا مَا اتَّخَذَ صَٰحِبَة وَلَا وَلَدا3And that He — exalted be the majesty of our Lord! — hath taken neither spouse nor son.
وَأَنَّهُ كَانَ يَقُولُ سَفِيهُنَا عَلَى اللَّهِ شَطَطا4And that the foolish one among us used to utter against God an outrageous lie.
وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّا أَن لَّن تَقُولَ الإِنسُ وَالجِنُّ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبا5And that we supposed that humankind and the Spectres would never utter a lie against God.
وَأَنَّهُ كَانَ رِجَال مِّنَ الإِنسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجَال مِّنَ الجِنِّ فَزَادُوهُم رَهَقا6And that men among humankind used to seek refuge with men among the Spectres, so they increased them in burden.498
وَأَنَّهُم ظَنُّواْ كَمَا ظَنَنتُم أَن لَّن يَبعَثَ اللَّهُ أَحَدا7And that they supposed, even as ye supposed, that God would never raise anyone.
وَأَنَّا لَمَسنَا السَّمَاءَ فَوَجَدنَٰهَا مُلِئَت حَرَسا شَدِيدا وَشُهُبا8And that we touched the heaven and found it filled with stern guards and shooting stars.499
وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقعُدُ مِنهَا مَقَٰعِدَ لِلسَّمعِ فَمَن يَستَمِعِ الأنَ يَجِد لَهُ شِهَابا رَّصَدا9And that we used to sit in seats thereof to hear; but whosoever hearkeneth now findeth a shooting star lying in wait for him.
وَأَنَّا لَا نَدرِي أَشَرٌّ أُرِيدَ بِمَن فِي الأَرضِ أَم أَرَادَ بِهِم رَبُّهُم رَشَدا10And that we know not whether evil is intended for those in the earth, or whether their Lord intendeth for them right guidance.
وَأَنَّا مِنَّا الصَّـٰلِحُونَ وَمِنَّا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ كُنَّا طَرَائِقَ قِدَدا11And that among us are the righteous, and among us are other than that; we are sects of diverse ways.
وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّا أَن لَّن نُّعجِزَ اللَّهَ فِي الأَرضِ وَلَن نُّعجِزَهُ هَرَبا12And that we supposed that we could never frustrate God in the earth, nor could we frustrate Him by flight.
وَأَنَّا لَمَّا سَمِعنَا الهُدَىٰ ءَامَنَّا بِهِ فَمَن يُؤمِن بِرَبِّهِ فَلَا يَخَافُ بَخسا وَلَا رَهَقا13And that when we heard the Guidance, we believed in it; and whosoever believeth in his Lord, he feareth neither loss nor burden.
وَأَنَّا مِنَّا المُسلِمُونَ وَمِنَّا القَٰسِطُونَ فَمَن أَسلَمَ فَأُوْلَـٰئِكَ تَحَرَّواْ رَشَدا14And that among us are the Devoted, and among us are the unjust. And whosoever hath Devoted, those have sought right guidance.500
وَأَمَّا القَٰسِطُونَ فَكَانُواْ لِجَهَنَّمَ حَطَبا15But as for the unjust, they shall be firewood for Hell.'"
وَأَلَّوِ استَقَٰمُواْ عَلَى الطَّرِيقَةِ لَأَسقَينَٰهُم مَّاءً غَدَقا16And had they kept straight upon the way, We would have given them water in abundance to drink,
لِّنَفتِنَهُم فِيهِ وَمَن يُعرِض عَن ذِكرِ رَبِّهِ يَسلُكهُ عَذَابا صَعَدا17that We might try them thereby. And whosoever turneth away from the remembrance of his Lord, He will thrust him into an ever-mounting chastisement.
وَأَنَّ المَسَٰجِدَ لِلَّهِ فَلَا تَدعُواْ مَعَ اللَّهِ أَحَدا18And that the places of worship are for God, so call not upon anyone alongside God.501
وَأَنَّهُ لَمَّا قَامَ عَبدُ اللَّهِ يَدعُوهُ كَادُواْ يَكُونُونَ عَلَيهِ لِبَدا19And that when the servant of God stood up calling upon Him, they wellnigh pressed upon him in crowds.
قُل إِنَّمَا أَدعُواْ رَبِّي وَلَا أُشرِكُ بِهِ أَحَدا20Say: "I call only upon my Lord, and I associate with Him no one."
قُل إِنِّي لَا أَملِكُ لَكُم ضَرّا وَلَا رَشَدا21Say: "Verily, I possess for you neither harm nor right guidance."
قُل إِنِّي لَن يُجِيرَنِي مِنَ اللَّهِ أَحَد وَلَن أَجِدَ مِن دُونِهِ مُلتَحَدًا22Say: "Verily, none shall shelter me from God, nor shall I find apart from Him any refuge,
إِلَّا بَلَٰغا مِّنَ اللَّهِ وَرِسَٰلَٰتِهِ وَمَن يَعصِ اللَّهَ وَرَسُولَهُ فَإِنَّ لَهُ نَارَ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدًا23save a conveyance from God and His messages. And whosoever disobeyeth God and His Messenger, verily for him is the Fire of Hell, to abide therein forever."
حَتَّىٰ إِذَا رَأَواْ مَا يُوعَدُونَ فَسَيَعلَمُونَ مَن أَضعَفُ نَاصِرا وَأَقَلُّ عَدَدا24Until when they see that which they are promised, then they shall know who is weaker in helpers and fewer in number.
قُل إِن أَدرِي أَقَرِيب مَّا تُوعَدُونَ أَم يَجعَلُ لَهُ رَبِّي أَمَدًا25Say: "I know not whether that which ye are promised is nigh, or whether my Lord will appoint for it a distant term."
عَٰلِمُ الغَيبِ فَلَا يُظهِرُ عَلَىٰ غَيبِهِ أَحَدًا26Knower of the unseen! And He revealeth unto none His unseen,
إِلَّا مَنِ ارتَضَىٰ مِن رَّسُول فَإِنَّهُ يَسلُكُ مِن بَينِ يَدَيهِ وَمِن خَلفِهِ رَصَدا27save unto a messenger whom He hath approved; then verily, He sendeth before him and behind him watchers,
لِّيَعلَمَ أَن قَد أَبلَغُواْ رِسَٰلَٰتِ رَبِّهِم وَأَحَاطَ بِمَا لَدَيهِم وَأَحصَىٰ كُلَّ شَيءٍ عَدَدَا28that He may know that they have indeed conveyed the messages of their Lord. And He encompasseth what is with them, and He reckoneth all things in number.

497 The entire surah is the hidden dimension speaking. Verse 1: "a company of the Spectres hearkened, and they said: 'Verily, we have heard a wondrous Recitation.'" The spectres — the concealed ones (root --) — are listeners to revelation. They do not create or rival revelation; they receive it. This...

498 Verse 6 ("men among humankind used to seek refuge with men among the Spectres, so they increased them in burden") connects to 55:33's address to both spectres and men — the two communities are intertwined, and their relationship can be beneficial or burdensome. The spectres' confession "we touche...

499 The spectres' confession "we touched the heaven and found it filled with stern guards" (v.8) connects to 67:5 where the nearest heaven is adorned with lamps that are missiles against devils. The cosmic architecture is consistent: the heaven is guarded.

500 Verse 14: "among us are the Devoted (), and among us are the unjust." The spectres themselves divide into those who devotion and those who do not. -- here applies to non-human beings — devotion is not a human-only category. If the spectres represent the hidden energetic dimension (see 55:15, "cur...

501 Verse 18: "the places of worship () are for God, so call not upon anyone alongside God." -- (to prostrate) appears here in the spectres' testimony. Worship-places belong to God alone — stated by the hidden beings, not by humans. The spectres affirm tawḥīd (divine unity) from within the concealed ...

Surah 73: Al-Muzzammil — The Enwrapped

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا المُزَّمِّلُ1O thou who art enwrapped!502
قُمِ الَّيلَ إِلَّا قَلِيلا2Rise in the night, save a little,503
نِّصفَهُ أَوِ انقُص مِنهُ قَلِيلًا3half of it, or diminish therefrom a little,
أَو زِد عَلَيهِ وَرَتِّلِ القُرءَانَ تَرتِيلًا4or add thereto, and chant the Recitation with a measured chanting.504
إِنَّا سَنُلقِي عَلَيكَ قَولا ثَقِيلًا5Verily, We shall cast upon thee a weighty word.
إِنَّ نَاشِئَةَ الَّيلِ هِيَ أَشَدُّ وَطـا وَأَقوَمُ قِيلًا6Verily, the rising by night, it is firmer of tread and more upright of speech.
إِنَّ لَكَ فِي النَّهَارِ سَبحا طَوِيلا7Verily, for thee in the day is a long course of duties.
وَاذكُرِ اسمَ رَبِّكَ وَتَبَتَّل إِلَيهِ تَبتِيلا8And remember the name of thy Lord, and surrender thyself unto Him with utter surrender.
رَّبُّ المَشرِقِ وَالمَغرِبِ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ فَاتَّخِذهُ وَكِيلا9Lord of the East and the West; there is no god but He; so take Him for a Guardian.
وَاصبِر عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَاهجُرهُم هَجرا جَمِيلا10And bear patiently what they say, and withdraw from them with a fair withdrawal.
وَذَرنِي وَالمُكَذِّبِينَ أُوْلِي النَّعمَةِ وَمَهِّلهُم قَلِيلًا11And leave Me with those who deny, possessors of ease, and grant them respite a little.
إِنَّ لَدَينَا أَنكَالا وَجَحِيما12Verily, with Us are shackles and a Blaze,
وَطَعَاما ذَا غُصَّة وَعَذَابًا أَلِيما13and food that choketh, and a painful chastisement,
يَومَ تَرجُفُ الأَرضُ وَالجِبَالُ وَكَانَتِ الجِبَالُ كَثِيبا مَّهِيلًا14on the Day when the earth and the mountains shall quake, and the mountains shall become a heap of sand, poured forth.
