إِنَّا أَرسَلنَا إِلَيكُم رَسُولا شَٰهِدًا عَلَيكُم كَمَا أَرسَلنَا إِلَىٰ فِرعَونَ رَسُولا15Verily, We have sent unto you a Messenger, a witness over you, even as We sent unto Pharaoh a messenger.505
فَعَصَىٰ فِرعَونُ الرَّسُولَ فَأَخَذنَٰهُ أَخذا وَبِيلا16But Pharaoh disobeyed the messenger, so We seized him with a ruinous seizure.
فَكَيفَ تَتَّقُونَ إِن كَفَرتُم يَوما يَجعَلُ الوِلدَٰنَ شِيبًا17Then how shall ye, if ye conceal, guard yourselves against a Day that shall make the children grey-haired?
السَّمَاءُ مُنفَطِرُ بِهِ كَانَ وَعدُهُ مَفعُولًا18The heaven shall be rent asunder thereby; His promise is ever fulfilled.
إِنَّ هَٰذِهِ تَذكِرَة فَمَن شَاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ سَبِيلًا19Verily, this is a Reminder; so let him who willeth take a path unto his Lord.
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَعلَمُ أَنَّكَ تَقُومُ أَدنَىٰ مِن ثُلُثَيِ الَّيلِ وَنِصفَهُ وَثُلُثَهُ وَطَائِفَة مِّنَ الَّذِينَ مَعَكَ وَاللَّهُ يُقَدِّرُ الَّيلَ وَالنَّهَارَ عَلِمَ أَن لَّن تُحصُوهُ فَتَابَ عَلَيكُم فَاقرَءُواْ مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنَ القُرءَانِ عَلِمَ أَن سَيَكُونُ مِنكُم مَّرضَىٰ وَءَاخَرُونَ يَضرِبُونَ فِي الأَرضِ يَبتَغُونَ مِن فَضلِ اللَّهِ وَءَاخَرُونَ يُقَٰتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ فَاقرَءُواْ مَا تَيَسَّرَ مِنهُ وَأَقِيمُواْ الصَّلَوٰةَ وَءَاتُواْ الزَّكَوٰةَ وَأَقرِضُواْ اللَّهَ قَرضًا حَسَنا وَمَا تُقَدِّمُواْ لِأَنفُسِكُم مِّن خَير تَجِدُوهُ عِندَ اللَّهِ هُوَ خَيرا وَأَعظَمَ أَجرا وَاستَغفِرُواْ اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ غَفُور رَّحِيمُ20Verily, thy Lord knoweth that thou risest nearly two-thirds of the night, and half of it, and a third of it — and a party of those who are with thee. And God measureth the night and the day; He knoweth that ye will not reckon it, so He hath turned unto you in mercy. So recite what is easy of the Recitation; He knoweth that there will be among you sick ones, and others who journey in the earth seeking of the bounty of God, and others who fight in the way of God. So recite what is easy thereof; and establish the prayer, and pay the alms, and lend unto God a goodly loan. And whatsoever good ye send ahead for your souls, ye shall find it with God — it is better and greater in reward. And seek forgiveness of God; verily, God is Forgiving, Merciful.

502 Verse 1: "O thou who art enwrapped ()!" -- means to wrap up, to bundle, to carry a load. The Prophet is enwrapped — concealed in garments, in a j-n-n-like state of hiddenness. This is the companion surah to 74 (, the Cloaked): both open with the Prophet in concealment and command him to emerge. T...

503 Verse 5: "We shall cast upon thee a weighty word ( )." -- means heavy, burdensome, weighty. The Qur'an is described as physically heavy — revelation has weight. This is not metaphorical: the Prophet reportedly experienced physical distress during revelation. The weighty word requires the night ri...

504 Verse 4: "chant the Recitation with a measured chanting ( )." -- means to arrange, to set in order, to recite clearly and slowly. The cognate accusative () intensifies: chant with true chanting. The Recitation is not to be rushed but measured — each word given its due weight. This connects the Re...

505 Verse 15 ("We have sent unto you a Messenger, a witness over you, even as We sent unto Pharaoh a messenger") directly parallels the Muhammad-Moses typology, connecting to 44:17–18 and the Pharaoh narratives throughout. Verse 20's permission to "recite what is easy of the Recitation" softens the i...

Surah 74: Al-Muddaththir — The Cloaked

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

يَـٰأَيُّهَا المُدَّثِّرُ1O thou who art cloaked!
قُم فَأَنذِر2Arise and warn!
وَرَبَّكَ فَكَبِّر3And thy Lord, magnify Him!
وَثِيَابَكَ فَطَهِّر4And thy garments, purify them!
وَالرُّجزَ فَاهجُر5And defilement, shun it!
وَلَا تَمنُن تَستَكثِرُ6And bestow not favour, seeking increase.
وَلِرَبِّكَ فَاصبِر7And for thy Lord, be patient.
فَإِذَا نُقِرَ فِي النَّاقُورِ8Then when the Trumpet is sounded,
فَذَٰلِكَ يَومَئِذ يَومٌ عَسِيرٌ9that, on that Day, shall be a difficult day,
عَلَى الكَٰفِرِينَ غَيرُ يَسِير10for those who conceal, not easy.506
ذَرنِي وَمَن خَلَقتُ وَحِيدا11Leave Me with him whom I created alone,
وَجَعَلتُ لَهُ مَالا مَّمدُودا12and unto whom I appointed wealth extended,
وَبَنِينَ شُهُودا13and sons as witnesses,
وَمَهَّدتُّ لَهُ تَمهِيدا14and for whom I made all things smooth.
ثُمَّ يَطمَعُ أَن أَزِيدَ15Then he coveteth that I should give more.
كَلَّا إِنَّهُ كَانَ لِأيَٰتِنَا عَنِيدا16Nay! Verily, he hath been stubborn toward Our signs.
سَأُرهِقُهُ صَعُودًا17I shall constrain him to a hard ascent.
إِنَّهُ فَكَّرَ وَقَدَّرَ18Verily, he reflected and he measured.
فَقُتِلَ كَيفَ قَدَّرَ19So may he perish, how he measured!
ثُمَّ قُتِلَ كَيفَ قَدَّرَ20Then may he perish, how he measured!
ثُمَّ نَظَرَ21Then he looked,
ثُمَّ عَبَسَ وَبَسَرَ22then he frowned and scowled,
ثُمَّ أَدبَرَ وَاستَكبَرَ23then he turned his back and was proud,
فَقَالَ إِن هَٰذَا إِلَّا سِحر يُؤثَرُ24and said: "This is naught but sorcery handed down.
إِن هَٰذَا إِلَّا قَولُ البَشَرِ25This is naught but the speech of a mortal."
سَأُصلِيهِ سَقَرَ26I shall cast him into Hellfire.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا سَقَرُ27And what shall make thee know what Hellfire is?
لَا تُبقِي وَلَا تَذَرُ28It spareth not and leaveth not.
لَوَّاحَة لِّلبَشَرِ29It scorcheth mortals.
عَلَيهَا تِسعَةَ عَشَرَ30Over it are nineteen.507
وَمَا جَعَلنَا أَصحَٰبَ النَّارِ إِلَّا مَلَـٰئِكَة وَمَا جَعَلنَا عِدَّتَهُم إِلَّا فِتنَة لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ لِيَستَيقِنَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ الكِتَٰبَ وَيَزدَادَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ إِيمَٰنا وَلَا يَرتَابَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ الكِتَٰبَ وَالمُؤمِنُونَ وَلِيَقُولَ الَّذِينَ فِي قُلُوبِهِم مَّرَض وَالكَٰفِرُونَ مَاذَا أَرَادَ اللَّهُ بِهَٰذَا مَثَلا كَذَٰلِكَ يُضِلُّ اللَّهُ مَن يَشَاءُ وَيَهدِي مَن يَشَاءُ وَمَا يَعلَمُ جُنُودَ رَبِّكَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَمَا هِيَ إِلَّا ذِكرَىٰ لِلبَشَرِ31And We have set none but angels as wardens of the Fire; and We have made their number only a trial for those who conceal — that those who were given the Book may be certain, and those who believe may increase in faith, and those who were given the Book and the believers may not doubt — and that those in whose hearts is a sickness and those who conceal may say: "What doth God intend by this as a likeness?" Thus God sendeth astray whom He willeth and guideth whom He willeth. And none knoweth the hosts of thy Lord save He; and it is naught but a Reminder for mortals.
كَلَّا وَالقَمَرِ32Nay! By the moon,
وَالَّيلِ إِذ أَدبَرَ33and the night when it retreateth,
وَالصُّبحِ إِذَا أَسفَرَ34and the morning when it brighteneth!
إِنَّهَا لَإِحدَى الكُبَرِ35Verily, it is one of the greatest things,
نَذِيرا لِّلبَشَرِ36a warning unto mortals,
لِمَن شَاءَ مِنكُم أَن يَتَقَدَّمَ أَو يَتَأَخَّرَ37for him among you who willeth to advance or to hold back.
كُلُّ نَفسِ بِمَا كَسَبَت رَهِينَةٌ38Every soul is pledged for what it hath earned,
إِلَّا أَصحَٰبَ اليَمِينِ39save the companions of the right hand.
فِي جَنَّـٰت يَتَسَاءَلُونَ40In Gardens, they shall question one another
عَنِ المُجرِمِينَ41concerning the guilty:
مَا سَلَكَكُم فِي سَقَرَ42"What hath brought you into Hellfire?"
قَالُواْ لَم نَكُ مِنَ المُصَلِّينَ43They shall say: "We were not of those who prayed,
وَلَم نَكُ نُطعِمُ المِسكِينَ44and we fed not the indigent,
وَكُنَّا نَخُوضُ مَعَ الخَائِضِينَ45and we used to plunge with those who plunge,
وَكُنَّا نُكَذِّبُ بِيَومِ الدِّينِ46and we used to deny the Day of Judgement,
حَتَّىٰ أَتَىٰنَا اليَقِينُ47until the certainty came upon us."
فَمَا تَنفَعُهُم شَفَٰعَةُ الشَّـٰفِعِينَ48So the intercession of the intercessors shall avail them not.
فَمَا لَهُم عَنِ التَّذكِرَةِ مُعرِضِينَ49What aileth them then, that they turn away from the Reminder,
كَأَنَّهُم حُمُر مُّستَنفِرَة50as though they were startled donkeys
فَرَّت مِن قَسوَرَةِ51that had fled from a lion?
بَل يُرِيدُ كُلُّ امرِي مِّنهُم أَن يُؤتَىٰ صُحُفا مُّنَشَّرَة52Nay, every man among them desireth that he should be given scrolls unrolled.
كَلَّا بَل لَّا يَخَافُونَ الأخِرَةَ53Nay! But they fear not the Hereafter.
كَلَّا إِنَّهُ تَذكِرَة54Nay! Verily, it is a Reminder.
فَمَن شَاءَ ذَكَرَهُ55So whosoever willeth, let him remember it.
وَمَا يَذكُرُونَ إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ هُوَ أَهلُ التَّقوَىٰ وَأَهلُ المَغفِرَةِ56And they will not remember unless God willeth. He is worthy of being feared, and He is worthy of forgiveness.

506 Verses 10, 31, and the structure of the entire surah pivot on covering over. Verse 10: the Day is "for those who cover over, not easy." Verse 31: the number nineteen is a trial specifically for "those who have covered over." saturates the surah — covering over is the opposite of the Prophet's com...

507 Verse 30: "Over it are nineteen." This is the most numerically charged verse in the Qur'an. Verse 31 immediately explains: the number is "a trial for those who have covered over" and a means of certainty for the People of the Book. The number 19 has generated vast commentary — from the Bahá'í cal...

Surah 75: Al-Qiyamah — The Resurrection

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

لَا أُقسِمُ بِيَومِ القِيَٰمَةِ1Nay! I swear by the Day of Resurrection,
وَلَا أُقسِمُ بِالنَّفسِ اللَّوَّامَةِ2and nay! I swear by the self-reproaching soul.508
أَيَحسَبُ الإِنسَٰنُ أَلَّن نَّجمَعَ عِظَامَهُ3Doth man suppose that We shall not gather his bones?
بَلَىٰ قَٰدِرِينَ عَلَىٰ أَن نُّسَوِّيَ بَنَانَهُ4Yea indeed! We are able to fashion even his fingertips.
بَل يُرِيدُ الإِنسَٰنُ لِيَفجُرَ أَمَامَهُ5Nay, but man desireth to go on sinning before him.
يَسـَلُ أَيَّانَ يَومُ القِيَٰمَةِ6He asketh: "When is the Day of Resurrection?"
فَإِذَا بَرِقَ البَصَرُ7Then when the sight is dazzled,
وَخَسَفَ القَمَرُ8and the moon is eclipsed,
وَجُمِعَ الشَّمسُ وَالقَمَرُ9and the sun and moon are joined together,
يَقُولُ الإِنسَٰنُ يَومَئِذٍ أَينَ المَفَرُّ10on that Day man shall say: "Whither to flee?"
كَلَّا لَا وَزَرَ11Nay! There is no refuge.
إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ يَومَئِذٍ المُستَقَرُّ12Unto thy Lord on that Day is the settlement.
يُنَبَّؤُاْ الإِنسَٰنُ يَومَئِذِ بِمَا قَدَّمَ وَأَخَّرَ13Man shall be informed on that Day of what he sent ahead and what he left behind.
بَلِ الإِنسَٰنُ عَلَىٰ نَفسِهِ بَصِيرَة14Nay, man shall be a witness against himself,
وَلَو أَلقَىٰ مَعَاذِيرَهُ15even though he casteth forth his excuses.
لَا تُحَرِّك بِهِ لِسَانَكَ لِتَعجَلَ بِهِ16Move not thy tongue therewith to hasten it.
إِنَّ عَلَينَا جَمعَهُ وَقُرءَانَهُ17Verily, upon Us is its gathering and its recitation.
فَإِذَا قَرَأنَٰهُ فَاتَّبِع قُرءَانَهُ18So when We recite it, follow thou its recitation.
ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَينَا بَيَانَهُ19Then verily, upon Us is its exposition.
كَلَّا بَل تُحِبُّونَ العَاجِلَةَ20Nay! But ye love the fleeting life,
وَتَذَرُونَ الأخِرَةَ21and ye forsake the Hereafter.
وُجُوه يَومَئِذ نَّاضِرَةٌ22Faces on that Day shall be radiant,
إِلَىٰ رَبِّهَا نَاظِرَة23looking unto their Lord.
وَوُجُوه يَومَئِذِ بَاسِرَة24And faces on that Day shall be gloomy,
تَظُنُّ أَن يُفعَلَ بِهَا فَاقِرَة25knowing that a calamity is to befall them.
كَلَّا إِذَا بَلَغَتِ التَّرَاقِيَ26Nay! When it reacheth the collarbones,
وَقِيلَ مَن رَاق27and it is said: "Who is a healer?"
وَظَنَّ أَنَّهُ الفِرَاقُ28and he thinketh that it is the parting,
وَالتَفَّتِ السَّاقُ بِالسَّاقِ29and leg is entwined with leg,
إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ يَومَئِذٍ المَسَاقُ30unto thy Lord on that Day is the driving.
فَلَا صَدَّقَ وَلَا صَلَّىٰ31For he affirmed not, nor did he pray,
وَلَٰكِن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ32but he denied and turned away,
ثُمَّ ذَهَبَ إِلَىٰ أَهلِهِ يَتَمَطَّىٰ33then he went unto his family, swaggering.
أَولَىٰ لَكَ فَأَولَىٰ34Woe unto thee, and woe!
ثُمَّ أَولَىٰ لَكَ فَأَولَىٰ35Then woe unto thee, and woe!
أَيَحسَبُ الإِنسَٰنُ أَن يُترَكَ سُدًى36Doth man suppose that he will be left aimless?
أَلَم يَكُ نُطفَة مِّن مَّنِيّ يُمنَىٰ37Was he not a drop of seed that was emitted?
ثُمَّ كَانَ عَلَقَة فَخَلَقَ فَسَوَّىٰ38Then he was a clot, and He created and proportioned,
فَجَعَلَ مِنهُ الزَّوجَينِ الذَّكَرَ وَالأُنثَىٰ39and He made of him the two kinds, the male and the female.
أَلَيسَ ذَٰلِكَ بِقَٰدِرٍ عَلَىٰ أَن يُحـِيَ المَوتَىٰ40Is not He able to give life unto the dead?

508 Verse 2: "I swear by the self-reproaching soul ( )." -- means to blame, to reproach. The (lawwāmah) is the soul that blames itself — the conscience. God swears by the conscience alongside the Resurrection: the inner judge and the outer Judge. The self-reproaching soul is the bāṭin of judgement; t...

Surah 76: Al-Insan — The Human

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

هَل أَتَىٰ عَلَى الإِنسَٰنِ حِين مِّنَ الدَّهرِ لَم يَكُن شَيـا مَّذكُورًا1Hath there come upon man a span of time when he was a thing not remembered?509
إِنَّا خَلَقنَا الإِنسَٰنَ مِن نُّطفَةٍ أَمشَاج نَّبتَلِيهِ فَجَعَلنَٰهُ سَمِيعَا بَصِيرًا2Verily, We created man from a mixed drop, to try him; and We made him hearing, seeing.
إِنَّا هَدَينَٰهُ السَّبِيلَ إِمَّا شَاكِرا وَإِمَّا كَفُورًا3Verily, We guided him unto the way, whether he be thankful or whether he be ungrateful.510
إِنَّا أَعتَدنَا لِلكَٰفِرِينَ سَلَٰسِلَاْ وَأَغلَٰلا وَسَعِيرًا4Verily, We have prepared for those who conceal chains and shackles and a Blaze.
إِنَّ الأَبرَارَ يَشرَبُونَ مِن كَأس كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا كَافُورًا5Verily, the righteous shall drink of a cup whose mixture is of camphor,
عَينا يَشرَبُ بِهَا عِبَادُ اللَّهِ يُفَجِّرُونَهَا تَفجِيرا6a fountain wherefrom the servants of God drink, making it gush forth abundantly.
يُوفُونَ بِالنَّذرِ وَيَخَافُونَ يَوما كَانَ شَرُّهُ مُستَطِيرا7They fulfil the vow and fear a Day whose evil shall be widespread,
وَيُطعِمُونَ الطَّعَامَ عَلَىٰ حُبِّهِ مِسكِينا وَيَتِيما وَأَسِيرًا8and they feed, for the love of Him, the indigent, the orphan, and the captive:511
إِنَّمَا نُطعِمُكُم لِوَجهِ اللَّهِ لَا نُرِيدُ مِنكُم جَزَاء وَلَا شُكُورًا9"We feed you only for the countenance of God; we desire from you neither recompense nor thanks.
إِنَّا نَخَافُ مِن رَّبِّنَا يَومًا عَبُوسا قَمطَرِيرا10Verily, we fear from our Lord a Day frowning and distressful."
فَوَقَىٰهُمُ اللَّهُ شَرَّ ذَٰلِكَ اليَومِ وَلَقَّىٰهُم نَضرَة وَسُرُورا11So God shall shield them from the evil of that Day and shall grant them radiance and joy,
وَجَزَىٰهُم بِمَا صَبَرُواْ جَنَّة وَحَرِيرا12and shall reward them, for that they were patient, with a Garden and silk.
مُّتَّكِـِينَ فِيهَا عَلَى الأَرَائِكِ لَا يَرَونَ فِيهَا شَمسا وَلَا زَمهَرِيرا13Reclining therein upon couches, they shall see therein neither sun nor bitter cold.
وَدَانِيَةً عَلَيهِم ظِلَٰلُهَا وَذُلِّلَت قُطُوفُهَا تَذلِيلا14And near above them are its shades, and its clusters are made submissive, utterly.
وَيُطَافُ عَلَيهِم بِـَانِيَة مِّن فِضَّة وَأَكوَاب كَانَت قَوَارِيرَا15And there shall be passed around among them vessels of silver and goblets that are of crystal,
قَوَارِيرَاْ مِن فِضَّة قَدَّرُوهَا تَقدِيرا16crystal of silver, which they have measured with a due measure.
وَيُسقَونَ فِيهَا كَأسا كَانَ مِزَاجُهَا زَنجَبِيلًا17And therein they are given to drink a cup whose mixture is of ginger,
عَينا فِيهَا تُسَمَّىٰ سَلسَبِيلا18a fountain therein named Salsabil.512
وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيهِم وِلدَٰن مُّخَلَّدُونَ إِذَا رَأَيتَهُم حَسِبتَهُم لُؤلُؤا مَّنثُورا19And there shall wait upon them youths made eternal; when thou seest them, thou deemest them scattered pearls.
وَإِذَا رَأَيتَ ثَمَّ رَأَيتَ نَعِيما وَمُلكا كَبِيرًا20And when thou lookest, thou shalt see bliss and a great dominion.
عَٰلِيَهُم ثِيَابُ سُندُسٍ خُضر وَإِستَبرَق وَحُلُّواْ أَسَاوِرَ مِن فِضَّة وَسَقَىٰهُم رَبُّهُم شَرَابا طَهُورًا21Upon them are garments of fine green silk and brocade, and they are adorned with bracelets of silver; and their Lord giveth them to drink a pure draught.
إِنَّ هَٰذَا كَانَ لَكُم جَزَاء وَكَانَ سَعيُكُم مَّشكُورًا22"Verily, this is for you a recompense, and your striving is accepted."
إِنَّا نَحنُ نَزَّلنَا عَلَيكَ القُرءَانَ تَنزِيلا23Verily, We, even We, have sent down unto thee the Recitation, a sending down.513
فَاصبِر لِحُكمِ رَبِّكَ وَلَا تُطِع مِنهُم ءَاثِمًا أَو كَفُورا24So be patient for the judgement of thy Lord, and obey not from among them any sinner or ingrate.
وَاذكُرِ اسمَ رَبِّكَ بُكرَة وَأَصِيلا25And remember the name of thy Lord morning and evening,
وَمِنَ الَّيلِ فَاسجُد لَهُ وَسَبِّحهُ لَيلا طَوِيلًا26and in the night prostrate thyself before Him, and glorify Him a long night through.
إِنَّ هَـٰؤُلَاءِ يُحِبُّونَ العَاجِلَةَ وَيَذَرُونَ وَرَاءَهُم يَوما ثَقِيلا27Verily, these love the fleeting life and leave behind them a heavy Day.
نَّحنُ خَلَقنَٰهُم وَشَدَدنَا أَسرَهُم وَإِذَا شِئنَا بَدَّلنَا أَمثَٰلَهُم تَبدِيلًا28We created them and strengthened their frame; and when We will, We shall replace them with their like, a replacement.
إِنَّ هَٰذِهِ تَذكِرَة فَمَن شَاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ سَبِيلا29Verily, this is a Reminder; so let him who willeth take a path unto his Lord.
وَمَا تَشَاءُونَ إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ إِنَّ اللَّهَ كَانَ عَلِيمًا حَكِيما30And ye will not, unless God willeth. Verily, God is ever Knowing, Wise.
يُدخِلُ مَن يَشَاءُ فِي رَحمَتِهِ وَالظَّـٰلِمِينَ أَعَدَّ لَهُم عَذَابًا أَلِيمَا31He admitteth whom He willeth into His mercy; and the wrongdoers, He hath prepared for them a painful chastisement.

509 Verse 1: "Hath there come upon man () a span of time when he was a thing not remembered?" carries the sense of being social, visible, familiar — the opposite of -- (concealment). The Human is the manifest counterpart to the Spectres. This surah opens by confronting the Human with a time before hi...

510 Verse 3: "whether he be thankful () or whether he be ungrateful ()." The two possibilities for the human are framed as root opposites: (opening, gratitude, acknowledgment) versus (covering, ingratitude, concealment). The human's fundamental choice is between uncovering and covering — between mani...

511 Verse 8 ("they feed, for the love of Him, the indigent, the orphan, and the captive") connects to 69:34 and 74:44 where failure to feed the indigent is named as the sin that leads to the Fire. Feeding the deprived is the consistent ethical test across surahs.

512 Verse 18: "a fountain therein named Salsabil ()." This is a hapax legomenon — appearing only here. The word can be parsed as — "ask the way" — a fountain whose very name is an invitation to seek the path. resonance with -- (surrender, peace) and -- (way, path) makes the fountain's name a compress...

513 Verse 23: "Verily, We, even We, have sent down unto thee the Recitation (), a sending down." The emphatic "We, even We" ( ) and the cognate accusative (, "a sending down") together stress divine authorship. The Recitation is named here in a surah about the human — the Human receives the Recitatio...

Surah 77: Al-Mursalat — The Sent Ones

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالمُرسَلَٰتِ عُرفا1By those sent one after another514
فَالعَٰصِفَٰتِ عَصفا2and the violent blows515
وَالنَّـٰشِرَٰتِ نَشرا3and the scattering far and wide516
فَالفَٰرِقَٰتِ فَرقا4and the Measure of separation
فَالمُلقِيَٰتِ ذِكرًا5and the descendance of the Reminder
عُذرًا أَو نُذرًا6which holds for true or warns:
إِنَّمَا تُوعَدُونَ لَوَٰقِع7verily, what We promised ye shall pass.517
فَإِذَا النُّجُومُ طُمِسَت8So when the stars shall be extinguished518
وَإِذَا السَّمَاءُ فُرِجَت9and the heaven is riven asunder519
وَإِذَا الجِبَالُ نُسِفَت10and the mountains are blown away520
وَإِذَا الرُّسُلُ أُقِّتَت11and the messengers assemble:521
لِأَيِّ يَومٍ أُجِّلَت12For what day hath been set?
لِيَومِ الفَصلِ13For the Day of Judgement.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا يَومُ الفَصلِ14And what will tell the Day of Judgement?
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ15Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
أَلَم نُهلِكِ الأَوَّلِينَ16Did We not destroy the former,522
ثُمَّ نُتبِعُهُمُ الأخِرِينَ17then let the latter follow?523
كَذَٰلِكَ نَفعَلُ بِالمُجرِمِينَ18Thus We deal with the wicked.
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ19Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
أَلَم نَخلُقكُّم مِّن مَّاء مَّهِين20Did We not create you from a despicable drop524
فَجَعَلنَٰهُ فِي قَرَار مَّكِينٍ21which we put in a safe abode525
إِلَىٰ قَدَر مَّعلُوم22for a fixed time?526
فَقَدَرنَا فَنِعمَ القَٰدِرُونَ23Thus We measure, and We measure best.527
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ24Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
أَلَم نَجعَلِ الأَرضَ كِفَاتًا25Did We not make the Earth receiving528
أَحيَاء وَأَموَٰتا26both living and dead?
وَجَعَلنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِيَ شَٰمِخَٰت وَأَسقَينَٰكُم مَّاء فُرَاتا27And We made therein firm and lofty mountains, and We gave ye sweet water to drink.529
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ28Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
انطَلِقُواْ إِلَىٰ مَا كُنتُم بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ29Go in to what ye used to deny.530
انطَلِقُواْ إِلَىٰ ظِلّ ذِي ثَلَٰثِ شُعَب30Go in to the three-pillared shadow.531
لَّا ظَلِيل وَلَا يُغنِي مِنَ اللَّهَبِ31Not a fresh shadow, not availing against the flame.532
إِنَّهَا تَرمِي بِشَرَر كَالقَصرِ32Verily, it sendeth out sparks like a fortress,533
كَأَنَّهُ جِمَٰلَت صُفر33like yellow camels.534
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ34Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
هَٰذَا يَومُ لَا يَنطِقُونَ35This day they will not speak535
وَلَا يُؤذَنُ لَهُم فَيَعتَذِرُونَ36and they will not be permitted to excuse themselves.
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ37Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
هَٰذَا يَومُ الفَصلِ جَمَعنَٰكُم وَالأَوَّلِينَ38This is the Day of Judgement: we have collected ye and the former.536
فَإِن كَانَ لَكُم كَيد فَكِيدُونِ39So if ye have a plan, use it against Me.537
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ40Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
إِنَّ المُتَّقِينَ فِي ظِلَٰل وَعُيُون41Indeed, the righteous are in shades and spring538
وَفَوَٰكِهَ مِمَّا يَشتَهُونَ42and fruits from what they desire.
كُلُواْ وَاشرَبُواْ هَنِيـَا بِمَا كُنتُم تَعمَلُونَ43Eat and drink happily for what ye used to do.539
إِنَّا كَذَٰلِكَ نَجزِي المُحسِنِينَ44Verily, thus we recompense those who do good.540
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ45Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
كُلُواْ وَتَمَتَّعُواْ قَلِيلًا إِنَّكُم مُّجرِمُونَ46Eat and enjoy some, verily ye are wicked.541
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ47Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ اركَعُواْ لَا يَركَعُونَ48And when it is said, bow, they bow not.542
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ49Woe, that Day, to the deniers.
فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثِ بَعدَهُ يُؤمِنُونَ50Then in what later message do they believe?543

514 Verses 1–7: The Oath Cluster The surah opens with a cascade of participles, all feminine plural, describing agents of divine action. The identity of these agents is deliberately ambiguous:

515 v.2 "violent blows" — (fal-ʿāṣifāt ʿaṣfā). -- (ʿ-ṣ-f) means to blow violently, to storm. The cognate accusative () intensifies — blowing a violent blow.

516 v.3 "scattering far and wide" — (wan-nāshirāt nashrā). -- (n-sh-r) means to spread, scatter, publish, resurrect. It carries both the sense of wind scattering and of spreading/publishing a message.

517 v.7 "what We promised ye shall pass" — — literally "what ye are promised is surely coming to pass." The oath cluster resolves here: all those cosmic agents swear to the certainty of the promise.

518 Verse 8: "estinguished" Note: this appears to be a typo for "extinguished." (ṭumisat) means to be blotted out, obliterated. The stars losing their light is a standard Qur'anic sign of the end times (cf. 81:2 , "folded up").

519 Verse 9: "riven asunder" (furijat) — from -- (f-r-j), to open, to cleave, to relieve. The heaven being split open. "Riven asunder" has good KJV resonance (cf. Mark 1:10, "he saw the heavens rent asunder").

520 Verse 10: "blown away" (nusifat) — from -- (n-s-f), to blow away, to winnow, to demolish. The mountains reduced to nothing. Cf. 20:105 where the same root describes mountains being scattered as dust.

521 v.11 "messengers assemble" — (uqqitat) means "are appointed/timed." The messengers (, ar-rusul) are given their appointed time. This is the Day when all prophets and messengers are gathered.

522 Verses 16–19: Historical Warning

523 v.17 "the latter" — (al-ākhirīn), those who come after. The warning: destruction is not just historical but ongoing — each generation of the wicked follows the former into ruin.

524 Verses 20–24: Creation from Water

525 v.21 "safe abode" — (qarārin makīn), a firm/secure resting place — the womb.

526 v.22 "fixed time" — (qadarin maʿlūm), a known measure. The gestation period as divine decree.

527 v.23 "We measure, and We measure best" — . -- (q-d-r) means to measure, decree, have power over. The verse plays on both senses: God measures (decrees the creation) and is the best of those who measure (most powerful). Good rendering that preserves the double meaning.

528 v.25 "receiving" for (kifāt) — the root -- (k-f-t) means to gather, collect, contain. Most translate as "a container" or "a place that draws together." "Receiving" captures the earth as actively taking in — receiving the living on its surface and the dead in its soil.

529 v.27 "firm and lofty mountains" — (rawāsiya shāmikhāt). from -- (r-s-w), to be firm, anchored. from -- (sh-m-kh), to be lofty, proud. Mountains as anchors and watchtowers.

530 Verses 29–34: The Three-Pillared Shadow This is one of the most vivid hellfire passages:

531 v.30 "three-pillared shadow" — (ẓillin dhī thalāthi shuʿab). (shuʿab) means branches, divisions, prongs. The shadow of hellfire splits into three — a dark parody of shade, offering no comfort. Some commentators connect the three branches to the three categories of people on the Day (cf. 56:7–10).

532 v.31 "not a fresh shadow" — (lā ẓalīl), not shading, not cool. , not availing against the flame. Anti-paradise: where the righteous have shade and springs (v.41), the wicked have shadow that does not shade.

533 v.32 "sparks like a fortress" — (bi-shararin kal-qaṣr). (qaṣr) can mean fortress, palace, or large log. The sparks are the size of buildings — a terrifying image.

534 v.33 "yellow camels" — (jimālatun ṣufr). The sparks are also compared to yellow (or tawny) camels — a desert image of thundering, unstoppable force.

535 Verses 35–40: Silence of the Wicked

536 v.38 "we have collected ye and the former" — . All generations assembled together — time collapsed.

537 v.39 "if ye have a plan, use it against Me" — . A devastating divine challenge. (kayd) means stratagem, plot — the same word used for human scheming throughout the Qur'an (e.g., 12:5, Yusuf's brothers). Here God turns it back: scheme against Me, if you can.

538 v.41 "shades and springs" — (ẓilāl wa-ʿuyūn). In contrast to the three-pillared shadow that gives no shade, the righteous have true shade. The parallel structure is deliberate.

539 v.43 "Eat and drink happily" — . (hanī'an) — pleasantly, without consequence. The food and drink of paradise cause no ill effect.

540 v.44 "those who do good" — (al-muḥsinīn), from the root -- (ḥ-s-n), to do good, to beautify. (iḥsān) in Islamic theology is the highest level of worship — to worship as if you see God.

541 Verses 46–47: Eating and Wickedness

542 v.48 "bow, they bow not" — . -- (r-k-ʿ) — to bow in prayer. Those who refuse the physical act of submission (rukūʿ) embody their spiritual refusal.

543 v.50 "what later message" — . (ḥadīth) means speech, narrative, message. The surah closes with a devastating question: if not this message (the Qur'an), then what message will they believe? This resonates with the Bahá'í understanding of progressive revelation — each new (message) from God builds...

Surah 78: An-Naba — The Tidings

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

عَمَّ يَتَسَاءَلُونَ1Of what do they question one another?
عَنِ النَّبَإِ العَظِيمِ2Of the mighty tidings544
الَّذِي هُم فِيهِ مُختَلِفُونَ3concerning which they are in dispute.
كَلَّا سَيَعلَمُونَ4Nay! They shall come to know.
ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَيَعلَمُونَ5Then nay! They shall come to know.
أَلَم نَجعَلِ الأَرضَ مِهَٰدا6Did We not make the earth a cradle
وَالجِبَالَ أَوتَادا7and the mountains stakes?
وَخَلَقنَٰكُم أَزوَٰجا8And We created you in pairs
وَجَعَلنَا نَومَكُم سُبَاتا9and We made your sleep a repose
وَجَعَلنَا الَّيلَ لِبَاسا10and We made the night a covering
وَجَعَلنَا النَّهَارَ مَعَاشا11and We made the day a livelihood.
وَبَنَينَا فَوقَكُم سَبعا شِدَادا12And We built above you seven firm ones
وَجَعَلنَا سِرَاجا وَهَّاجا13and We made a blazing lamp
وَأَنزَلنَا مِنَ المُعصِرَٰتِ مَاء ثَجَّاجا14and We sent down from the rain-clouds water pouring forth
لِّنُخرِجَ بِهِ حَبّا وَنَبَاتا15that We might bring forth thereby grain and vegetation
وَجَنَّـٰتٍ أَلفَافًا16and gardens dense and intertwined.
إِنَّ يَومَ الفَصلِ كَانَ مِيقَٰتا17Verily, the Day of Separation hath been appointed,545
يَومَ يُنفَخُ فِي الصُّورِ فَتَأتُونَ أَفوَاجا18the Day when the trumpet is blown and ye come in multitudes
وَفُتِحَتِ السَّمَاءُ فَكَانَت أَبوَٰبا19and the heaven is opened and becometh gates
وَسُيِّرَتِ الجِبَالُ فَكَانَت سَرَابًا20and the mountains are set moving and become a mirage.
إِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ كَانَت مِرصَادا21Verily, Hell hath been lying in ambush,
لِّلطَّـٰغِينَ مَـَابا22for the transgressors a resort,
لَّـٰبِثِينَ فِيهَا أَحقَابا23abiding therein for ages,
لَّا يَذُوقُونَ فِيهَا بَردا وَلَا شَرَابًا24tasting therein neither coolness nor drink,
إِلَّا حَمِيما وَغَسَّاقا25save boiling water and a dark, foul discharge —
جَزَاء وِفَاقًا26a fitting recompense.
إِنَّهُم كَانُواْ لَا يَرجُونَ حِسَابا27Verily, they were not expecting a reckoning
وَكَذَّبُواْ بِـَايَٰتِنَا كِذَّابا28and they denied Our signs utterly.
وَكُلَّ شَيءٍ أَحصَينَٰهُ كِتَٰبا29And every thing have We numbered in a Book.
فَذُوقُواْ فَلَن نَّزِيدَكُم إِلَّا عَذَابًا30So taste! For We shall not increase you save in torment.
إِنَّ لِلمُتَّقِينَ مَفَازًا31Verily, for the God-fearing there is a triumph:
حَدَائِقَ وَأَعنَٰبا32gardens and vineyards
وَكَوَاعِبَ أَترَابا33and companions well-matched
وَكَأسا دِهَاقا34and a cup overflowing.
لَّا يَسمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغوا وَلَا كِذَّـٰبا35They shall hear therein neither idle speech nor falsehood —
جَزَاء مِّن رَّبِّكَ عَطَاءً حِسَابا36a recompense from thy Lord, a gift in full measure,
رَّبِّ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَمَا بَينَهُمَا الرَّحمَٰنِ لَا يَملِكُونَ مِنهُ خِطَابا37Lord of the heavens and the earth and what is between them, the All-Merciful; they possess not from Him any address.
يَومَ يَقُومُ الرُّوحُ وَالمَلَـٰئِكَةُ صَفّا لَّا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَن أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحمَٰنُ وَقَالَ صَوَابا38The Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks, they shall not speak, save he whom the All-Merciful hath permitted, and he speaketh aright.546
ذَٰلِكَ اليَومُ الحَقُّ فَمَن شَاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِ مَـَابًا39That is the Day of Truth; so whoso willeth, let him take a way of return unto his Lord.
إِنَّا أَنذَرنَٰكُم عَذَابا قَرِيبا يَومَ يَنظُرُ المَرءُ مَا قَدَّمَت يَدَاهُ وَيَقُولُ الكَافِرُ يَٰلَيتَنِي كُنتُ تُرَٰبَا40Verily, We have warned you of a torment near at hand, the Day when a man shall behold what his hands have sent forth, and the one who concealed shall say: "Would that I were dust!"547

544 (an-naba') shares its root with (nabī, prophet). The "mighty tidings" (v.2) they dispute is not mere news but prophetic announcement. Every prophet brings a naba' — the question "of what do they question one another?" is really: which prophecy are you arguing about?

545 (al-faṣl, v.17) — the "Day of Separation" — shares its root with the decisive separation in 77:13-14. The Day does not merely judge; it separates truth from falsehood as a weaver separates threads.

546 v.38 "the Spirit and the angels" ↔ 97:4: the Spirit descends on the Night of Power and stands in rank on the Day of Separation — the same agent bookends revelation and judgement.

547 Verse 40: "the one who covered over shall say: Would that I were dust!" The surah ends with the coverer wishing to become the very earth that covers — a bitter irony. He who covered truth wishes to be covered himself.

Surah 79: An-Nazi'at — The Pluckers

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالنَّـٰزِعَٰتِ غَرقا1By those who pluck out violently
وَالنَّـٰشِطَٰتِ نَشطا2and those who draw forth gently
وَالسَّـٰبِحَٰتِ سَبحا3and those who glide swimming
فَالسَّـٰبِقَٰتِ سَبقا4then those who race ahead in racing
فَالمُدَبِّرَٰتِ أَمرا5then those who govern the affair:
يَومَ تَرجُفُ الرَّاجِفَةُ6the Day when the quaking quaketh
تَتبَعُهَا الرَّادِفَةُ7followed by the successor —
قُلُوب يَومَئِذ وَاجِفَةٌ8hearts that Day shall be trembling,
أَبصَٰرُهَا خَٰشِعَة9their eyes cast down.
يَقُولُونَ أَءِنَّا لَمَردُودُونَ فِي الحَافِرَةِ10They say: "Shall we indeed be returned to the former state?
أَءِذَا كُنَّا عِظَٰما نَّخِرَة11What, when we are crumbled bones?"
قَالُواْ تِلكَ إِذا كَرَّةٌ خَاسِرَة12They say: "That then would be a losing return!"
فَإِنَّمَا هِيَ زَجرَة وَٰحِدَة13But it shall be only a single blast
فَإِذَا هُم بِالسَّاهِرَةِ14and behold, they are upon the open plain.
هَل أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ مُوسَىٰ15Hath the story of Moses come to thee?
إِذ نَادَىٰهُ رَبُّهُ بِالوَادِ المُقَدَّسِ طُوًى16When his Lord called unto him in the holy valley of Tuwa:
اذهَب إِلَىٰ فِرعَونَ إِنَّهُ طَغَىٰ17"Go unto Pharaoh; verily, he hath transgressed.
فَقُل هَل لَّكَ إِلَىٰ أَن تَزَكَّىٰ18And say: 'Hast thou the will to purify thyself?
وَأَهدِيَكَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَتَخشَىٰ19And I shall guide thee unto thy Lord, that thou mayest fear.'"
فَأَرَىٰهُ الأيَةَ الكُبرَىٰ20Then he showed him the greatest sign,
فَكَذَّبَ وَعَصَىٰ21but he denied and disobeyed,548
ثُمَّ أَدبَرَ يَسعَىٰ22then he turned away, hastening,
فَحَشَرَ فَنَادَىٰ23and he gathered and proclaimed
فَقَالَ أَنَا رَبُّكُمُ الأَعلَىٰ24and said: "I am your lord, the most high!"549
فَأَخَذَهُ اللَّهُ نَكَالَ الأخِرَةِ وَالأُولَىٰ25So God seized him with the punishment of the Hereafter and the former.
إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبرَة لِّمَن يَخشَىٰ26Verily, in that is a lesson for him who feareth.
ءَأَنتُم أَشَدُّ خَلقًا أَمِ السَّمَاءُ بَنَىٰهَا27Are ye harder to create or the heaven? He built it.
رَفَعَ سَمكَهَا فَسَوَّىٰهَا28He raised its canopy and fashioned it,
وَأَغطَشَ لَيلَهَا وَأَخرَجَ ضُحَىٰهَا29and He darkened its night and brought forth its forenoon,
وَالأَرضَ بَعدَ ذَٰلِكَ دَحَىٰهَا30and the earth — after that He spread it out.
أَخرَجَ مِنهَا مَاءَهَا وَمَرعَىٰهَا31He brought forth from it its water and its pasture
وَالجِبَالَ أَرسَىٰهَا32and the mountains He set firm —
مَتَٰعا لَّكُم وَلِأَنعَٰمِكُم33a provision for you and for your cattle.
فَإِذَا جَاءَتِ الطَّامَّةُ الكُبرَىٰ34Then, when the Great Calamity cometh,
يَومَ يَتَذَكَّرُ الإِنسَٰنُ مَا سَعَىٰ35the Day when the human remembereth what he strove for
وَبُرِّزَتِ الجَحِيمُ لِمَن يَرَىٰ36and the Blaze is brought forth for him who seeth —
فَأَمَّا مَن طَغَىٰ37then as for him who transgressed
وَءَاثَرَ الحَيَوٰةَ الدُّنيَا38and preferred the life of this world,
فَإِنَّ الجَحِيمَ هِيَ المَأوَىٰ39verily, the Blaze shall be his refuge.
وَأَمَّا مَن خَافَ مَقَامَ رَبِّهِ وَنَهَى النَّفسَ عَنِ الهَوَىٰ40And as for him who feared the station of his Lord and forbade the soul its caprice,
فَإِنَّ الجَنَّةَ هِيَ المَأوَىٰ41verily, the Garden shall be his refuge.550
يَسـَلُونَكَ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ أَيَّانَ مُرسَىٰهَا42They ask thee of the Hour: "When is its arrival?"
فِيمَ أَنتَ مِن ذِكرَىٰهَا43What part hast thou in the mention thereof?
إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ مُنتَهَىٰهَا44Unto thy Lord is its final end.
إِنَّمَا أَنتَ مُنذِرُ مَن يَخشَىٰهَا45Thou art but a warner unto him who feareth it.
كَأَنَّهُم يَومَ يَرَونَهَا لَم يَلبَثُواْ إِلَّا عَشِيَّةً أَو ضُحَىٰهَا46As though, the Day they see it, they had not tarried save an evening or its forenoon.

548 Verse 18: Moses asks Pharaoh "Hast thou the will to purify thyself?" (tazakkā) shares the root of (zakāt, purifying alms). Pharaoh is offered purification — not threatened first but invited. His refusal in v.21 ("he denied and disobeyed") makes the punishment just.

549 v.24 Pharaoh's "I am your lord, the most high" ↔ 87:1 "Glorify the name of thy Lord, the Most High": Pharaoh claims the title that belongs to God alone — Al-A'la responds with the true Most High.

550 Verse 41: "the Garden shall be his refuge" — the term (al-janna) as the hidden place. Paired with v.40's "feared the station of his Lord," the concealed garden is refuge for those who did not conceal themselves from God's gaze.

Surah 80: 'Abasa — He Frowned

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

عَبَسَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ1He frowned and turned away
أَن جَاءَهُ الأَعمَىٰ2because the blind man came to him.
وَمَا يُدرِيكَ لَعَلَّهُ يَزَّكَّىٰ3And what would make thee know? Perhaps he would purify himself551
أَو يَذَّكَّرُ فَتَنفَعَهُ الذِّكرَىٰ4or be reminded, and the reminder profit him.
أَمَّا مَنِ استَغنَىٰ5As for him who deemeth himself self-sufficient,
فَأَنتَ لَهُ تَصَدَّىٰ6to him thou dost attend,
وَمَا عَلَيكَ أَلَّا يَزَّكَّىٰ7though it is not upon thee if he purify not himself.
وَأَمَّا مَن جَاءَكَ يَسعَىٰ8But as for him who came to thee striving
وَهُوَ يَخشَىٰ9and he feareth —
فَأَنتَ عَنهُ تَلَهَّىٰ10from him thou art distracted.
كَلَّا إِنَّهَا تَذكِرَة11Nay! Verily, it is a reminder,
فَمَن شَاءَ ذَكَرَهُ12so let whoso willeth heed it,
فِي صُحُف مُّكَرَّمَة13in scrolls honoured,
مَّرفُوعَة مُّطَهَّرَةِ14exalted, purified,
بِأَيدِي سَفَرَة15by the hands of scribes
كِرَامِ بَرَرَة16noble, righteous.
قُتِلَ الإِنسَٰنُ مَا أَكفَرَهُ17Perish the human! How ungrateful he is!552
مِن أَيِّ شَيءٍ خَلَقَهُ18From what thing did He create him?
مِن نُّطفَةٍ خَلَقَهُ فَقَدَّرَهُ19From a drop He created him and measured him,
ثُمَّ السَّبِيلَ يَسَّرَهُ20then the way He made easy for him,
ثُمَّ أَمَاتَهُ فَأَقبَرَهُ21then He caused him to die and buried him,
ثُمَّ إِذَا شَاءَ أَنشَرَهُ22then, when He willeth, He shall raise him.
كَلَّا لَمَّا يَقضِ مَا أَمَرَهُ23Nay! He hath not yet fulfilled what He commanded him.
فَليَنظُرِ الإِنسَٰنُ إِلَىٰ طَعَامِهِ24So let the human look at his food:
أَنَّا صَبَبنَا المَاءَ صَبّا25that We poured down water in abundance,
ثُمَّ شَقَقنَا الأَرضَ شَقّا26then We split the earth asunder,
فَأَنبَتنَا فِيهَا حَبّا27and We caused to grow therein grain
وَعِنَبا وَقَضبا28and grapes and fresh herbage
وَزَيتُونا وَنَخلا29and olives and palms
وَحَدَائِقَ غُلبا30and gardens dense
وَفَٰكِهَة وَأَبّا31and fruit and fodder —
مَّتَٰعا لَّكُم وَلِأَنعَٰمِكُم32a provision for you and for your cattle.
فَإِذَا جَاءَتِ الصَّاخَّةُ33Then, when the Deafening Blast cometh,
يَومَ يَفِرُّ المَرءُ مِن أَخِيهِ34the Day when a man shall flee from his brother
وَأُمِّهِ وَأَبِيهِ35and his mother and his father
وَصَٰحِبَتِهِ وَبَنِيهِ36and his spouse and his sons —
لِكُلِّ امرِي مِّنهُم يَومَئِذ شَأن يُغنِيهِ37every man among them that Day shall have an affair to suffice him.
وُجُوه يَومَئِذ مُّسفِرَة38Faces that Day shall be radiant,
ضَاحِكَة مُّستَبشِرَة39laughing, rejoicing,
وَوُجُوه يَومَئِذٍ عَلَيهَا غَبَرَة40and faces that Day shall have dust upon them,
تَرهَقُهَا قَتَرَةٌ41covered in darkness —553
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُمُ الكَفَرَةُ الفَجَرَةُ42those are the ones who concealed, the wicked.

551 (yazzakkā, v.3) and (v.14, of the scrolls) use the same purification root. The blind man might "purify himself" through the message; the scrolls themselves are "purified." The medium and the seeker share the same quality — purity meets purity.

552 v.17 "Perish the human! How ungrateful he is!" ↔ 100:6 "the human is ungrateful unto his Lord": both surahs diagnose the same disease — human ingratitude despite being shown the path.

553 Verse 42: "those are the ones who covered over, the wicked." (al-kafara) closes the surah. Faces covered in darkness (v.41) are those who cover over — the outer darkness mirrors the inner act of concealment.

Surah 81: At-Takwir — The Folding Up

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا الشَّمسُ كُوِّرَت1When the sun is folded up554
وَإِذَا النُّجُومُ انكَدَرَت2and when the stars fall dim
وَإِذَا الجِبَالُ سُيِّرَت3and when the mountains are set moving
وَإِذَا العِشَارُ عُطِّلَت4and when the ten-month pregnant camels are abandoned
وَإِذَا الوُحُوشُ حُشِرَت5and when the wild beasts are gathered
وَإِذَا البِحَارُ سُجِّرَت6and when the seas are set afire
وَإِذَا النُّفُوسُ زُوِّجَت7and when the souls are paired
وَإِذَا المَوءُدَةُ سُئِلَت8and when the girl buried alive is asked
بِأَيِّ ذَنب قُتِلَت9for what sin she was slain
وَإِذَا الصُّحُفُ نُشِرَت10and when the scrolls are spread open
وَإِذَا السَّمَاءُ كُشِطَت11and when the heaven is stripped away
وَإِذَا الجَحِيمُ سُعِّرَت12and when the Blaze is kindled
وَإِذَا الجَنَّةُ أُزلِفَت13and when the Garden is brought nigh —
عَلِمَت نَفس مَّا أَحضَرَت14a soul shall know what it hath brought forth.
فَلَا أُقسِمُ بِالخُنَّسِ15Nay! I swear by the retreating ones,555
الجَوَارِ الكُنَّسِ16the runners, the concealers,
وَالَّيلِ إِذَا عَسعَسَ17and by the night when it departeth
وَالصُّبحِ إِذَا تَنَفَّسَ18and by the dawn when it breatheth —
إِنَّهُ لَقَولُ رَسُول كَرِيم19verily, it is the word of a noble messenger,
ذِي قُوَّةٍ عِندَ ذِي العَرشِ مَكِين20possessed of power, secure with the Lord of the Throne,
مُّطَاع ثَمَّ أَمِين21obeyed there, trustworthy.
وَمَا صَاحِبُكُم بِمَجنُون22And your companion is not possessed.
وَلَقَد رَءَاهُ بِالأُفُقِ المُبِينِ23And verily, he saw him on the clear horizon,
وَمَا هُوَ عَلَى الغَيبِ بِضَنِين24and he is not withholding of the Unseen,
وَمَا هُوَ بِقَولِ شَيطَٰن رَّجِيم25and it is not the word of an accursed devil.
فَأَينَ تَذهَبُونَ26So whither do ye go?
إِن هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكر لِّلعَٰلَمِينَ27It is naught but a Reminder unto the worlds,556
لِمَن شَاءَ مِنكُم أَن يَستَقِيمَ28for whoso among you willeth to go straight.
وَمَا تَشَاءُونَ إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ رَبُّ العَٰلَمِينَ29And ye will not, unless God willeth, the Lord of the worlds.

554 (kuwwirat, v.1) describes the sun being folded up like a turban being wound. suggests wrapping, coiling — the sun's light is not destroyed but folded away. The image is of revelation's cosmic bookend: what was unfurled at creation is wound back.

555 (al-khunnas, v.15) — "the retreating ones" — describes stars or planets that appear to move backward. The oath "by the retreating ones, the runners, the concealers" (vv.15-16) layers three roots: retreat (kh-n-s), running (j-r-y), and concealment (k-n-s). Hidden celestial movements swear to the t...

556 v.27 "a Reminder unto the worlds" ↔ 55:1-2: the Recitation as universal reminder — not addressed to one people but to all worlds.

Surah 82: Al-Infitar — The Cleaving

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا السَّمَاءُ انفَطَرَت1When the heaven is cleft asunder557
وَإِذَا الكَوَاكِبُ انتَثَرَت2and when the stars are scattered
وَإِذَا البِحَارُ فُجِّرَت3and when the seas are burst forth
وَإِذَا القُبُورُ بُعثِرَت4and when the graves are overturned —
عَلِمَت نَفس مَّا قَدَّمَت وَأَخَّرَت5a soul shall know what it sent forth and what it held back.
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الإِنسَٰنُ مَا غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الكَرِيمِ6O thou human! What hath deluded thee concerning thy Lord, the Generous,558
الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّىٰكَ فَعَدَلَكَ7Who created thee, then fashioned thee, then proportioned thee?559
فِي أَيِّ صُورَة مَّا شَاءَ رَكَّبَكَ8In whatever form He willed, He assembled thee.
كَلَّا بَل تُكَذِّبُونَ بِالدِّينِ9Nay! But ye deny the Judgement,
وَإِنَّ عَلَيكُم لَحَٰفِظِينَ10and verily, over you are guardians,
كِرَاما كَٰتِبِينَ11noble ones, recording,
يَعلَمُونَ مَا تَفعَلُونَ12who know what ye do.
إِنَّ الأَبرَارَ لَفِي نَعِيم13Verily, the righteous shall be in bliss
وَإِنَّ الفُجَّارَ لَفِي جَحِيم14and verily, the wicked shall be in a blaze.
يَصلَونَهَا يَومَ الدِّينِ15They shall enter it on the Day of Judgement
وَمَا هُم عَنهَا بِغَائِبِينَ16and they shall not be absent therefrom.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا يَومُ الدِّينِ17And what shall make thee know what the Day of Judgement is?
ثُمَّ مَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا يَومُ الدِّينِ18Then what shall make thee know what the Day of Judgement is?
يَومَ لَا تَملِكُ نَفس لِّنَفس شَيـا وَالأَمرُ يَومَئِذ لِّلَّهِ19The Day when no soul shall avail another soul in aught, and the command that Day shall be God's.560

557 (infaṭarat, v.1) — "cleft asunder" — shares its root with (fiṭra, primordial nature) and (fāṭir, Originator). The cleaving of heaven on the Day of Judgement echoes the original cleaving of creation. What God split open to begin, He splits open again to renew.

558 Verse 6: "What hath deluded thee concerning thy Lord, the Generous?" (gharraka) — the root of (ghurūr, delusion). The question is devastating: God is (al-Karīm, the Generous) — what possible delusion makes you turn from generosity itself?

559 v.7 "created thee, fashioned thee, proportioned thee" ↔ 87:2-3: both surahs use the same three-step creation sequence — Al-A'la adds "measured, then guided."

560 v.19 "the command that Day shall be God's" ↔ 55:29 "each day He is upon some task": divine sovereignty operates daily but is revealed fully on the Day of Judgement.

Surah 83: Al-Mutaffifin — The Defrauders

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَيل لِّلمُطَفِّفِينَ1Woe unto the defrauders,561
الَّذِينَ إِذَا اكتَالُواْ عَلَى النَّاسِ يَستَوفُونَ2who, when they take measure from the people, take full measure,
وَإِذَا كَالُوهُم أَو وَّزَنُوهُم يُخسِرُونَ3but when they measure for them or weigh for them, give less.
أَلَا يَظُنُّ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ أَنَّهُم مَّبعُوثُونَ4Do those not reckon that they shall be raised up
لِيَومٍ عَظِيم5for a mighty Day,
يَومَ يَقُومُ النَّاسُ لِرَبِّ العَٰلَمِينَ6the Day when the people shall stand before the Lord of the worlds?
كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ الفُجَّارِ لَفِي سِجِّين7Nay! Verily, the record of the wicked is in Sijjin.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا سِجِّين8And what shall make thee know what Sijjin is?
كِتَٰب مَّرقُوم9A book inscribed.
وَيل يَومَئِذ لِّلمُكَذِّبِينَ10Woe that Day unto the deniers,
الَّذِينَ يُكَذِّبُونَ بِيَومِ الدِّينِ11who deny the Day of Judgement.
وَمَا يُكَذِّبُ بِهِ إِلَّا كُلُّ مُعتَدٍ أَثِيمٍ12And none denieth it save every sinful transgressor
إِذَا تُتلَىٰ عَلَيهِ ءَايَٰتُنَا قَالَ أَسَٰطِيرُ الأَوَّلِينَ13who, when Our signs are recited unto him, saith: "Tales of the ancients!"
كَلَّا بَل رَانَ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِم مَّا كَانُواْ يَكسِبُونَ14Nay! But what they earned hath rusted upon their hearts.562
كَلَّا إِنَّهُم عَن رَّبِّهِم يَومَئِذ لَّمَحجُوبُونَ15Nay! Verily, that Day they shall be veiled from their Lord.563
ثُمَّ إِنَّهُم لَصَالُواْ الجَحِيمِ16Then verily, they shall enter the Blaze.
ثُمَّ يُقَالُ هَٰذَا الَّذِي كُنتُم بِهِ تُكَذِّبُونَ17Then it shall be said: "This is that which ye used to deny."
كَلَّا إِنَّ كِتَٰبَ الأَبرَارِ لَفِي عِلِّيِّينَ18Nay! Verily, the record of the righteous is in 'Illiyyin.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا عِلِّيُّونَ19And what shall make thee know what 'Illiyyin is?
كِتَٰب مَّرقُوم20A book inscribed,
يَشهَدُهُ المُقَرَّبُونَ21witnessed by those brought near.
إِنَّ الأَبرَارَ لَفِي نَعِيمٍ22Verily, the righteous shall be in bliss,
عَلَى الأَرَائِكِ يَنظُرُونَ23upon couches, gazing.
تَعرِفُ فِي وُجُوهِهِم نَضرَةَ النَّعِيمِ24Thou shalt recognize in their faces the radiance of bliss.
يُسقَونَ مِن رَّحِيق مَّختُومٍ25They are given to drink of a sealed nectar
خِتَٰمُهُ مِسك وَفِي ذَٰلِكَ فَليَتَنَافَسِ المُتَنَٰفِسُونَ26whose seal is musk — and for that let the strivers strive —
وَمِزَاجُهُ مِن تَسنِيمٍ27and its mixture is of Tasnim,
عَينا يَشرَبُ بِهَا المُقَرَّبُونَ28a spring from which those brought near do drink.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ أَجرَمُواْ كَانُواْ مِنَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ يَضحَكُونَ29Verily, those who sinned used to laugh at those who believed,
وَإِذَا مَرُّواْ بِهِم يَتَغَامَزُونَ30and when they passed by them, they would wink at one another,
وَإِذَا انقَلَبُواْ إِلَىٰ أَهلِهِمُ انقَلَبُواْ فَكِهِينَ31and when they returned to their folk, they returned jesting,
وَإِذَا رَأَوهُم قَالُواْ إِنَّ هَـٰؤُلَاءِ لَضَالُّونَ32and when they saw them, they said: "Verily, these are astray!"
وَمَا أُرسِلُواْ عَلَيهِم حَٰفِظِينَ33Yet they were not sent as guardians over them.
فَاليَومَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ مِنَ الكُفَّارِ يَضحَكُونَ34So this Day those who believed shall laugh at those who concealed,
عَلَى الأَرَائِكِ يَنظُرُونَ35upon couches, gazing.
هَل ثُوِّبَ الكُفَّارُ مَا كَانُواْ يَفعَلُونَ36Have those who concealed been recompensed for what they used to do?

561 (al-muṭaffifīn, v.1) — defrauders who take full but give less. means to skim, to diminish. This surah places economic injustice as the gateway sin: cheat the scales and the heart rusts, the heart rusts and you are veiled from God.

562 v.14 rust on hearts ↔ 2:7 "God hath sealed their hearts": two mechanisms of spiritual blindness — rust (gradual accumulation) and seal (divine response to persistent covering).

563 Verse 14: "what they earned hath rusted upon their hearts." (rāna) describes rust or tarnish accumulating on a surface. This is a covering parallel to -- (k-f-r): where kafara covers truth deliberately, rāna covers the heart through accumulated sin. The heart becomes opaque — and v.15 follows: "t...

Surah 84: Al-Inshiqaq — The Splitting

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا السَّمَاءُ انشَقَّت1When the heaven is split asunder564
وَأَذِنَت لِرَبِّهَا وَحُقَّت2and giveth ear unto its Lord, as it must,
وَإِذَا الأَرضُ مُدَّت3and when the earth is stretched out
وَأَلقَت مَا فِيهَا وَتَخَلَّت4and casteth forth what is in it and is emptied
وَأَذِنَت لِرَبِّهَا وَحُقَّت5and giveth ear unto its Lord, as it must —
يَـٰأَيُّهَا الإِنسَٰنُ إِنَّكَ كَادِحٌ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَدحا فَمُلَٰقِيهِ6O thou human! Verily, thou art labouring toward thy Lord, a hard labour, and thou shalt meet Him.565
فَأَمَّا مَن أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُ بِيَمِينِهِ7Then as for him who is given his book in his right hand,
فَسَوفَ يُحَاسَبُ حِسَابا يَسِيرا8he shall be reckoned with an easy reckoning
وَيَنقَلِبُ إِلَىٰ أَهلِهِ مَسرُورا9and shall return unto his folk rejoicing.
وَأَمَّا مَن أُوتِيَ كِتَٰبَهُ وَرَاءَ ظَهرِهِ10But as for him who is given his book behind his back,
فَسَوفَ يَدعُواْ ثُبُورا11he shall cry out for destruction
وَيَصلَىٰ سَعِيرًا12and shall enter the Blaze.
إِنَّهُ كَانَ فِي أَهلِهِ مَسرُورًا13Verily, he was among his folk rejoicing;
إِنَّهُ ظَنَّ أَن لَّن يَحُورَ14verily, he supposed he would never return.
بَلَىٰ إِنَّ رَبَّهُ كَانَ بِهِ بَصِيرا15Nay! Verily, his Lord was ever watchful of him.
فَلَا أُقسِمُ بِالشَّفَقِ16Nay! I swear by the twilight glow
وَالَّيلِ وَمَا وَسَقَ17and by the night and what it gathereth
وَالقَمَرِ إِذَا اتَّسَقَ18and by the moon when it is full —
لَتَركَبُنَّ طَبَقًا عَن طَبَق19ye shall surely ride stage upon stage.566
فَمَا لَهُم لَا يُؤمِنُونَ20So what aileth them that they believe not?
وَإِذَا قُرِئَ عَلَيهِمُ القُرءَانُ لَا يَسجُدُونَ21And when the Recitation is recited unto them, they prostrate not.
بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ يُكَذِّبُونَ22Nay, those who concealed deny,
وَاللَّهُ أَعلَمُ بِمَا يُوعُونَ23and God knoweth best what they harbour.
فَبَشِّرهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ24So give them tidings of a painful torment,
إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ لَهُم أَجرٌ غَيرُ مَمنُونِ25save those who believe and do righteous deeds — for them is a reward unfailing.

564 (inshaqqat, v.1) — the heaven splits and obeys its Lord. gives us (shiqāq, discord/schism). When heaven splits, it is not rebellion but obedience — "as it must" (v.2). True splitting is submission; human schism is refusal.

565 Verse 6: "thou art labouring toward thy Lord, a hard labour." (kādiḥ) means one who toils until the skin is worn. Life itself is hard labour toward a meeting with God — not punishment but pilgrimage. The verse addresses every human: the meeting is inevitable; only the quality of labour differs.

566 Verse 19: "ye shall surely ride stage upon stage." (ṭabaqan 'an ṭabaq) means layer upon layer, state after state. suggests progressive movement through conditions — death, resurrection, judgement as successive stages. This is not cyclical but ascending.

Surah 85: Al-Buruj — The Constellations

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالسَّمَاءِ ذَاتِ البُرُوجِ1By the heaven of the constellations
وَاليَومِ المَوعُودِ2and by the Promised Day
وَشَاهِد وَمَشهُود3and by the witness and the witnessed:
قُتِلَ أَصحَٰبُ الأُخدُودِ4Perish the people of the trench,
النَّارِ ذَاتِ الوَقُودِ5the fire abounding in fuel,
إِذ هُم عَلَيهَا قُعُود6when they sat beside it
وَهُم عَلَىٰ مَا يَفعَلُونَ بِالمُؤمِنِينَ شُهُود7and they were witnesses to what they did unto the believers.
وَمَا نَقَمُواْ مِنهُم إِلَّا أَن يُؤمِنُواْ بِاللَّهِ العَزِيزِ الحَمِيدِ8And they took revenge upon them for naught save that they believed in God, the Mighty, the Praised,
الَّذِي لَهُ مُلكُ السَّمَٰوَٰتِ وَالأَرضِ وَاللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَيء شَهِيدٌ9He to Whom belongeth the dominion of the heavens and the earth; and God is Witness over all things.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ فَتَنُواْ المُؤمِنِينَ وَالمُؤمِنَٰتِ ثُمَّ لَم يَتُوبُواْ فَلَهُم عَذَابُ جَهَنَّمَ وَلَهُم عَذَابُ الحَرِيقِ10Verily, those who persecuted the believing men and the believing women, then repented not — for them is the torment of Hell and for them is the torment of the Burning.567
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ لَهُم جَنَّـٰت تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ ذَٰلِكَ الفَوزُ الكَبِيرُ11Verily, those who believe and do righteous deeds — for them are Gardens beneath which rivers flow; that is the great triumph.
إِنَّ بَطشَ رَبِّكَ لَشَدِيدٌ12Verily, the grip of thy Lord is severe.568
إِنَّهُ هُوَ يُبدِئُ وَيُعِيدُ13Verily, He it is Who beginneth and repeateth,569
وَهُوَ الغَفُورُ الوَدُودُ14and He is the Forgiving, the Loving,
ذُو العَرشِ المَجِيدُ15Lord of the Throne, the Glorious,
فَعَّال لِّمَا يُرِيدُ16Doer of what He willeth.
هَل أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ الجُنُودِ17Hath the story of the hosts come to thee —
فِرعَونَ وَثَمُودَ18of Pharaoh and Thamud?
بَلِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ فِي تَكذِيب19Nay, those who concealed are in denial,
وَاللَّهُ مِن وَرَائِهِم مُّحِيطُ20and God encompasseth them from behind.
بَل هُوَ قُرءَان مَّجِيد21Nay, it is a glorious Recitation
فِي لَوح مَّحفُوظِ22in a Guarded Tablet.

567 Verse 10: "those who persecuted the believing men and women." (fatanū) shares its root with (fitna, trial/persecution). The People of the Trench burn believers alive — the ultimate fitna. Yet v.10 offers even them repentance: "then repented not" implies they could have.

568 Verse 14: "He is the Forgiving, the Loving." (al-Wadūd) — God as the Loving One. Placed directly after "the grip of thy Lord is severe" (v.12), divine love and divine severity are not contradictions but two faces of the same engagement.

569 v.13 "He beginneth and repeateth" ↔ progressive revelation: God's creative pattern is initiation and return — each Dispensation a new beginning that repeats the eternal message.

Surah 86: At-Tariq — The Night-Comer

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالسَّمَاءِ وَالطَّارِقِ1By the heaven and the night-comer —
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا الطَّارِقُ2and what shall make thee know what the night-comer is?
النَّجمُ الثَّاقِبُ3The piercing star.570
إِن كُلُّ نَفس لَّمَّا عَلَيهَا حَافِظ4There is no soul but hath a guardian over it.571
فَليَنظُرِ الإِنسَٰنُ مِمَّ خُلِقَ5So let the human look at what he was created from:
خُلِقَ مِن مَّاء دَافِق6he was created from a gushing water
يَخرُجُ مِن بَينِ الصُّلبِ وَالتَّرَائِبِ7issuing from between the backbone and the ribs.
إِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ رَجعِهِ لَقَادِر8Verily, He is able to bring him back,
يَومَ تُبلَى السَّرَائِرُ9the Day when the secrets are tried.
فَمَا لَهُ مِن قُوَّة وَلَا نَاصِر10Then he shall have no strength and no helper.
وَالسَّمَاءِ ذَاتِ الرَّجعِ11By the heaven of the returning rain572
وَالأَرضِ ذَاتِ الصَّدعِ12and by the earth of the splitting seed —573
إِنَّهُ لَقَول فَصل13verily, it is a decisive word574
وَمَا هُوَ بِالهَزلِ14and it is no jest.
إِنَّهُم يَكِيدُونَ كَيدا15Verily, they are plotting a plot
وَأَكِيدُ كَيدا16and I am plotting a plot.
فَمَهِّلِ الكَٰفِرِينَ أَمهِلهُم رُوَيدَا17So grant respite to those who conceal; grant them respite awhile.

570 (aṭ-ṭāriq) — one who comes knocking at night. carries both the sense of night-arrival and of hammering/striking. The piercing star (v.3) is not merely bright but arrives — it knocks on the darkness. Revelation itself is a night-comer: it arrives when least expected and pierces through.

571 v.4 "there is no soul but hath a guardian over it" ↔ 82:10-12 "over you are guardians, noble ones": the guardian principle spans both surahs — every soul is watched, preserved, accounted for.

572 Verse 11: "the heaven of the returning rain." (ar-raj') means the return — rain that returns cyclically. Verse 8: "He is able to bring him back" uses the same concept. The rain's return and the human's return are sworn together — natural cycles testify to resurrection.

573 Verse 12: "the earth of the splitting seed." (aṣ-ṣad') — the earth cracks open for new life. The paired oaths — returning rain above, splitting earth below — frame creation as a cycle of descent and emergence, exactly as resurrection will be.

574 v.13 "a decisive word, no jest" ↔ 77:50 "what later message do they believe?": the Recitation is not entertainment but separation of truth from falsehood.

Surah 87: Al-A'la — The Most High

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

سَبِّحِ اسمَ رَبِّكَ الأَعلَى1Glorify the name of thy Lord, the Most High,
الَّذِي خَلَقَ فَسَوَّىٰ2Who created, then proportioned,
وَالَّذِي قَدَّرَ فَهَدَىٰ3and Who measured, then guided,
وَالَّذِي أَخرَجَ المَرعَىٰ4and Who brought forth the pasture
فَجَعَلَهُ غُثَاءً أَحوَىٰ5then made it darkened stubble.
سَنُقرِئُكَ فَلَا تَنسَىٰ6We shall make thee recite, and thou shalt not forget,
إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ إِنَّهُ يَعلَمُ الجَهرَ وَمَا يَخفَىٰ7save what God willeth; verily, He knoweth what is manifest and what is hidden.
وَنُيَسِّرُكَ لِليُسرَىٰ8And We shall ease thee unto the easiest way.575
فَذَكِّر إِن نَّفَعَتِ الذِّكرَىٰ9So remind, if the reminder profiteth.
سَيَذَّكَّرُ مَن يَخشَىٰ10He who feareth shall remember,
وَيَتَجَنَّبُهَا الأَشقَى11and the most wretched shall shun it,
الَّذِي يَصلَى النَّارَ الكُبرَىٰ12he who shall enter the Great Fire,
ثُمَّ لَا يَمُوتُ فِيهَا وَلَا يَحيَىٰ13then neither dieth therein nor liveth.
قَد أَفلَحَ مَن تَزَكَّىٰ14He hath prospered who purifieth himself
وَذَكَرَ اسمَ رَبِّهِ فَصَلَّىٰ15and remembereth the name of his Lord, and prayeth.
بَل تُؤثِرُونَ الحَيَوٰةَ الدُّنيَا16Nay, but ye prefer the life of this world,
وَالأخِرَةُ خَير وَأَبقَىٰ17while the Hereafter is better and more lasting.
إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَفِي الصُّحُفِ الأُولَىٰ18Verily, this is in the former Scriptures,
صُحُفِ إِبرَٰهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ19the Scriptures of Abraham and Moses.

575 Verse 7: "He knoweth what is manifest and what is hidden." (what is manifest) and (what is hidden) — the same ẓāhir/bāṭin principle documented in roots.md. God's knowledge encompasses both dimensions. The surah then eases the Prophet to "the easiest way" (v.8) — the path that navigates both.

Surah 88: Al-Ghashiyah — The Overwhelming

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

هَل أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ الغَٰشِيَةِ1Hath the tidings of the Overwhelming come to thee?
وُجُوه يَومَئِذٍ خَٰشِعَةٌ2Faces, that Day, downcast,
عَامِلَة نَّاصِبَة3labouring, toiling,
تَصلَىٰ نَارًا حَامِيَة4entering a scorching fire,
تُسقَىٰ مِن عَينٍ ءَانِيَة5given to drink from a boiling spring.
لَّيسَ لَهُم طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِن ضَرِيع6No food for them save bitter thorn,
لَّا يُسمِنُ وَلَا يُغنِي مِن جُوع7which neither nourisheth nor availeth against hunger.
وُجُوه يَومَئِذ نَّاعِمَة8Faces, that Day, blissful,
لِّسَعيِهَا رَاضِيَة9with their striving well-pleased,
فِي جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَة10in a lofty Garden,
لَّا تَسمَعُ فِيهَا لَٰغِيَة11wherein they hear no idle talk,
فِيهَا عَين جَارِيَة12wherein is a flowing spring,576
فِيهَا سُرُر مَّرفُوعَة13wherein are raised couches
وَأَكوَاب مَّوضُوعَة14and goblets set forth
وَنَمَارِقُ مَصفُوفَة15and cushions ranged in rows
وَزَرَابِيُّ مَبثُوثَةٌ16and carpets spread about.
أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى الإِبِلِ كَيفَ خُلِقَت17Do they not look at the camels, how they are created?
وَإِلَى السَّمَاءِ كَيفَ رُفِعَت18And at the heaven, how it is raised?
وَإِلَى الجِبَالِ كَيفَ نُصِبَت19And at the mountains, how they are set firm?
وَإِلَى الأَرضِ كَيفَ سُطِحَت20And at the earth, how it is spread?
فَذَكِّر إِنَّمَا أَنتَ مُذَكِّر21So remind; thou art but a reminder.577
لَّستَ عَلَيهِم بِمُصَيطِرٍ22Thou art not a taskmaster over them —
إِلَّا مَن تَوَلَّىٰ وَكَفَرَ23save him who turneth away and concealeth;578
فَيُعَذِّبُهُ اللَّهُ العَذَابَ الأَكبَرَ24him God shall chastise with the greater chastisement.
إِنَّ إِلَينَا إِيَابَهُم25Verily, unto Us is their return,
ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَينَا حِسَابَهُم26then verily, upon Us is their reckoning.

576 Verse 12: "a flowing spring" — the term ('ayn) in the Garden carries the double meaning documented in roots.md. The spring in the lofty Garden is also an eye — a source of insight that flows.

577 Verse 22: "Thou art not a taskmaster over them." (bi-musayṭir) — the Prophet is explicitly denied coercive authority. His role is reminder only (v.21). This is a foundational verse for religious freedom in Qur'anic ethics: the messenger reminds, he does not compel.

578 v.23 "him who turneth away and covereth over" ↔ 84:22 "those who covered over deny": the pairing of turning away and covering over as a single act appears in both surahs.

Surah 89: Al-Fajr — The Dawn

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالفَجرِ1By the Dawn
وَلَيَالٍ عَشر2and by ten nights
وَالشَّفعِ وَالوَترِ3and by the even and the odd
وَالَّيلِ إِذَا يَسرِ4and by the night when it departeth —
هَل فِي ذَٰلِكَ قَسَم لِّذِي حِجرٍ5is there in that an oath for one possessed of understanding?
أَلَم تَرَ كَيفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ6Hast thou not seen how thy Lord dealt with 'Ad,
إِرَمَ ذَاتِ العِمَادِ7Iram of the pillars,
الَّتِي لَم يُخلَق مِثلُهَا فِي البِلَٰدِ8the like of which was not created in the lands?
وَثَمُودَ الَّذِينَ جَابُواْ الصَّخرَ بِالوَادِ9And Thamud, who hewed the rocks in the valley?
وَفِرعَونَ ذِي الأَوتَادِ10And Pharaoh of the stakes?
الَّذِينَ طَغَواْ فِي البِلَٰدِ11Who transgressed in the lands
فَأَكثَرُواْ فِيهَا الفَسَادَ12and wrought much corruption therein?
فَصَبَّ عَلَيهِم رَبُّكَ سَوطَ عَذَابٍ13So thy Lord poured upon them a lash of chastisement.
إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَبِالمِرصَادِ14Verily, thy Lord is at the watch.
فَأَمَّا الإِنسَٰنُ إِذَا مَا ابتَلَىٰهُ رَبُّهُ فَأَكرَمَهُ وَنَعَّمَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَكرَمَنِ15As for the human, when his Lord trieth him and honoureth him and blesseth him, he saith: My Lord hath honoured me!
وَأَمَّا إِذَا مَا ابتَلَىٰهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيهِ رِزقَهُ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّي أَهَٰنَنِ16But when He trieth him and straiteneth his provision, he saith: My Lord hath abased me!
كَلَّا بَل لَّا تُكرِمُونَ اليَتِيمَ17Nay! But ye honour not the orphan
وَلَا تَحَـٰضُّونَ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ المِسكِينِ18and ye urge not the feeding of the destitute
وَتَأكُلُونَ التُّرَاثَ أَكلا لَّمّا19and ye devour the inheritance with devouring greed
وَتُحِبُّونَ المَالَ حُبّا جَمّا20and ye love wealth with exceeding love.
كَلَّا إِذَا دُكَّتِ الأَرضُ دَكّا دَكّا21Nay! When the earth is ground to dust, ground and ground again,579
وَجَاءَ رَبُّكَ وَالمَلَكُ صَفّا صَفّا22and thy Lord cometh, and the angels rank upon rank,
وَجِاْيءَ يَومَئِذِ بِجَهَنَّمَ يَومَئِذ يَتَذَكَّرُ الإِنسَٰنُ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُ الذِّكرَىٰ23and Hell is brought forth that Day — that Day the human shall remember, but what shall the remembrance avail him?
يَقُولُ يَٰلَيتَنِي قَدَّمتُ لِحَيَاتِي24He shall say: Would that I had sent forth for my life!
فَيَومَئِذ لَّا يُعَذِّبُ عَذَابَهُ أَحَد25So on that Day, none shall punish as He punisheth
وَلَا يُوثِقُ وَثَاقَهُ أَحَد26and none shall bind as He bindeth.
يَـٰأَيَّتُهَا النَّفسُ المُطمَئِنَّةُ27O thou soul at peace!
ارجِعِي إِلَىٰ رَبِّكِ رَاضِيَة مَّرضِيَّة28Return unto thy Lord, well-pleased, well-pleasing.
فَادخُلِي فِي عِبَٰدِي29So enter among My servants
وَادخُلِي جَنَّتِي30and enter My Garden.

579 Verse 21: "when the earth is ground to dust, ground and ground again." (dakkan dakkā) — the emphatic repetition conveys total pulverization. The earth that was spread as a cradle (78:6) is ground to nothing. Creation's hospitality is temporary.

Surah 90: Al-Balad — The City

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

لَا أُقسِمُ بِهَٰذَا البَلَدِ1Nay! I swear by this city —
وَأَنتَ حِلُّ بِهَٰذَا البَلَدِ2and thou art a dweller in this city —
وَوَالِد وَمَا وَلَدَ3and by a begetter and what he begat:
لَقَد خَلَقنَا الإِنسَٰنَ فِي كَبَدٍ4verily, We created the human in toil.580
أَيَحسَبُ أَن لَّن يَقدِرَ عَلَيهِ أَحَد5Doth he think that none hath power over him?
يَقُولُ أَهلَكتُ مَالا لُّبَدًا6He saith: I have squandered wealth in abundance!
أَيَحسَبُ أَن لَّم يَرَهُ أَحَدٌ7Doth he think that none hath seen him?
أَلَم نَجعَل لَّهُ عَينَينِ8Did We not make for him two eyes
وَلِسَانا وَشَفَتَينِ9and a tongue and two lips
وَهَدَينَٰهُ النَّجدَينِ10and guide him to the two paths?
فَلَا اقتَحَمَ العَقَبَةَ11Yet he hath not attempted the steep pass.
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا العَقَبَةُ12And what shall make thee know what the steep pass is?
فَكُّ رَقَبَةٍ13The freeing of a neck,581
أَو إِطعَٰم فِي يَوم ذِي مَسغَبَة14or feeding on a day of hunger
يَتِيما ذَا مَقرَبَةٍ15an orphan of near kin
أَو مِسكِينا ذَا مَترَبَة16or a destitute one in the dust.
ثُمَّ كَانَ مِنَ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَتَوَاصَواْ بِالصَّبرِ وَتَوَاصَواْ بِالمَرحَمَةِ17Then he be of those who believe and enjoin one another to steadfastness and enjoin one another to mercy.582
أُوْلَـٰئِكَ أَصحَٰبُ المَيمَنَةِ18Those are the companions of the right hand.
وَالَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ بِـَايَٰتِنَا هُم أَصحَٰبُ المَشـَمَةِ19And those who concealed Our signs — they are the companions of the left hand.
عَلَيهِم نَار مُّؤصَدَةُ20Upon them is a fire closed over.

580 Verse 4: "We created the human in toil." (kabad) means liver (the organ associated with suffering) and hardship. The human condition is inherently laborious — echoing 84:6 "thou art labouring toward thy Lord."

581 Verse 11-12: "he hath not attempted the steep pass." (al-'aqaba) — a mountain pass, an ascent requiring effort. suggests that which comes after, consequence. The steep pass IS the consequence of faith made visible: freeing slaves (v.13), feeding orphans (v.15), feeding the destitute (v.16). Spiri...

582 v.17 "enjoin one another to mercy" ↔ 55:1 "the All-Merciful": the root -- (r-ḥ-m) connects mutual human mercy to God's primary self-identification. To counsel mercy is to counsel the quality God names first.

Surah 91: Ash-Shams — The Sun

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالشَّمسِ وَضُحَىٰهَا1By the sun and its morning brightness
وَالقَمَرِ إِذَا تَلَىٰهَا2and by the moon when it followeth it
وَالنَّهَارِ إِذَا جَلَّىٰهَا3and by the day when it revealeth it
وَالَّيلِ إِذَا يَغشَىٰهَا4and by the night when it enshroudeth it
وَالسَّمَاءِ وَمَا بَنَىٰهَا5and by the heaven and what built it
وَالأَرضِ وَمَا طَحَىٰهَا6and by the earth and what spread it
وَنَفس وَمَا سَوَّىٰهَا7and by a soul and what fashioned it
فَأَلهَمَهَا فُجُورَهَا وَتَقوَىٰهَا8and inspired it with its wickedness and its God-consciousness —583
قَد أَفلَحَ مَن زَكَّىٰهَا9he hath prospered who purifieth it584
وَقَد خَابَ مَن دَسَّىٰهَا10and he hath failed who corrupteth it.
كَذَّبَت ثَمُودُ بِطَغوَىٰهَا11Thamud denied in their transgression,
إِذِ انبَعَثَ أَشقَىٰهَا12when the most wretched of them rose up.
فَقَالَ لَهُم رَسُولُ اللَّهِ نَاقَةَ اللَّهِ وَسُقيَٰهَا13Then the messenger of God said unto them: The she-camel of God! And her drink!
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَعَقَرُوهَا فَدَمدَمَ عَلَيهِم رَبُّهُم بِذَنبِهِم فَسَوَّىٰهَا14But they denied him and hamstrung her, so their Lord crushed them for their sin and levelled it,
وَلَا يَخَافُ عُقبَٰهَا15and He feareth not its consequence.

583 Verse 7: "by a soul and what fashioned it." (sawwāhā) — to make even, to proportion, to complete. The soul is fashioned with balance — then given both wickedness and God-consciousness (v.8). The fashioning is neutral; the choice is the human's.

584 Verse 9: "he hath prospered who purifieth it." (zakkāhā) — the same purification root as 79:18 (Moses to Pharaoh) and 87:14. Prosperity (, aflaḥa — also "to till the soil") comes through purification. The farmer-metaphor is embedded: to prosper is to cultivate the soul as one cultivates earth.

Surah 92: Al-Layl — The Night

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالَّيلِ إِذَا يَغشَىٰ1By the night when it enshroudeth585
وَالنَّهَارِ إِذَا تَجَلَّىٰ2and by the day when it unveileth
وَمَا خَلَقَ الذَّكَرَ وَالأُنثَىٰ3and by what created the male and the female:
إِنَّ سَعيَكُم لَشَتَّىٰ4verily, your striving is diverse.
فَأَمَّا مَن أَعطَىٰ وَاتَّقَىٰ5As for him who giveth and is God-conscious
وَصَدَّقَ بِالحُسنَىٰ6and affirmeth the best —
فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِليُسرَىٰ7We shall ease him unto ease.
وَأَمَّا مَن بَخِلَ وَاستَغنَىٰ8And as for him who is miserly and deemeth himself without need
وَكَذَّبَ بِالحُسنَىٰ9and denieth the best —
فَسَنُيَسِّرُهُ لِلعُسرَىٰ10We shall ease him unto hardship.
وَمَا يُغنِي عَنهُ مَالُهُ إِذَا تَرَدَّىٰ11And his wealth shall not avail him when he falleth.
إِنَّ عَلَينَا لَلهُدَىٰ12Verily, upon Us is the guidance,
وَإِنَّ لَنَا لَلأخِرَةَ وَالأُولَىٰ13and verily, unto Us belong the Hereafter and the first.
فَأَنذَرتُكُم نَارا تَلَظَّىٰ14So I have warned you of a blazing fire.
لَا يَصلَىٰهَا إِلَّا الأَشقَى15None shall enter it save the most wretched,
الَّذِي كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ16who denied and turned away.
وَسَيُجَنَّبُهَا الأَتقَى17And the most God-conscious shall be kept far from it,
الَّذِي يُؤتِي مَالَهُ يَتَزَكَّىٰ18he who giveth his wealth to purify himself
وَمَا لِأَحَدٍ عِندَهُ مِن نِّعمَة تُجزَىٰ19and none hath with him any favour to be repaid —
إِلَّا ابتِغَاءَ وَجهِ رَبِّهِ الأَعلَىٰ20only seeking the countenance of his Lord, the Most High.586
وَلَسَوفَ يَرضَىٰ21And he shall indeed be well-pleased.

585 Verse 1: "By the night when it enshroudeth." (yaghshā) — the same root as (88:1, the Overwhelming). The night covers as the Day overwhelms. The surah pairs night's covering (v.1) with day's unveiling (v.2, tajallā — to shine forth, to manifest). Covering and unveiling are the daily rhythm.

586 Verse 20: "only seeking the countenance of his Lord, the Most High." (wajh) — face, countenance, direction. The most God-conscious one seeks not reward but the Face of God. The same word appears in 55:27: "Only the Countenance of thy Lord remaineth."

Surah 93: Ad-Duha — The Morning Hours

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالضُّحَىٰ1By the morning hours
وَالَّيلِ إِذَا سَجَىٰ2and by the night when it is still:587
مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ3thy Lord hath not forsaken thee, nor doth He abhor thee.588
وَلَلأخِرَةُ خَير لَّكَ مِنَ الأُولَىٰ4And verily, the Hereafter is better for thee than the first.589
وَلَسَوفَ يُعطِيكَ رَبُّكَ فَتَرضَىٰ5And verily, thy Lord shall give unto thee, and thou shalt be well-pleased.
أَلَم يَجِدكَ يَتِيما فَـَاوَىٰ6Did He not find thee an orphan and shelter thee?590
وَوَجَدَكَ ضَالّا فَهَدَىٰ7And He found thee wandering and guided thee?
وَوَجَدَكَ عَائِلا فَأَغنَىٰ8And He found thee in need and enriched thee?
فَأَمَّا اليَتِيمَ فَلَا تَقهَر9So as for the orphan, oppress him not,
وَأَمَّا السَّائِلَ فَلَا تَنهَر10and as for the one who asketh, rebuke him not,
وَأَمَّا بِنِعمَةِ رَبِّكَ فَحَدِّث11and as for the bounty of thy Lord, proclaim it.

587 (aḍ-ḍuḥā) — the bright morning hours after sunrise. carries warmth and visibility. Paired with "the night when it is still" (v.2), the surah moves from light to darkness to reassurance: "thy Lord hath not forsaken thee" (v.3). The morning always returns.

588 Verse 3: "nor doth He abhor thee." (qalā) — to hate, to be disgusted with. The denial is emphatic: God neither forsook (, wadda'aka — from --, to leave) nor abhors. Two roots, two negations — the Prophet's dark night of the soul is addressed at its deepest fears.

589 v.4 "the Hereafter is better for thee than the first" ↔ 87:17 "the Hereafter is better and more lasting": identical teaching, near-identical phrasing across the two surahs.

590 Verse 6: "Did He not find thee an orphan and shelter thee?" (āwā) — to give refuge. The Prophet's personal history becomes universal instruction: because God sheltered you (v.6-8), you must shelter others (v.9-11). Biography becomes ethics.

Surah 94: Ash-Sharh — The Opening Forth

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

أَلَم نَشرَح لَكَ صَدرَكَ1Did We not open forth thy breast for thee591
وَوَضَعنَا عَنكَ وِزرَكَ2and lift from thee thy burden
الَّذِي أَنقَضَ ظَهرَكَ3which weighed down thy back
وَرَفَعنَا لَكَ ذِكرَكَ4and raise for thee thy renown?592
فَإِنَّ مَعَ العُسرِ يُسرًا5So verily, with hardship cometh ease.
إِنَّ مَعَ العُسرِ يُسرا6Verily, with hardship cometh ease.
فَإِذَا فَرَغتَ فَانصَب7So when thou art free, strive on593
وَإِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ فَارغَب8and unto thy Lord, turn in longing.

591 (sharaḥa, v.1) — to open the breast, to expand the chest. means to lay open, to explain, to make spacious. "Did We not open forth thy breast?" — the heart is made spacious enough to receive revelation. The same root gives (sharḥ, commentary/explanation) — to open a text is to open the breast.

592 Verse 4: "raise for thee thy renown." (dhikraka) — your remembrance, your mention. The same root as dhikr (invocation of God). God raised the Prophet's remembrance — every time God is mentioned, Muhammad is mentioned alongside Him in the shahada.

593 v.7 "when thou art free, strive on" ↔ 84:6 "thou art labouring toward thy Lord": rest is not the end — freedom from one burden is the beginning of the next striving.

Surah 95: At-Tin — The Fig

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالتِّينِ وَالزَّيتُونِ1By the fig and the olive
وَطُورِ سِينِينَ2and by Mount Sinai594
وَهَٰذَا البَلَدِ الأَمِينِ3and by this city secure:
لَقَد خَلَقنَا الإِنسَٰنَ فِي أَحسَنِ تَقوِيم4verily, We created the human in the finest stature,
ثُمَّ رَدَدنَٰهُ أَسفَلَ سَٰفِلِينَ5then We reduced him to the lowest of the low,595
إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ فَلَهُم أَجرٌ غَيرُ مَمنُون6save those who believe and do righteous deeds — for them is a reward unfailing.
فَمَا يُكَذِّبُكَ بَعدُ بِالدِّينِ7So what, after this, maketh thee deny the Judgement?
أَلَيسَ اللَّهُ بِأَحكَمِ الحَٰكِمِينَ8Is not God the wisest of judges?596

594 Verse 1: "By the fig and the olive." (at-tīn) and (az-zaytūn) — these are not random fruits but geographic markers. The fig points to the land of Jesus (the fig tree cursed in Mark 11); the olive to the land of Moses and Abraham (Mount of Olives); Mount Sinai (v.2) is explicit; "this city secure"...

595 Verse 4: "We created the human in the finest stature." (taqwīm) — from the same root as (resurrection, the standing up) and the Straight Path (). The human is created in the most upright form — both physically and spiritually. The fall to "lowest of the low" (v.5) is a fall from this uprightness.

596 Verse 8: "Is not God the wisest of judges?" (aḥkam al-ḥākimīn) — the superlative of wisdom applied to judgement. unites wisdom and justice: to judge wisely, to rule with knowledge. The surah's question is rhetorical — after showing creation, fall, and exception, who could judge better?

Surah 96: Al-Alaq — The Clot

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

اقرَأ بِاسمِ رَبِّكَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ1Recite! In the name of thy Lord Who created —597
خَلَقَ الإِنسَٰنَ مِن عَلَقٍ2created the human from a clinging clot.598
اقرَأ وَرَبُّكَ الأَكرَمُ3Recite! And thy Lord is the Most Generous,
الَّذِي عَلَّمَ بِالقَلَمِ4Who taught by the pen,
عَلَّمَ الإِنسَٰنَ مَا لَم يَعلَم5taught the human what he knew not.
كَلَّا إِنَّ الإِنسَٰنَ لَيَطغَىٰ6Nay! Verily, the human doth transgress
أَن رَّءَاهُ استَغنَىٰ7when he seeth himself without need.
إِنَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ الرُّجعَىٰ8Verily, unto thy Lord is the return.
أَرَءَيتَ الَّذِي يَنهَىٰ9Hast thou seen him who forbiddeth
عَبدًا إِذَا صَلَّىٰ10a servant when he prayeth?
أَرَءَيتَ إِن كَانَ عَلَى الهُدَىٰ11Hast thou seen if he be upon guidance
أَو أَمَرَ بِالتَّقوَىٰ12or commandeth God-consciousness?
أَرَءَيتَ إِن كَذَّبَ وَتَوَلَّىٰ13Hast thou seen if he denieth and turneth away?
أَلَم يَعلَم بِأَنَّ اللَّهَ يَرَىٰ14Knoweth he not that God seeth?
كَلَّا لَئِن لَّم يَنتَهِ لَنَسفَعَا بِالنَّاصِيَةِ15Nay! If he desist not, We shall seize him by the forelock —
نَاصِيَة كَٰذِبَةٍ خَاطِئَة16a lying, sinning forelock.
فَليَدعُ نَادِيَهُ17So let him call upon his company;
سَنَدعُ الزَّبَانِيَةَ18We shall call the guardians of Hell.
كَلَّا لَا تُطِعهُ وَاسجُد وَاقتَرِب19Nay! Obey him not, but prostrate and draw nigh.

597 Verse 1: "Recite!" (iqra') — the first word revealed. This root gives us (al-Qur'ān, the Recitation). The command connects directly to 55:2: "taught the Recitation" — God's first command to humanity through Muhammad is the same act that 55:2 names as God's first teaching. Recitation is both the o...

598 Verse 2: "created the human from a clinging clot." ('alaq) — something that clings or hangs. means attachment, dependence, connection. The human begins as something that clings — physically to the womb, spiritually to God. The title "The Clot" names humanity's essential condition: we are beings t...

Surah 97: Al-Qadr — The Power

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِنَّا أَنزَلنَٰهُ فِي لَيلَةِ القَدرِ1Verily, We sent it down in the Night of Power.599
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا لَيلَةُ القَدرِ2And what shall make thee know what the Night of Power is?
لَيلَةُ القَدرِ خَير مِّن أَلفِ شَهر3The Night of Power is better than a thousand months.600
تَنَزَّلُ المَلَـٰئِكَةُ وَالرُّوحُ فِيهَا بِإِذنِ رَبِّهِم مِّن كُلِّ أَمر4The angels and the Spirit descend therein, by the leave of their Lord, with every command.601
سَلَٰمٌ هِيَ حَتَّىٰ مَطلَعِ الفَجرِ5Peace it is, until the rising of the dawn.602

599 Verse 1: "We sent it down." (anzalnāhu) — the Qur'an descends. Verse 4: the angels and the Spirit "descend therein" — the term (tanazzalu). The Night of Power is defined by descent: the Book descends, the angels descend, the Spirit descends. Heaven pours into earth.

600 v.3 "better than a thousand months" ↔ 84:19 "stage upon stage": the Night of Power compresses a lifetime into one night — spiritual stages are not bound by ordinary time.

601 v.4 "the angels and the Spirit descend" ↔ 78:38 "the Day when the Spirit and the angels stand in ranks": the Spirit descends on the Night of Power and stands in rank on the Day of Judgement — descent and standing as bookends of the divine plan.

602 Verse 5: "Peace it is, until the rising of the dawn." (salām) — the entire Night is peace, unbroken until dawn. of Islam (devotion/peace) saturates this night. The Night of Power IS a night of Devotion — the same root that names the religion names the quality of this night.
