Surah 98: Al-Bayyinah — The Clear Proof

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

لَم يَكُنِ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن أَهلِ الكِتَٰبِ وَالمُشرِكِينَ مُنفَكِّينَ حَتَّىٰ تَأتِيَهُمُ البَيِّنَةُ1Those who concealed from among the People of the Book and those who associate would not desist until the Clear Proof came unto them:603
رَسُول مِّنَ اللَّهِ يَتلُواْ صُحُفا مُّطَهَّرَة2a messenger from God, reciting purified scriptures,
فِيهَا كُتُب قَيِّمَة3wherein are upright writings.
وَمَا تَفَرَّقَ الَّذِينَ أُوتُواْ الكِتَٰبَ إِلَّا مِن بَعدِ مَا جَاءَتهُمُ البَيِّنَةُ4And those who were given the Book did not divide save after the Clear Proof came unto them.604
وَمَا أُمِرُواْ إِلَّا لِيَعبُدُواْ اللَّهَ مُخلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ حُنَفَاءَ وَيُقِيمُواْ الصَّلَوٰةَ وَيُؤتُواْ الزَّكَوٰةَ وَذَٰلِكَ دِينُ القَيِّمَةِ5And they were commanded naught but to worship God, devoting the religion purely unto Him, as seekers of truth, and to establish the prayer and to give the alms; and that is the religion of the upright.605
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن أَهلِ الكِتَٰبِ وَالمُشرِكِينَ فِي نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُم شَرُّ البَرِيَّةِ6Verily, those who concealed from among the People of the Book and those who associate shall be in the fire of Hell, abiding therein; those are the worst of creation.
إِنَّ الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ أُوْلَـٰئِكَ هُم خَيرُ البَرِيَّةِ7Verily, those who believe and do righteous deeds — those are the best of creation.
جَزَاؤُهُم عِندَ رَبِّهِم جَنَّـٰتُ عَدن تَجرِي مِن تَحتِهَا الأَنهَٰرُ خَٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا أَبَدا رَّضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنهُم وَرَضُواْ عَنهُ ذَٰلِكَ لِمَن خَشِيَ رَبَّهُ8Their reward with their Lord is Gardens of Eden, beneath which rivers flow, abiding therein forever. God is well-pleased with them and they are well-pleased with Him. That is for him who feareth his Lord.

603 v.1 "would not desist until the Clear Proof came" ↔ progressive revelation: the People of the Book and the idolaters both awaited a proof — when it arrived, some recognized it and some divided. This pattern repeats with every Dispensation.

604 (al-bayyina) — the Clear Proof. gives us (bayān, Expression — 55:4) and (mubīn, manifest/clear). The Clear Proof that causes division (v.4) and the Expression that God teaches (55:4) share the same root. Clarity itself divides: once truth is made plain, people must choose.

605 Verse 3: "upright writings" and v.5: "the religion of the upright." (qayyima) — from the same root as (resurrection) and (the Straight Path). The writings are not merely correct but self-standing, upright. The religion of the upright ( ) is religion that stands on its own truth.

Surah 99: Az-Zalzalah — The Earthquake

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا زُلزِلَتِ الأَرضُ زِلزَالَهَا1When the earth is shaken with her shaking
وَأَخرَجَتِ الأَرضُ أَثقَالَهَا2and the earth bringeth forth her burdens606
وَقَالَ الإِنسَٰنُ مَا لَهَا3and the human saith: What aileth her? —
يَومَئِذ تُحَدِّثُ أَخبَارَهَا4that Day she shall tell her tidings,
بِأَنَّ رَبَّكَ أَوحَىٰ لَهَا5for that thy Lord hath inspired her.607
يَومَئِذ يَصدُرُ النَّاسُ أَشتَاتا لِّيُرَواْ أَعمَٰلَهُم6That Day, the people shall come forth in scattered groups to be shown their deeds.
فَمَن يَعمَل مِثقَالَ ذَرَّةٍ خَيرا يَرَهُ7So whoso doeth an atom's weight of good shall see it
وَمَن يَعمَل مِثقَالَ ذَرَّة شَرّا يَرَهُ8and whoso doeth an atom's weight of evil shall see it.

606 ... (zulzilat... zilzālahā) — the earth is shaken with HER shaking. The possessive "her" is key: the earthquake is not imposed but belongs to the earth. She shakes with her own quaking, brings forth her own burdens (v.2), tells her own tidings (v.4). The earth is an active witness, not a passive ...

607 Verse 5: "thy Lord hath inspired her." (awḥā) — the same word used for God's inspiration of prophets (42:3, 53:4). The earth receives waḥy — divine inspiration. The ground beneath your feet is a recipient of revelation, commanded to testify.

Surah 100: Al-Adiyat — The Chargers

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالعَٰدِيَٰتِ ضَبحا1By the charging steeds, panting,
فَالمُورِيَٰتِ قَدحا2then the strikers of sparks,
فَالمُغِيرَٰتِ صُبحا3then the raiders at dawn,
فَأَثَرنَ بِهِ نَقعا4then they stir up therewith a trail of dust,
فَوَسَطنَ بِهِ جَمعًا5then they plunge therewith into a host:
إِنَّ الإِنسَٰنَ لِرَبِّهِ لَكَنُود6verily, the human is ungrateful unto his Lord608
وَإِنَّهُ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ لَشَهِيد7and verily, he is a witness unto that
وَإِنَّهُ لِحُبِّ الخَيرِ لَشَدِيدٌ8and verily, in the love of wealth, he is fierce.609
أَفَلَا يَعلَمُ إِذَا بُعثِرَ مَا فِي القُبُورِ9Doth he not know? When what is in the graves is overturned610
وَحُصِّلَ مَا فِي الصُّدُورِ10and what is in the breasts is brought forth —
إِنَّ رَبَّهُم بِهِم يَومَئِذ لَّخَبِيرُ11verily, their Lord, that Day, is well aware of them.

608 Verse 6: "the human is ungrateful unto his Lord." (kanūd) — intensely ungrateful, covering over blessings. Though a different word from kāfir, the meaning overlaps: to be kanūd is to cover over the gifts one has received. Ingratitude is a form of covering.

609 (al-'ādiyāt) — the charging horses. means both to run/charge and to be an enemy. The horses charge in battle; the human charges through life with equal ferocity but misdirected — toward wealth (v.8) rather than toward God. The oath swears by focused energy to condemn wasted energy.

610 Verse 10: "what is in the breasts is brought forth." (ḥuṣṣila) means to be extracted, separated, brought to light. On the Day, the hidden contents of the heart are turned out like graves are overturned (v.9). Two extractions: from the ground, from the breast. Nothing remains buried.

Surah 101: Al-Qari'ah — The Striking

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

القَارِعَةُ1The Striking!
مَا القَارِعَةُ2What is the Striking?
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا القَارِعَةُ3And what shall make thee know what the Striking is?
يَومَ يَكُونُ النَّاسُ كَالفَرَاشِ المَبثُوثِ4The Day when the people shall be like scattered moths611
وَتَكُونُ الجِبَالُ كَالعِهنِ المَنفُوشِ5and the mountains shall be like carded wool.
فَأَمَّا مَن ثَقُلَت مَوَٰزِينُهُ6Then as for him whose balances are heavy,
فَهُوَ فِي عِيشَة رَّاضِيَة7he shall be in a life well-pleasing.
وَأَمَّا مَن خَفَّت مَوَٰزِينُهُ8And as for him whose balances are light,
فَأُمُّهُ هَاوِيَة9his abode shall be the Abyss.612
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا هِيَه10And what shall make thee know what it is?
نَارٌ حَامِيَةُ11A scorching fire.

611 v.4 "scattered moths" ↔ 81:1-6 cosmic unraveling: both surahs use images of disintegration — moths scattered, mountains like wool — to describe the end of the material order.

612 Verse 9: "his abode shall be the Abyss." (hāwiya) — literally, the bottomless, the falling-place. means to fall, to plunge. The one whose balance is light falls — not pushed but emptied of weight. Lightness of deeds IS the fall.

Surah 102: At-Takathur — Rivalry

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

أَلهَىٰكُمُ التَّكَاثُرُ1Rivalry in increase distracteth you613
حَتَّىٰ زُرتُمُ المَقَابِرَ2until ye visit the graves.
كَلَّا سَوفَ تَعلَمُونَ3Nay! Ye shall come to know.
ثُمَّ كَلَّا سَوفَ تَعلَمُونَ4Then nay! Ye shall come to know.
كَلَّا لَو تَعلَمُونَ عِلمَ اليَقِينِ5Nay! If ye but knew with the knowledge of certainty —
لَتَرَوُنَّ الجَحِيمَ6ye shall surely see the Blazing Fire.
ثُمَّ لَتَرَوُنَّهَا عَينَ اليَقِينِ7Then ye shall surely see it with the eye of certainty.614
ثُمَّ لَتُسـَلُنَّ يَومَئِذٍ عَنِ النَّعِيمِ8Then ye shall surely be asked, that Day, concerning the bounties.615

613 v.1 rivalry in increase ↔ 104:2 "who gathereth wealth and counteth it": At-Takathur diagnoses the disease (competitive accumulation); Al-Humazah names the patient (the slanderer-hoarder). The two surahs are companion diagnoses.

614 Verse 7: "ye shall surely see it with the eye of certainty." ('ayn al-yaqīn) — the eye of certainty. -- means both eye and spring (see roots.md). Certainty flows from direct seeing, as water flows from a spring. Three degrees of certainty appear: knowledge of certainty (v.5, ), eye of certainty (...

615 v.8 "asked concerning the bounties" ↔ 55's refrain "which of thy Lord's Bestowals will ye contradict?": the bounties (, na'īm) you will be asked about are the same Bestowals Ar-Rahman catalogues. Accumulation distracts from gratitude.

Surah 103: Al-Asr — The Fading Day

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَالعَصرِ1By the fading day,616
إِنَّ الإِنسَٰنَ لَفِي خُسرٍ2Verily, humankind is in a state of loss,617
إِلَّا الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُواْ وَعَمِلُواْ الصَّـٰلِحَٰتِ وَتَوَاصَواْ بِالحَقِّ وَتَوَاصَواْ بِالصَّبرِ3Save those who believe and perform righteous deeds, and enjoin one another to truth, and enjoin one another to steadfastness.618

616 v.1: (al-ʿaṣr) — root -- (ʿ-ṣ-r): to squeeze, to press, to wring out; also: age, era, epoch, afternoon. The same root gives us (ʿaṣīr, juice — what is pressed out) and (ʿaṣr, the afternoon prayer). "The Fading Day" in the translation captures the temporal decline; "The Age" captures the cosmic sw...

617 v.2: (al-insān) — root -- ('-n-s): to be familiar, to be human, to be sociable. The human as the social being, the one who seeks company. (khusr) — root -- (kh-s-r): to lose, to be in deficit, to perish. The same root appears in 55:9 ( , "make not the Balance deficient"). The human is in — loss, ...

618 v.3: The Four-Part Remedy (āmanū) — root -- ('-m-n): to believe, to be safe, to trust. The same root as (amāna, the Trust of 33:72). Belief IS the Trust. (ʿamilū aṣ-ṣāliḥāt) — righteous deeds. Root -- (ṣ-l-ḥ): to be whole, to reform, to function properly. The same root as (iṣlāḥ, reconciliation) ...

Surah 104: Al-Humazah — The Slanderer

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

وَيل لِّكُلِّ هُمَزَة لُّمَزَةٍ1Woe unto every slanderer, backbiter,
الَّذِي جَمَعَ مَالا وَعَدَّدَهُ2who gathereth wealth and counteth it,
يَحسَبُ أَنَّ مَالَهُ أَخلَدَهُ3thinking that his wealth hath made him immortal.
كَلَّا لَيُنبَذَنَّ فِي الحُطَمَةِ4Nay! He shall surely be cast into the Crusher.619
وَمَا أَدرَىٰكَ مَا الحُطَمَةُ5And what shall make thee know what the Crusher is?
نَارُ اللَّهِ المُوقَدَةُ6The kindled fire of God,
الَّتِي تَطَّلِعُ عَلَى الأَفـِدَةِ7which leapeth up over the hearts.620
إِنَّهَا عَلَيهِم مُّؤصَدَة8Verily, it is closed upon them
فِي عَمَد مُّمَدَّدَةِ9in pillars outstretched.

619 Verse 4: "He shall surely be cast into the Crusher." (al-ḥuṭama) — the Crusher. The one who crushes reputations (humaza) is cast into the Crusher (ḥuṭama). The punishment mirrors the sin. means to break into pieces — the slanderer who shattered others is himself shattered.

620 Verse 7: "which leapeth up over the hearts." (al-af'ida) — the hearts, the innermost seats of feeling. The fire of God targets the hearts specifically — not the flesh. The slanderer's sin was a sin of the heart (malice, greed); the fire returns to its source.

Surah 105: Al-Fil — The Elephant

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

أَلَم تَرَ كَيفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصحَٰبِ الفِيلِ1Hast thou not seen how thy Lord dealt with the people of the Elephant?
أَلَم يَجعَل كَيدَهُم فِي تَضلِيل2Did He not make their plot go astray?621
وَأَرسَلَ عَلَيهِم طَيرًا أَبَابِيلَ3And He sent against them birds in flocks,622
تَرمِيهِم بِحِجَارَة مِّن سِجِّيل4pelting them with stones of baked clay,
فَجَعَلَهُم كَعَصف مَّأكُولِ5and He made them like devoured husks.623

621 Verse 2: "Did He not make their plot go astray?" (kaydahum) — their stratagem. The same root appears in 77:39 ("if ye have a stratagem, use it against Me") and 86:15-16. Abraha's army plotted against the Ka'ba with overwhelming military force; God reduced it to nothing. tracks the futility of sch...

622 Verse 3: "birds in flocks." (abābīl) — a rare word, likely meaning in successive waves or swarms. The rarity itself is significant: an extraordinary event receives an extraordinary word. The birds are not named by species but by formation — coordinated divine intervention.

623 Verse 5: "devoured husks." ('aṣf) — chaff, dried husks, what remains after grain is consumed. The same root appears in 77:2 (violent blowing). The mightiest army reduced to what the wind scatters — emptied of substance.

Surah 106: Quraysh

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

لِإِيلَٰفِ قُرَيشٍ1For the binding together of Quraysh,
إِلَٰفِهِم رِحلَةَ الشِّتَاءِ وَالصَّيفِ2their binding together for the journey of winter and of summer:
فَليَعبُدُواْ رَبَّ هَٰذَا البَيتِ3so let them worship the Lord of this House,624
الَّذِي أَطعَمَهُم مِّن جُوع وَءَامَنَهُم مِّن خَوفِ4Who fed them against hunger and secured them against fear.625

624 Verse 3: "the Lord of this House." (rabb) — Lord, but also nurturer, sustainer, educator. Quraysh is told to worship the of the Ka'ba — the one who sustains the House that sustains their trade. The chain: God sustains the House, the House sustains the trade, the trade sustains Quraysh. Remove the...

625 v.4 security from fear ↔ 95:3 "this city secure": the security of Mecca is sworn by in At-Tin and explained in Quraysh — the city is secure because its Lord secures it.

Surah 107: Al-Ma'un — Small Kindnesses

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

أَرَءَيتَ الَّذِي يُكَذِّبُ بِالدِّينِ1Hast thou seen him who denieth the Judgement?626
فَذَٰلِكَ الَّذِي يَدُعُّ اليَتِيمَ2That is he who repelleth the orphan
وَلَا يَحُضُّ عَلَىٰ طَعَامِ المِسكِينِ3and urgeth not the feeding of the destitute.
فَوَيل لِّلمُصَلِّينَ4So woe unto the praying ones
الَّذِينَ هُم عَن صَلَاتِهِم سَاهُونَ5who are heedless of their prayer,
الَّذِينَ هُم يُرَاءُونَ6who make display
وَيَمنَعُونَ المَاعُونَ7and withhold small kindnesses.627

626 Verse 1: "him who denieth the Judgement." (ad-dīn) — simultaneously judgement, religion, and the concept of debt/reciprocity. To deny the dīn is to deny all three: that there is reckoning, that there is a way of life, that one owes anything to anyone. The surah then shows what this denial looks l...

627 Verse 7: "withhold small kindnesses." (al-mā'ūn) — the smallest acts of help: lending a pot, offering salt, sharing water. relates to (ma'īn, a flowing spring). The withholding of small kindnesses is the damming of a spring — blocking what should flow naturally between people.

Surah 108: Al-Kawthar — Abundance

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِنَّا أَعطَينَٰكَ الكَوثَرَ1Verily, We have given thee Abundance.628
فَصَلِّ لِرَبِّكَ وَانحَر2So pray unto thy Lord and sacrifice.629
إِنَّ شَانِئَكَ هُوَ الأَبتَرُ3Verily, it is he who hateth thee that is cut off.630

628 v.1 abundance ↔ 55:13 "which of thy Lord's Bestowals": divine abundance is the Bestowals, and contradicting them is self-severing

629 (inḥar, v.2) — root -- (n-ḥ-r): to sacrifice, to slaughter (at the throat). "So pray unto thy Lord and sacrifice." Prayer and sacrifice as paired responses to abundance — gratitude expressed through worship and giving.

630 (al-abtar, v.3) — root -- (b-t-r): to cut off, to sever, to be without posterity. "Thy detractor — he is the cut off one." The one who attacks the Prophet is the one truly severed. Spiritual posterity outlasts biological lineage.

Surah 109: Al-Kafirun — Those Who Conceal

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

قُل يَـٰأَيُّهَا الكَٰفِرُونَ1Say: O ye who conceal!
لَا أَعبُدُ مَا تَعبُدُونَ2I worship not what ye worship631
وَلَا أَنتُم عَٰبِدُونَ مَا أَعبُدُ3and ye worship not what I worship.
وَلَا أَنَا عَابِد مَّا عَبَدتُّم4And I shall not worship what ye have worshipped
وَلَا أَنتُم عَٰبِدُونَ مَا أَعبُدُ5and ye will not worship what I worship.
لَكُم دِينُكُم وَلِيَ دِينِ6Unto you your religion, and unto me my religion.632

631 (lā aʿbudu, v.2) — root -- (ʿ-b-d): to worship, to serve. Repeated four times in six verses — the surah is built on the worship root. "I worship not what ye worship" is not hostility but clarity of distinction.

632 (dīn, v.6) — root -- (d-y-n): religion, requital, debt — same as 1:4 (Day of Judgement/Requital). "Unto you your religion and unto me my religion" — the ultimate religious freedom verse. Each walks their own path of requital.

Surah 110: An-Nasr — The Help

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

إِذَا جَاءَ نَصرُ اللَّهِ وَالفَتحُ1When the help of God cometh, and the opening,633
وَرَأَيتَ النَّاسَ يَدخُلُونَ فِي دِينِ اللَّهِ أَفوَاجا2and thou seest the people entering the religion of God in multitudes,
فَسَبِّح بِحَمدِ رَبِّكَ وَاستَغفِرهُ إِنَّهُ كَانَ تَوَّابَا3then glorify with the praise of thy Lord and seek His forgiveness; verily, He is ever relenting.634

633 (naṣr, v.1) — root -- (n-ṣ-r): to help, to aid, to grant victory. The same root gives us (anṣār, helpers — the Medinan supporters) and (naṣārā, Christians — possibly "the helpers"). Help, victory, and the name for Christians share one root.

634 (istaghfir, v.3) — root -- (gh-f-r): to cover, to forgive. Form X: seek forgiveness. At the moment of victory, the command is: seek forgiveness. Triumph requires humility, not celebration.

Surah 111: Al-Masad — The Fibre

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

تَبَّت يَدَا أَبِي لَهَب وَتَبَّ1Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!635
مَا أَغنَىٰ عَنهُ مَالُهُ وَمَا كَسَبَ2His wealth availed him not, nor what he earned.
سَيَصلَىٰ نَارا ذَاتَ لَهَب3He shall enter a fire of flame
وَامرَأَتُهُ حَمَّالَةَ الحَطَبِ4and his wife, the carrier of firewood,
فِي جِيدِهَا حَبل مِّن مَّسَدِ5around her neck a rope of palm-fibre.636

635 (tabbat, v.1) — root -- (t-b-b): to perish, to be ruined, to lose. "Perish the hands of Abu Lahab, and perish he!" The doubling (hands + he himself) emphasizes total ruin — both his actions (hands) and his being.

636 (masad, v.5) — root -- (m-s-d): twisted fibre, palm-leaf rope. His wife carries fuel with "a rope of twisted fibre" around her neck — the one who spreads slander is bound by what she spreads.

Surah 112: Al-Ikhlas — The Sincerity

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

قُل هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ1Say: He is the One God637
اللَّهُ الصَّمَدُ2God the Everlasting638
لَم يَلِد وَلَم يُولَد3He begat not, nor was He begotten639
وَلَم يَكُن لَّهُ كُفُوًا أَحَدُ4and none is His equal640

637 v.1: (aḥad) — root -- (w-ḥ-d): one, unique, singular. Not merely the number one (, wāḥid) but — the absolutely unique, the one beyond comparison. The distinction matters: = first in a series; = the only one, with no series possible.

638 v.2: (aṣ-Ṣamad) — root -- (ṣ-m-d): to be solid, impervious, self-sufficient, eternal, to resist penetration. "Everlasting" in the translation captures duration but the root means more: the one to whom all turn in need, yet who Himself needs nothing. The one everything leans on, who leans on nothi...

639 v.3: (yalid) — root -- (w-l-d): to beget, to give birth. The past tense with : "He begat not" — it never happened, at any point. (yūlad) — passive of the same root: "nor was He begotten." God is neither cause nor effect in a generative chain.

640 v.4: (kufuw) — root -- (k-f-'): to be equal, comparable, equivalent. "None is His equal." No equivalence exists — nothing can be placed beside God on the Balance. This is the anti-shirk (anti-association) verse in its purest form.

Surah 113: Al-Falaq — Dawn

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

قُل أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ الفَلَقِ1Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of the Dawn641
مِن شَرِّ مَا خَلَقَ2from the evil of what He created642
وَمِن شَرِّ غَاسِقٍ إِذَا وَقَبَ3and from the evil of the darkening, when it setteth in643
وَمِن شَرِّ النَّفَّـٰثَٰتِ فِي العُقَدِ4and from the evil of those who blow upon the knots644
وَمِن شَرِّ حَاسِدٍ إِذَا حَسَدَ5and from evil of an envier when he envies.645

641 v.1: (aʿūdhu) — root -- (ʿ-w-dh): to seek refuge, to take shelter. The act of seeking divine protection. (al-falaq) — root -- (f-l-q): to split, to cleave, to break open. Dawn IS the splitting of darkness. The same root appears in 6:95 ( , "the Splitter of the grain and the date-stone"). God spli...

642 v.2: (sharr) — root -- (sh-r-r): evil, harm, wickedness. "From the evil of what He created" — evil exists within creation, not outside it. God created the capacity for both good and evil.

643 v.3: (ghāsiq) — root -- (gh-s-q): to darken, to flow in, to become obscure. "The darkening when it setteth in" — not merely night but the active process of darkening, the encroachment of obscurity. (waqaba) — root -- (w-q-b): to penetrate, to enter into, to set (of celestial bodies). Darkness doe...

644 v.4: (an-naffāthāt) — root -- (n-f-th): to blow, to breathe out, to spit lightly. The feminine plural — "those (f.) who blow." Traditionally: sorceresses who blow on knots to cast spells. But the root is about breath, exhalation — those who exhale upon the ties/knots of social bonds. (al-ʿuqad) —...

645 v.5: (ḥāsid) — root -- (ḥ-s-d): to envy. The envier "when he envies" — envy as an active, willed state, not a passive feeling.

Surah 114: An-Nas — Humanity

بِسمِ اللَّهِ الرَّحمَٰنِ الرَّحِيمِ

In the Name of God, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful.

قُل أَعُوذُ بِرَبِّ النَّاسِ1Say: I seek refuge in the Lord of Humanity,
مَلِكِ النَّاسِ2King of Men,
إِلَٰهِ النَّاسِ3God of Men,
مِن شَرِّ الوَسوَاسِ الخَنَّاسِ4from the evil of the whisperer, the withdrawer,646
الَّذِي يُوَسوِسُ فِي صُدُورِ النَّاسِ5the whisperer in the breasts of mankind,647
مِنَ الجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ6of spectres and men.648

646 v.4: (al-waswās) — root --- (w-s-w-s): to whisper, to insinuate, to suggest. The reduplication () conveys repetition — whisper after whisper, relentless suggestion. The Whisperer is not a one-time tempter but a persistent voice. (al-khannās) — root -- (kh-n-s): to withdraw, to shrink back, to hid...

647 v.5: (ṣudūr) — root -- (ṣ-d-r): breast, chest, front, the seat of feeling. Not the head (, reason) but the breast (, feeling/intuition). The whisperer targets the emotional center, not the rational center. Evil enters through feeling before it reaches thought.

648 v.6: (al-jinna) — root -- (j-n-n): the hidden ones, the spectres. The SAME root as (janna, garden/hidden realm) and all the j-n-n saturation of Surah 55. The whisperer comes from both the hidden dimension (jinn) and the visible dimension (people). Evil has both a hidden and a manifest source.

Appendix: Root Discoveries

The Principle

The Qur'an was preserved in Arabic because Arabic's root system is tamper-resistant. Every word derives from a three- or four-letter root, and every use of that root cross-references every other use throughout the text. You can distort one verse's translation, but the root appears in fifty others that contradict the distortion. The roots don't lie — they've been there for 1400 years, waiting to be read without the interpretive overlay.

This document catalogues the root discoveries made during this translation project — cases where returning to the Arabic root recovers a meaning that centuries of conventional translation have obscured.

The Central Axis: s-l-m and k-f-r

The two most important roots in the Qur'an are also its two opposing movements:

• **س-ل-م (s-l-m)** — to be whole, at peace, devoted, open. The movement of the soul *toward* God. Opening up. Letting light in.

• **ك-ف-ر (k-f-r)** — to cover, conceal, close, be ungrateful. The movement of the soul *away from* God. Closing down. Blocking the light.

Every person, every moment, every choice is somewhere on the axis between s-l-m (opening) and k-f-r (covering). The Qur'an's entire project is to move the reader from one to the other — from covering to opening, from ingratitude to wholeness.

These are not merely two important terms among many. They are THE two roots — the axis around which the entire Book turns. And this translation project itself enacts the same movement: uncovering (reversing k-f-r) what centuries of conventional interpretation have covered over, and devoting careful attention (performing s-l-m) to what the roots actually say.

Roots That Change Everything

Root ك-ف-ر (k-f-r) — "to cover over"

**Conventional:** "to disbelieve," "infidel," "kafir"

**Root meaning:** to cover, conceal, hide. A farmer is كافر because he covers seeds with earth.

**Impact:** "Those who disbelieve" becomes "those who cover over" — describing an *action* (covering truth), not an *identity* (disbeliever). Anyone can stop covering; it is not a permanent category. This transforms every verse about "unbelievers" from a judgement on people to a description of what they *do*.

**Key verses:** Used hundreds of times. Compare 2:6–7 (hearts sealed = covering made permanent), 2:42 (لا تلبسوا الحق بالباطل, "dress not the truth in falsehood" — another covering metaphor), 4:157 (covering over vs. certainty about the crucifixion).

**Related:** 83:14 uses رَانَ (rāna, root ر-ي-ن, to rust over) — sin as rust on the heart. Another kind of covering.

Root ض-ر-ب (ḍ-r-b) — "to put forth, to set apart"

**Conventional:** "to strike, to beat"

**Root meaning:** to put forth, to set forth, to travel, to separate, to coin, to strike

**Impact:** 4:34 (وَاضْرِبُوهُنَّ) conventionally "strike them [your wives]" becomes "set them apart" — following the escalation pattern in the verse: counsel → emigrate from the bed → set apart. Each step increases *distance*, not *violence*.

**The proof:** 24:31 uses the identical root (وَلْيَضْرِبْنَ) for the gentle gesture of women *drawing* their coverings over their bosoms. The Qur'an itself demonstrates that ḍ-r-b means "to put forth / to draw" in a protective, non-violent context. God Himself "sets forth" (يَضْرِبُ) parables throughout the Qur'an using this root (2:26, 24:35, etc.).

**Key verses:** 4:34 (set apart), 24:31 (draw coverings), 2:26 (God sets forth a likeness), 8:12 (strike above the necks — to angels), 24:35 (God sets forth likenesses for people).

Root ج-ن-ن (j-n-n) — "to conceal, to hide"

**Conventional:** جَنَّة = "paradise/garden," جِنّ = "jinn/genie"

**Root meaning:** to conceal, to hide, to cover from sight

**Impact:** The "garden" of paradise is fundamentally the "hidden place." The jinn are the "hidden ones." The entire garden section of Surah 55 is saturated with this root — not a description of material luxury but the unveiling of concealed spiritual reality.

**The saturation in Surah 55:**

• The term جَنَّة (janna) = the hidden place/garden

• The term جِنّ / جَانّ (jinn/jānn) = the hidden beings (named in vv.56, 74)

• The term جَنَى (janā) = the harvest of what was hidden (v.54)

• The term عَبْقَرِيّ (ʿabqarī) = crafts from ʿAbqar, the legendary jinn-inhabited place (v.76)

**Related roots:** بَطَائِن (baṭā'in, inner linings, v.54) shares the root of بَاطِن (bāṭin, the hidden/esoteric). مُدْهَامَّتَان (mudhāmmatān, v.64, root د-ه-م, to overwhelm/darken) — the second pair of hidden realms is *deeper*. The garden reading (couches, fruit) is the ظَاهِر (ẓāhir, apparent); the root reading reveals the بَاطِن (bāṭin, hidden).

Root ح-و-ر (ḥ-w-r) — "to return"

**Conventional:** حُور = "houris," "fair maidens," "72 virgins"

**Root meaning:** to return, to whiten, to be intensely contrasting

**Impact:** حُور (ḥūr) shares its root with حَوَارِيُّون (ḥawāriyyūn) — the disciples of Jesus (3:52, 5:111). The "paradise maidens" and "Jesus's disciples" are the same root word. Both are the devoted returners — those who return to God from the margins. "The Returners" replaces "fair maidens" entirely.

**Key verses:** 55:72 (the Returners, sheltered in the dwellings), 3:52 (the Returners said: "We are the helpers of God"), 5:111–112 (the Returners and the Table).

**Connection:** The root meaning "return" links to تَوْبَة (tawba, repentance — root ت-و-ب, to return). Surah 9 (At-Tawbah/Repentance) and the ḥūr of Surah 55 share the concept: returning to God.

Root س-ل-م (s-l-m) — "to devote, to be whole, to be at peace"

**Conventional:** إسلام = "Islam" (the religion), مُسْلِم = "Muslim" (an adherent)

**Root meaning:** to devote, to submit, to be whole, to be at peace

**Impact:** When Abraham says أَسْلَمْتُ لِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ (2:131), he is not saying "I became a Muslim" — he is saying "I have devoted myself unto the Lord of the worlds." The word describes a *quality* (wholeness, devotion, peace), not a *label* (religious identity). This recovers the Qur'an's own universalism: anyone who devotes themselves to God is in a state of islām, regardless of which Manifestation they follow.

**Key verses:** 2:112 (whoso devoteth his countenance unto God), 2:128 (Abraham: make us Devoted unto Thee), 2:131 (Be devoted! / I have devoted myself), 3:19 (the religion with God is Devotion), 3:67 (Abraham was upright, devoted), 4:125 (he who hath devoted his face unto God), 5:3 (am well pleased with Devotion as a religion).

**Also:** 8:61 "if they incline to peace (السَّلْم, as-salm)" — same root. To incline toward peace IS to incline toward devotion. Peace and devotion are two faces of the same root.

**Related:** The cow in 2:71 is described as مُسَلَّمَة (musallamat, unblemished/whole) — the same root. The cow is in a state of devotion — wholeness, integrity, being complete.

Root ش-ب-ه (sh-b-h) — "to resemble, to be ambiguous"

**Conventional (4:157):** "a likeness was made for them" → substitution theology (someone else crucified in Jesus's place)

**Root meaning:** to resemble, to be made to seem, to be made ambiguous

**Impact:** شُبِّهَ لَهُمْ (shubbiha lahum) is **impersonal passive** — "it was made ambiguous unto them." The grammar provides no substitute person. What was ambiguous was the *event*, not a body double. Combined with يَقِينًا (yaqīnan, "with certainty") at the verse's end: "they slew him not with certainty" — they lack certain knowledge of what happened, which is different from flatly denying the crucifixion occurred.

**Key verse:** 4:157 — the entire substitution theology requires adding a grammatical subject the Arabic does not provide. The verse describes ambiguity of perception, not divine deception.

Root خ-م-ر (kh-m-r) — "to cover, to ferment"

**Conventional:** خِمَار = "veil/headscarf," خَمْر = "wine"

**Root meaning:** to cover, to conceal, to ferment

**Impact:** Veils and wine share a root. Both *cover* — one covers the body, the other clouds the mind. The root is neutral; the purpose determines the value. The 24:31 instruction to "draw their coverings (خُمُر) over their bosoms (جُيُوب)" uses this root — the covering is directed at the chest, not the head. The words for head (رأس) and hair (شعر) do not appear.

Root إ-س-ت-ب-ر-ق (istabraq) — "brilliance-seeking"

**Conventional:** A Persian loanword for "brocade/thick silk"

**Arabic root reading:** إِسْتَ (Form X prefix, "seeking") + بَرَقَ (b-r-q, to flash, to radiate — root of بَرْق/lightning)

**Impact:** The inner linings (بَطَائِن, baṭā'in — from the same root as بَاطِن, the esoteric) of the spreads in 55:54 are not of luxury fabric but of "inner radiance" — the hidden dimension shines with its own light. Since the Qur'an claims to be in "clear Arabic" (عربي مبين, 16:103), reading supposed loanwords through Arabic roots may recover meanings that the "loanword" classification has obscured.

Root خ-ض-ر (kh-ḍ-r) — "fresh, alive, new-grown"

**Conventional:** "green" (the color)

**Root meaning:** fresh, moist, new-grown, alive

**Impact:** The color green is a *consequence* of freshness, not the primary meaning. خُضْر in 55:76 means "fresh cushions," not merely "green cushions." Everything in the hidden Realm is alive, new, un-dried. The word also evokes الخِضْر (al-Khiḍr), the Fresh One of Surah 18 — Moses's mysterious companion who embodies hidden, living wisdom. Freshness is the quality of the hidden dimension; the anti-garden of 77:31 is defined by its absence of freshness.

Root خ-ت-م (kh-t-m) — "to seal, to authenticate"

**Conventional (33:40):** خاتم النبيين = "the last of the prophets"

**Root meaning:** to seal, to stamp, to conclude, to authenticate

**Impact:** A seal both *closes* and *validates*. خاتم carries both meanings simultaneously. Muhammad as the "Seal of the Prophets" can mean both "the one who concludes the prophetic cycle" AND "the one who authenticates all the prophets." The authentication meaning is consistent with the Qur'an's own progressive revelation framework (2:41, 2:87 — each Book "confirming what is with you"). The verse itself calls Muhammad رَسُول اللَّه (Messenger of God, root ر-س-ل) while sealing the نَبِيِّين (prophets, root ن-ب-أ) — the door of نبوة (prophethood) may be sealed while the door of رسالة (messengership) remains open.

Root ع-ي-ن (ʿ-y-n) — "spring AND eye"

**Conventional:** عَيْن = "spring (of water)" or "eye" (two separate meanings)

**Root meaning:** both simultaneously — the source that sees, the eye that flows

**Impact:** In 55:50 and 55:66, the "two springs" (عَيْنَان) in the hidden Realms are also "two eyes" — two wellsprings of insight that both see and give. The first pair flows (تَجْرِيَان); the second gushes (نَضَّاخَتَان). Deeper Realms = more intense outpouring of vision.

Root ق-و-م (q-w-m) — "to stand upright, to rise, to uphold"

**Conventional:** قَوَّامُون (4:34) = "in charge of," "authority over" women

**Root meaning:** to stand, to rise, to uphold, to sustain, to be upright

**Impact:** "Men are upholders over women" — bearing responsibility, not wielding authority. The same root gives us: الصراط المستقيم (the Straight Path, 1:6 — the path that stands upright), قِيَامَة (resurrection — the great standing up), قَوَّامِين بِالْقِسْطِ (33:35 — "upholders of equity," applied to ALL believers, male and female). The root's core meaning is uprightness and support, not domination.

Root ق-ص-ر (q-ṣ-r) — "to restrain, to shelter"

**Conventional:** Separate meanings for different forms

**Root meaning:** to limit, to restrain, to confine, to shelter

**Impact:** In Surah 55, the first hidden Realm contains قَاصِرَات الطَّرْف (qāṣirāt aṭ-ṭarf, v.56) — active restrainers of glance (focused devotion). The second contains مَقْصُورَات (maqṣūrāt, v.72) — those who are sheltered (passive, protected). Same root, shifted from active devotion to being protected. The beings of the hidden Realms are not objects of desire but beings in states of focused attention and sacred seclusion.

Root ف-ل-ق (f-l-q) — "to split, to cleave"

**Conventional:** الفلق (113:1) = "the dawn"

**Root meaning:** to split open, to cleave apart

**Impact:** Dawn IS the splitting of darkness. The same root appears in 6:95 (God as "the Splitter of the grain and the date-stone"). God splits seeds into life and darkness into light — the same action. Dawn as daily resurrection. Seeking refuge in the Lord of the Splitting is seeking refuge in the God who breaks open every darkness.

The Pattern: ẓāhir and bāṭin

The root ظ-ه-ر (ẓ-h-r) means "to appear, to be outward, to be manifest." The root ب-ط-ن (b-ṭ-n) means "to be hidden, to be inner, to be esoteric." These two roots describe the Qur'an's own hermeneutic method: every verse has an apparent (ẓāhir) meaning and a hidden (bāṭin) meaning.

The root-recovery approach of this translation works by reading the bāṭin through the ẓāhir — the hidden meaning through the apparent words. The Arabic root system IS the mechanism by which the bāṭin is preserved within the ẓāhir. You cannot change the roots without changing the language. The roots are the Qur'an's self-encrypting preservation system.

This is why the Qur'an commands its own preservation in Arabic: the true meaning manages to hide beyond the efforts to conceal it, because Arabic's root system is tamper-resistant. Every root cross-references every other use of itself, creating a web of meaning that no individual interpreter can override.

Additional Confirmed Roots

Root ن-س-خ (n-s-kh) — "to copy, to transcribe"

**Conventional (2:106):** "abrogate" — used to argue that later verses cancel earlier ones

**Root meaning:** to copy, to transcribe, to transfer. Abrogation is a secondary meaning.

**Impact:** The "abrogation verse" (2:106) can be read as: "Whatsoever sign We *transcribe* or cause to be forgotten, We bring one better than it or the like thereof." God transcribes/copies His signs into new forms — each revelation is a new transcription of the eternal message, not the cancellation of a previous one. This aligns with the progressive revelation principle: each Dispensation is a fresh copy of the divine template, adapted for its age.

Root ر-ح-م (r-ḥ-m) — "mercy AND womb"

**Conventional:** رحمة = "mercy," رحم = "womb" — treated as separate concepts

**Root meaning:** both derive from the same root — mercy IS womb, the place of nurture, gestation, protection

**Impact:** God's primary self-identification (الرحمن الرحيم, the All-Merciful, the Most Merciful) is rooted in the womb. Divine mercy is maternal, gestational, nurturing. Surah 19 (Maryam) has 16 occurrences of الرحمن — Mary's surah is drenched in womb-mercy. The Basmala that opens every surah (except 9) invokes the womb-root twice. And 1:1 instructs: "be mindful of God... and of the wombs (الأرحام)" — God and wombs in the same command, because they share the root of mercy.

**Key verses:** Every Basmala, 1:1, 19:passim, 55:1 (the All-Merciful as first word).

Root و-ل-ي (w-l-y) — "guardian, protector" (NOT "friend")

**Conventional (5:51):** "Take not the Jews and Christians as friends"

**Root meaning:** to be near, to be a guardian, to have authority, to protect

**Impact:** أَوْلِيَاء (awliyā') means guardians, protectors, those with authority over you — not friends. The verse forbids taking non-believers as *legal guardians or political protectors* in a specific wartime context, not as friends. The root gives us وَلِيّ (walī, guardian/saint), مَوْلَى (mawlā, master/patron), and وِلَايَة (wilāya, authority/guardianship). Friendship is صَدَاقَة (ṣadāqa) from a completely different root (ص-د-ق, truth). The mistranslation "friends" has caused immeasurable interfaith damage.

**Key verses:** 5:51 (guardians, not friends), 2:257 (God is the walī of the believers), 4:45 (God as walī and helper).

Root ح-م-د (ḥ-m-d) — "to praise, to commend"

**Conventional:** محمد = "Muhammad" (a name), أحمد = "Ahmad" (another name)

**Root meaning:** to praise, to commend, to be praiseworthy

**Impact:** Muhammad, Ahmad, Mahmud are all the same root — different forms of "the praised one." In 61:6, Jesus prophesies a messenger "whose name is Ahmad" — this is not merely a proper name but "the most praiseworthy," allowing it to point beyond a single individual. The root connects the Prophet's name to الحمد (al-ḥamd, praise) in 1:2 (الحمد لله, "Praise be to God") — the Qur'an's second word after the Basmala shares the Prophet's own root.

**Key verses:** 1:2, 61:6 (Ahmad prophecy), 33:40 (Muhammad as Seal), 17:79 (مقاما محمودا, "a praised station").

Root ك-ن-ن (k-n-n) — "to conceal, to treasure"

**Conventional:** مكنون = "well-guarded" (of a book or pearls)

**Root meaning:** to conceal, to treasure, to protect within

**Impact:** In 56:77–78, the Qur'an is called "a noble Recitation in a hidden Book" (كتاب مكنون). The root ك-ن-ن parallels ج-ن-ن (concealment) — the Recitation itself is concealed within a treasured Book. This second concealment root reinforces the j-n-n saturation of Surah 55: the hidden dimension has multiple layers of concealment, each with its own root. What is مكنون is doubly hidden — concealed AND treasured.

**Key verses:** 56:78 (hidden Book), 52:24 (pearls hidden/treasured), 37:49 (eggs hidden/treasured).

Root ظ-ه-ر / ب-ط-ن (ẓ-h-r / b-ṭ-n) — "manifest / hidden"

**Conventional:** treated as opposites

**Root meaning:** two aspects of every reality — the apparent and the concealed

**Impact:** 57:3 is the locus classicus: "He is the First and the Last, the Manifest (الظاهر) and the Hidden (الباطن)." God Himself encompasses both dimensions. This verse provides the Qur'anic foundation for the entire root-recovery method: every verse has a ẓāhir (what it appears to say) and a bāṭin (what it says at the root level). The four divine names in 57:3 — First, Last, Manifest, Hidden — map onto progressive revelation: God is the First (origin), the Last (destination), the Manifest (in each Manifestation), and the Hidden (in essence, always).

**Key verses:** 57:3, 55:54 (بطائن/inner linings from the same root), 18:60–82 (the entire Khiḍr passage as ẓāhir/bāṭin drama).

Root ق-ر-أ (q-r-') — "to recite, to read"

**Conventional:** القرآن = "the Quran" (a proper name)

**Root meaning:** to recite, to read aloud, to gather

**Impact:** Translating القرآن as "the Recitation" (as in 55:2) recovers the word's meaning. It is not a static book title but an active process — the act of reciting, of reading aloud, of gathering meaning through speech. The very first word revealed was اقرأ (iqra', "Recite!" — 96:1), from the same root. The Book is named after what you DO with it, not what it IS. And 55:1–2 places the teaching of the Recitation as God's first act after identifying Himself as the All-Merciful — mercy teaches recitation, recitation awakens the human (55:3), the awakened human receives expression (55:4).

**Key verses:** 96:1 (the first revelation — "Recite!"), 55:2 (taught the Recitation), 56:77 (a noble Recitation), 2:185 (Ramadan in which the Recitation was sent down).

Roots Still Under Investigation

• The term **م-ر-ج (m-r-j)** — 55:15/55:19: "current" (of fire) and "pastures" (of the seas). The diacritical dot correction that revealed the "current" reading for the jinn's creation. Connects jinn to energy, electricity, invisible currents.

• The term **ن-و-ر / ن-ا-ر (n-w-r / n-a-r)** — light and fire share a root with a vowel shift. The same source illuminates and burns. 24:35 (Light Verse) and 55:15 (current of fire) as two faces of the same reality. 36:80 "from the green tree, fire" connects kh-ḍ-r (fresh/green) to n-a-r (fire) — freshness becoming energy.

• The term **د-ن-و (d-n-w)** — nearness. 55:54 (harvest of the hidden Realms is *nigh*) and 33:59 (draw garments *close*) share a root. Proximity as both spiritual and physical act.

• The term **ر-ي-ن (r-y-n)** — 83:14 "rusted" (rāna) — sin as rust on the heart, another kind of covering parallel to ك-ف-ر. The heart can be covered (kafara) or rusted (rāna) — both obscure the inner light.
